Package identity (10 rows)
fieldvalue
nametamheed-package-v2
titleACMP — Architecture Committee Management Platform
profileunknown (v1-manifest-derived)
moderesume (v1-manifest-derived)
iteration1
package_version1.0.0
mvp_definitionThe PH-1 governance loop (topic intake → agenda → meeting → vote → decision → action) with RBAC+ABAC, in-app notifications, EN/AR+RTL, hash-chained audit, role dashboards, FTS, and typed traceability.
entry_point
go_no_goGO — MVP complete; continued PH-2/hardening under this package's governance.
created_at2026-07-06 (v1-manifest-derived)

requirementtitlemvpDecisions / ADRsWork itemsTestsAcceptance criteriaOther
FR-001The system shall authenticate users via OIDC against Keycloak. No credentials are stored in ACMP.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-002AC-001, AC-004
FR-002The system shall support an adapter for the org's existing internal auth service during Keycloak migration, with configuration to switch between providers without code change.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-2TEST-002AC-002
FR-003As any user, I want to switch the interface language between English and Arabic at any time without losing unsaved form data, so that I can work in my preferred language.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-022, TEST-040AC-039
FR-004As any user browsing in Arabic, I want the entire UI to render in full RTL layout (all text, icons, navigation, form controls, and data tables), so that the platform is comfortable and correct for AraMVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-034, TEST-035, TEST-040, TEST-042AC-040, AC-041, AC-046
FR-005As any user, I want to toggle between light and dark themes, and have my preference remembered across sessions, so that I can work in comfortable lighting conditions.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-034AC-042
FR-006The system shall store file attachments in an S3-compatible/Blob store via `IFileStore` abstraction; metadata (filename, size, MIME type, uploader, timestamp, entity link) is stored in SQL Server.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-023AC-049, AC-050
FR-007As an Administrator, I want health check endpoints for liveness and readiness, so that the Docker Compose deployment can verify the application is running correctly.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-002
FR-008The system shall expose an OpenAPI/Swagger document for all REST endpoints, accessible in development and staging environments.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-002
FR-009The system shall emit structured logs (Serilog) to the app-owned self-hosted Seq container; all log entries include correlation ID, user ID (masked per privacy policy), module, and operation. No depenMVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-002
FR-010The system shall emit distributed traces (OpenTelemetry) for all inbound HTTP requests and background jobs.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-002
FR-011All background jobs shall be managed via Hangfire; job status, history, and failures are visible to Administrator.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-031AC-056
FR-012The system shall run database migrations (EF Core code-first) automatically on startup in non-production environments and via explicit CLI command in production.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-002
FR-013The system shall externalize all configuration (connection strings, secrets, URLs, feature flags) via environment variables and/or a secrets manager; no secrets in source code or container images.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-002AC-004
FR-014As any user, I want to see clear, actionable validation error messages in my active language (EN or AR) when I submit invalid data, so that I can correct mistakes efficiently.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-002
FR-015As any user, I want a confirmation prompt when I navigate away from a form with unsaved changes, so that I do not accidentally lose my work.MVPADR-0002, ADR-0013WBS-1TEST-022AC-047, AC-048
FR-016As an Administrator, I want to create a user account by providing name, email, and stream membership, so that the person can log in via Keycloak and access the system with appropriate scope.MVPADR-0004, ADR-0015WBS-2TEST-001
FR-017Administrator shall be able to create a user account with name, email, and stream membership; the user's global role is sourced from Keycloak group/realm-role claims and mapped to ACMP roles on login.MVPADR-0004, ADR-0015WBS-3TEST-001
FR-018Global roles (Chairman, Secretary, Member, Reviewer, Auditor, Administrator, Submitter, Guest/Presenter) are sourced from Keycloak group/realm-role claims and mapped to ACMP roles on token validation;MVPADR-0004, ADR-0015WBS-2TEST-001, TEST-004AC-002, AC-003, AC-007
FR-019As an Administrator, I want to assign a user to one or more streams, so that their topic access and contributions are correctly scoped to their area of responsibility.MVPADR-0004, ADR-0015WBS-3TEST-005, TEST-006AC-010
FR-020As an Administrator, I want to deactivate a user account, so that a departing team member can no longer log in while their historical votes, authorship, and actions remain intact and attributed.MVPADR-0004, ADR-0015WBS-3TEST-002AC-058

FR-100ADR status lifecycle shall be: Draft → Proposed → Approved → (Superseded | Deprecated). No backward transitions from Approved.ADR-0009, ADR-0021WBS-17TEST-041

FR-115A Research Mission shall be linkable to a topic (as the output of a ResearchDiscovery or ResearchRequired decision); this link shall appear in the traceability graph.ADR-0007WBS-19TEST-041
FR-116Secretary shall be able to create and manage wiki/knowledge-base pages (DOC-…) in Markdown with: title, category, content, and cross-links to other pages or ACMP artifacts.ADR-0002WBS-1TEST-041
FR-117Wiki pages shall be versioned: each save creates an immutable version; previous versions are viewable and diffable.ADR-0002WBS-1TEST-041
FR-118Wiki content shall be indexed in SQL Server FTS and searchable alongside topics, decisions, and ADRs in the global search.ADR-0002WBS-1TEST-041
FR-119Secretary shall be able to create, edit, and delete templates (TPL-…) for artifact types: Topics (by type), MoM, ADR, and Research Mission. Templates are Markdown with placeholder fields.ADR-0002WBS-1TEST-041
FR-120Templates shall be selectable at artifact creation time; the template's Markdown content pre-fills the description/content field and is editable by the user.ADR-0002WBS-19TEST-041
FR-121Secretary or Member shall be able to create a diagram (DGM-…) by submitting a JSON spec conforming to the Tarseem v1.0 schema; the spec is stored as the version-controlled source of truth in SQL ServeADR-0006WBS-18TEST-041
FR-122The system shall render a diagram by submitting the JSON spec to the Tarseem sidecar (containerized render endpoint) via a Hangfire background job; the generated artifacts (SVG, PNG, PDF, draw.io, PPTADR-0006WBS-1TEST-041
FR-123On diagram spec update, the system shall queue a re-render job; the previous artifact version is retained and accessible in diagram version history.ADR-0006WBS-1TEST-041
FR-124Each generated artifact shall record the Tarseem engine version and spec hash used to produce it, enabling future reproducibility and diff.ADR-0006WBS-1TEST-041
FR-125Tarseem render errors shall be surfaced to the diagram author with the structured error list (code, path, message, hint) returned by `tarseem.generate()`; no silent failures.ADR-0006WBS-1TEST-041
FR-126Diagrams shall be attachable to topics (TOP-…), ADRs (ADR-…), and decisions (DECN-…) via the traceability relationship model.ADR-0006WBS-1TEST-041
FR-127All available export formats (SVG, PNG, PDF, draw.io, PPTX) shall be downloadable from the diagram detail page.ADR-0006WBS-1TEST-041
FR-128Diagram version history shall display: spec diff between versions, render timestamp, artifact hash, and engine version.ADR-0006WBS-18TEST-041
FR-129All outbound notifications shall be dispatched via the `INotificationChannel` abstraction. The **in-app notification center** is the sole concrete implementation in v1. The Webex adapter (Phase 2) andMVPADR-0005WBS-13TEST-029AC-053, AC-067, AC-071
FR-130The system shall send an **in-app notification** for each event in the notification catalog: topic status change, agenda published, vote opened, vote closed, MoM published, action reminder, action oveMVPADR-0005WBS-20TEST-028, TEST-029, TEST-041AC-051, AC-067
FR-131Each notification shall include a deep link to the relevant artifact in ACMP (e.g., clicking a vote-open notification takes the recipient directly to the voting UI for that topic).MVPADR-0005WBS-13TEST-029AC-052
FR-132Outbound integration/notification dispatch shall back off and retry transient failures via the background-job/outbox mechanism; delivery failures are logged and surfaced in the admin dashboard. (WebexMVPADR-0005WBS-13TEST-028AC-068
FR-133The system shall support a user notification preferences page (per-user, per-event-type: on/off) for all non-mandatory notifications.ADR-0005WBS-13TEST-028
FR-134A Hangfire job shall generate and send a daily/weekly digest of pending actions, upcoming meetings, and open votes to opted-in users.ADR-0005WBS-13TEST-028
FR-135As any authenticated committee member, I want a committee dashboard showing backlog summary by status/urgency, the next scheduled meeting with agenda link, open action counts by status, overdue actionMVPADR-0003, ADR-0022WBS-20.5TEST-037, TEST-043AC-064
FR-136As a Secretary, I want my dashboard to show topics in triage queue, pending MoM approvals, overdue actions awaiting escalation, and aging topics beyond SLA, so that I can immediately identify what neeMVPADR-0003, ADR-0022WBS-14.2TEST-037AC-065
FR-137As a Chairman, I want my dashboard to show votes pending my approval, escalated risks, escalated actions, and topics deferred ≥2 times, so that I can focus on the items that require my authority.MVPADR-0003, ADR-0022WBS-20.5TEST-037, TEST-043AC-066
FR-138The system shall provide a per-stream report: all topics, decisions, actions, and risks affecting a given stream, filterable by date range and status.ADR-0003, ADR-0022WBS-20.5TEST-037
FR-139The system shall provide a decision history report: all decisions in a date range, filterable by outcome and stream, exportable to CSV.ADR-0003, ADR-0022WBS-14.5TEST-037
FR-140The system shall provide an action completion rate trend chart (weekly/monthly): open vs. closed vs. overdue over time.ADR-0003, ADR-0022WBS-20.5TEST-037
FR-141The system shall provide a KPI/health dashboard with: average topic-to-decision days by type, action SLA compliance %, backlog age distribution, and vote-to-ratification time.ADR-0003, ADR-0022WBS-20.5TEST-037
FR-142All tabular reports shall be exportable to CSV; charts shall be exportable to PNG.ADR-0003, ADR-0022WBS-20.5TEST-037
FR-143As any committee member, I want a global search bar accessible from every page that searches across topics, decisions, and MoMs (by title, description, and content), with results grouped by artifact tMVPADR-0011, ADR-0020WBS-15TEST-045AC-060
FR-144Global search results shall be grouped by artifact type and display: ID, title, matched excerpt, status, and a deep link to the artifact.MVPADR-0011, ADR-0020WBS-15TEST-036AC-060
FR-145As any committee member who works in Arabic, I want global search to correctly process Arabic queries (Arabic word-breaking), so that I get relevant results when I search in Arabic.MVPADR-0011, ADR-0020WBS-15TEST-045AC-061
FR-146As any committee member, I want every artifact (topic, decision, action, risk, dependency, MoM) to display a traceability panel showing all upstream and downstream typed relationships, so that I can nMVPADR-0011, ADR-0020WBS-15TEST-036, TEST-046AC-062
FR-147As a Secretary, I want to create, read, and delete typed traceability edges between any two artifacts (DerivedFrom, Supersedes, Implements, Resolves, References, Blocks, DependsOn, RelatesTo), so thatMVPADR-0011, ADR-0020WBS-15TEST-036, TEST-046AC-063
FR-148The system shall perform a transitive impact analysis query: given artifact X, return all artifacts reachable via any relationship type within a configurable depth limit (default 3 hops); results dispADR-0011, ADR-0020WBS-15TEST-036
FR-149The system shall generate a traceability matrix for a topic (TOP-…): a table of the topic and all linked artifacts by type and relationship, exportable to CSV.ADR-0011, ADR-0020WBS-15TEST-036
FR-150As an Auditor or Administrator, I want every state transition, field update, and user action on governed entities (topics, decisions, votes, actions, risks, dependencies, MoMs) to be recorded automatiMVPADR-0009, ADR-0026WBS-16TEST-044AC-017
FR-151Each audit log entry shall record: entity type, entity ID, action type, actor user ID, timestamp (UTC), before-state (JSON), after-state (JSON), and correlation ID.MVPADR-0009, ADR-0026, ADR-0027WBS-16TEST-044AC-017
FR-152As an Auditor or Administrator, I want audit log entries to be immutable — no API, UI, or database-level process can update or delete an audit row in normal operation — so that the log is a tamper-eviMVPADR-0009, ADR-0026WBS-16TEST-044AC-018, AC-019
FR-153As an Auditor or Administrator, I want to search the audit log by entity type, entity ID, actor, action type, and date range (paginated results), so that I can investigate specific events efficiently.MVPADR-0009, ADR-0027WBS-16TEST-044AC-020
FR-154The audit log shall be exportable to CSV or JSON for a date range and entity filter; this export is accessible only to Auditor and Administrator.ADR-0009WBS-16TEST-044
FR-155Data retention policy: all records are retained; retention is **configurable** so legal can set periods later; **no automatic purge in v1**. Votes, issued decisions, ADRs, and published minutes are imADR-0009WBS-16TEST-044
NFR-001Page load time (P95) for any page rendered with full data (topic list, dashboard, meeting detail) shall not exceed 2 seconds on a standard office network connection (≥10 Mbps). **Target:** P95 ≤ 2 000MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-002API response time (P95) for list/query endpoints (backlog list, search results, audit log query) with up to 10 000 topic records shall not exceed 1 second. **Target:** P95 ≤ 1 000 ms.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-003API response time (P95) for individual artifact detail retrieval (topic, decision, meeting) shall not exceed 500 ms. **Target:** P95 ≤ 500 ms.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-004Full-text search query (SQL FTS) over topic titles, descriptions, and decision rationale with up to 10 000 records shall return results in ≤ 800 ms P95. **Target:** P95 ≤ 800 ms.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-005Tarseem diagram render (via sidecar Hangfire job) for a diagram spec of typical complexity (≤50 nodes, ≤100 edges) shall complete within 30 seconds of job enqueue. **Target:** P95 ≤ 30 s (end-to-end fMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-006Meeting notes auto-save (debounce on typing pause) shall complete a server-side persist within 2 seconds of the debounce trigger. **Target:** ≤ 2 000 ms round-trip from debounce trigger to server ACK.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-007Background job execution (Hangfire): action-overdue detection job shall process all open actions and send notifications within 5 minutes of scheduled run time. **Target:** Processing lag ≤ 5 min from MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-008The system shall support up to 20 committee user accounts in v1 without degradation in authentication or authorization latency. Right-sized for on-prem, low-traffic deployment. **Target:** 20 user accMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-009The system shall support up to 500 topics per year (2 500 total over a 5-year operational life) without schema migration, index degradation, or query plan regression. **Target:** 2 500 topic records; MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-010The system shall support 5 active streams with no hard-coded stream limit; stream count is configuration-driven. **Target:** 5 streams at go-live; configurable to ≥20.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-011File attachment storage shall support individual file uploads up to 100 MB; cumulative attachment storage shall be bounded only by the provisioned blob storage capacity (not ACMP application code). **MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-012The audit log shall support append-only writes at ≥ 100 events/second sustained for 60 seconds without data loss or lock contention on the primary transaction. **Target:** ≥ 100 audit log inserts/s unMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-013The SQL Server database shall support columnstore-indexed reporting queries (dashboard aggregations) over 3 years of data (estimated 10 000–50 000 rows across governed entities) with result times ≤ 3 MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-014The system shall be available **24×7** with a monthly availability target of **≥ 99.9%**, achieved via simple redundancy and nightly backups (no HA cluster, no horizontal scaling). Right-sized for on-MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-015Scheduled maintenance windows (for upgrades, backups, or ops tasks) are acceptable with ≥ 24 h advance notice to committee members; planned windows do not count against availability SLA. **Target:** MMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-016A single application process failure (unhandled exception, OOM) shall not result in data loss; the system shall recover from restart without manual intervention within 2 minutes. **Target:** Recovery MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-017Hangfire background job failures shall be retried automatically up to 3 times with exponential backoff (default Hangfire retry policy); persistent failures shall alert Administrator via self-hosted SeMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-018The application shall meet OWASP ASVS 5.0 Level 2 requirements for all applicable chapters. **Target:** 100% of L2 mandatory requirements pass.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-019All inter-service communication (application ↔ SQL Server, application ↔ Tarseem sidecar, application ↔ Keycloak) shall use TLS 1.2 or higher; TLS 1.0 and 1.1 shall be disabled. **Target:** TLS ≥ 1.2 MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-020Authentication tokens (JWT from Keycloak) shall be validated on every API request; requests without a valid token return HTTP 401; requests with insufficient role/scope return HTTP 403. No endpoint isMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-021All user input shall be validated server-side; SQL parameters shall use parameterized queries or EF Core LINQ only; raw SQL string concatenation with user input is prohibited. **Target:** 0 SQL injectMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-022The application shall implement CSRF protection for all state-changing requests (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) using the ASP.NET Core anti-forgery token or SameSite cookie policy. **Target:** 0 CSRF vulneMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-023Session tokens shall expire after 8 hours of inactivity [unverified — confirm with org security policy]; refresh tokens shall not extend sessions beyond 24 hours without re-authentication. **Target:**MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-024All application secrets (database connection strings, Keycloak client secret, S3/Blob credentials, API keys) shall be managed via environment variables or a secrets manager; no secrets shall appear inMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-025Transcript and recording data shall be accessible only to roles explicitly authorized (Chairman, Secretary, Auditor); all access to transcript content shall generate an audit log entry. **Target:** RoMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-026The application shall be protected against OWASP LLM01 (Prompt Injection) in all AI-extraction features: all LLM inputs from meeting transcripts shall be treated as untrusted data; LLM-generated outpuMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-027Meeting recording files and transcripts are sensitive data; they shall be stored in the object store (self-hosted MinIO) with access-controlled pre-signed URLs (time-limited, ≤ 1 h expiry); the URL isMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-028Structured logs and OTel traces shall not record personally identifiable information (PII) beyond user ID (pseudonymous); full names, email addresses, and vote content shall not appear in log payloadsMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-029Voting is always attributed in v1 (vote anonymity is out of scope for v1, per ADR-0010). Individual voter choices are recorded in the audit log and accessible to Auditor and Chairman; the results viewMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-030All user interface components shall conform to WCAG 2.2 Level AA (W3C Recommendation, October 2023). **Target:** 0 WCAG 2.2 AA failures per automated + manual audit.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-031All interactive elements (buttons, links, form fields, DnD handles) shall be keyboard accessible; drag-and-drop operations shall have a keyboard-accessible alternative (move up/down controls). **TargeMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-032All non-decorative images, icons, and data visualizations (charts, diagrams) shall have appropriate alternative text or ARIA labels in both EN and AR. **Target:** 0 non-decorative images without alt tMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-033Color contrast ratio for all text on background and UI component states (normal, hover, focused, disabled) shall meet WCAG 2.2 AA minimum (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text and UI components). *MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-034Status indicators (topic urgency, action overdue, risk severity) shall not rely on color alone; they shall also use text labels and/or icons with accessible names. **Target:** 0 color-only status indiMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-035All UI strings shall be externalized in `react-i18next` resource files; no hardcoded display strings in component code. **Target:** 0 hardcoded EN strings in component JSX/TSX.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-036The Arabic locale shall trigger full RTL layout via the `dir="rtl"` attribute on `<html>` and CSS logical properties throughout; no `left`/`right` CSS properties shall appear in component stylesheets.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-037All date, time, and number formats shall use locale-appropriate formatting: Gregorian calendar in both EN and AR (v1 only; Hijri display is a future option, not in v1). **Target:** Gregorian dates renMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-038Tarseem diagram output shall render Arabic text correctly (HarfBuzz shaping, RTL geometry mirror) when topic language is AR or diagram content includes Arabic labels. **Target:** 0 garbled Arabic textMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-039The EN↔AR terminology glossary (README §G, localized in design handoff) shall be the single source of canonical bilingual terms; no term shall have more than one AR translation within the application.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-040The audit log table shall have no `UPDATE` or `DELETE` triggers, constraints, or ORM operations defined; it is insert-only at the application layer. **Target:** 0 UPDATE/DELETE statements targeting thMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-041Votes (individual vote records) and issued decisions (DECN-…) shall be immutable after their respective immutability events (vote close, decision issue); no API endpoint shall permit field mutation onMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-042Every state transition on a governed entity shall generate a corresponding audit log entry within the same database transaction; no state transition without an audit log entry is a system defect. **TaMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-043Every inbound HTTP request shall generate an OTel trace with spans for: HTTP handler, DB query, Hangfire job dispatch, and external HTTP calls (Webex, Tarseem sidecar). **Target:** 100% of requests trMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-044Structured log entries (Serilog) shall include: `CorrelationId`, `UserId` (pseudonymous), `Module`, `Operation`, `ElapsedMs`, and `LogLevel`; minimum log level in production is `Information`. All logsMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-045Application health check endpoints shall respond within 1 second and return: application status, database connectivity, Hangfire connectivity, Seq connectivity, MinIO connectivity, and Tarseem sidecarMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-046Alerts shall be configured in self-hosted Seq for: application error rate > 5 errors/min, Hangfire failed jobs > 3 in 10 min, health check failure ≥ 3 consecutive, and audit log write failure (any). *MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-047Each canonical module (Membership, Topics, Meetings, Decisions, Actions, Risks, Dependencies, Governance, Research, Knowledge, Diagrams, Notifications, Reporting, Search&Traceability, Audit&Records, PMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-048Unit test coverage for domain logic (entity state machines, business rules, validation) shall be ≥ 80% line coverage. **Target:** ≥ 80% line coverage on domain layer projects.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-049Integration test coverage shall include at least one end-to-end happy-path test and one failure-path test per canonical workflow (topic intake, agenda creation, meeting + MoM, vote + decision, action MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-050Database schema migrations shall be backward-compatible with the running application version (expand-contract pattern); no migration shall drop a column or table that the current application version sMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-051All third-party dependencies shall be declared in `package.json` / `.csproj` with pinned or semver-constrained versions; Dependabot or equivalent shall be configured to alert on known CVEs within 48 hMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-052The application shall be fully containerized (Docker); a `docker-compose.yml` shall run the complete local development stack (app, SQL Server, self-hosted Seq, self-hosted MinIO, Tarseem sidecar [PhasMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-053All application configuration shall be externalized; switching deployment environments (dev/staging/prod) requires only environment variable changes, not code changes or image rebuilds. **Target:** 0 MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-054Container images shall be built from a minimal base image (e.g., `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:8.0-alpine` or equivalent distroless); image size shall not exceed 500 MB. **Target:** Image size ≤ 50MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-055The system shall not require Kubernetes, a service mesh, or a message broker to run; Docker Compose or equivalent single-host container orchestration is the supported deployment model in v1. **Target:MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-056Recovery Point Objective (RPO): in the event of a catastrophic failure, data loss shall not exceed 4 hours (i.e., database backups occur at least every 4 hours during business days). **Target:** RPO ≤MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-057Recovery Time Objective (RTO): the system shall be restorable to a functional state within 8 business hours of a declared disaster. **Target:** RTO ≤ 8 h.MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-058Database backups shall be stored in a location separate from the primary database server; backups shall be tested for restorability at least quarterly. **Target:** Backups on separate storage; quarterMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-059All records (including audit log data) are **retained indefinitely in v1; no automatic purge**. Retention periods are **configurable** so legal/compliance can set them later. Immutable records (votes,MVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-060File attachments (presentations, recordings, diagrams) are **retained; no automatic purge in v1**. Retention periods are configurable and will be set by legal/ops. No automated archival job deletes atMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-061The web application shall support the latest two major versions of: Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, and Apple Safari as of the release date. No Internet Explorer support. **Target:** FMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-062The application shall be usable on tablet devices (iPad or equivalent, landscape orientation, ≥1024 px viewport width) with no horizontal scroll and no overlapping UI elements. **Target:** 0 UI defectMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045
NFR-063The application is not required to support mobile phone viewports (< 768 px) in v1; a "not optimized for mobile" notice is acceptable on narrow viewports. **Target:** Mobile (< 768 px) displays a gracMVPADR-0012, ADR-0016WBS-1TEST-041, TEST-045

AC-012**Given** Alice owns action ACT-2026-010 and has marked it Completed <br> **When** Alice attempts to mark ACT-2026-010 aApprovedMetevidencedSegregationOfDutiesTests + ActionHandlerTests (owner-verify denied + `ActionVerifyDenied` audit, stays Completed) + ActionsApiTests (owner verify → 403, HTTP) + ActionActions.test (Verify hidden from owner/completer; server 403 surfaced without closing the dialog) + p17b-immutability.spec.ts (live real-stack: the owner/completer opens their own Completed action and is offered NO Verify button)
AC-013**Given** Bob (Secretary), not the owner of action ACT-2026-010 <br> **When** Bob marks ACT-2026-010 as Verified <br> **ApprovedMetevidencedSegregationOfDutiesTests + ActionHandlerTests (independent verifier → Verified + `ActionVerified` audit) + ActionsApiTests (third-party verify → 204, Verified) + ActionActions.test (a non-owner verifier sees + triggers Verify → `useVerifyAction`) + p17b-actions.spec.ts (live real-stack: a chairman owns + completes an action, then the secretary — a different sub — opens it and drives Verify in the UI → 204 → the action reaches Verified)
AC-014**Given** Secretary Carol is both sole author and approver of MoM MIN-2026-002 <br> **When** Carol approves it <br> **ThApprovedMetevidencedMinutesHandlerTests (sole-author approval allowed + flagged; different approver clears the flag) + MinutesApiTests (sole-author publish → ApprovedBySoleAuthor) + MeetingMinutes.test (approved sole-author MoM renders the warning badge; a non-sole-author approval renders none) + p17b-sole-author.spec.ts (live real-stack: a secretary authors → submits → approves a MoM — author === approver — and the minutes banner renders the "Approved by sole author" warning)
AC-015**Given** Chairman Dave attempts to close vote VOTE-2026-004 and record his own override on the same decision without a ApprovedMetevidencedSegregationOfDutiesTests + VoteHandlerTests (chair counted the vote → issue 403 + audited denial; vote stays Closed, not ratified) + RecordDecisionDialog.test (the issue 403 is surfaced inline; no navigation) + p17b-decision-issue.spec.ts (live real-stack: the chairman who CLOSED the coupled vote records a decision and attempts issue through the RecordDecisionDialog → server 403 surfaced inline; the vote stays Closed, not Ratified)
AC-016**Given** Secretary Eva co-attests the tally for vote VOTE-2026-004 and Chairman Dave records an Override decision <br> ApprovedMetevidencedSegregationOfDutiesTests + DecisionHandlerTests (override + justification + flag) + VoteHandlerTests (secretary counted → chair issue allowed + vote Ratified) + RecordDecisionDialog.test (record→issue two-step, override+justification body, record-once retry guard) + p17b-decision-issue.spec.ts (live real-stack, F-03 leg: a secretary closes the coupled vote, then the chairman records + issues the decision with an override + justification through the RecordDecisionDialog → decision Issued + vote Ratified)
AC-017**Given** a state transition on any governed entity (topic, decision, vote, action, risk, MoM) <br> **When** the transacApprovedMetevidencedAuditAtomicityTests (enriched before/after populated end-to-end on real SQL) + AuditApiTests (register surfaces normalized v1+v2 rows, entityType/actor filters, paginated) + the PR2 per-module emit-site migration to `EmitEnrichedAsync`
AC-018**Given** an audit log entry exists in the database <br> **When** any party (a DBA acting outside the app, or any API caApprovedMetevidencedAuditEventEnrichmentTests (valid v1→v2 chain verifies; enriched-field tamper flagged with the correct `Reason`) + AuditEvent design (no public setters, no Update/Delete path; SaveChanges only Adds) + UNIQUE `PreviousHash`/`Hash` non-forking indexes
AC-019**Given** the audit log implements a hash-chain where each entry's hash covers its data plus the previous entry's hash <ApprovedMetevidencedAuditEventEnrichmentTests (tamper → `Verify` reports non-null `BrokenAtSequence` with `Reason`) + AuditApiTests (`GET /api/audit/verify` → {IsValid, BrokenAtSequence, Reason} over an intact chain, RBAC-gated)

AC-022**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Open and Alice has already cast her vote <br> **When** Alice attempts a second ballot <bApprovedMetevidencedVoteTests (second cast throws) + VoteHandlerTests (double-vote audited denial `Decisions.BallotDenied`) + VotesApiTests (2nd cast → 409) + p17b-immutability.spec.ts (live real-stack: a voter who has cast is shown their recorded ballot + a "Change vote" action — Fork 1 — never an "already voted" rejection)
AC-023**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Closed <br> **When** the Auditor views the vote record <br> **Then** each eligible voterApprovedMetevidencedVoteHandlerTests + VotesApiTests (GetVoteByKey returns attributed ballots, no masking) + p17b-voting.spec.ts (live real-stack: a chairman genuinely casts a ballot, the secretary closes the vote, then the closed roster renders that ballot attributed to the chairman by name + choice — never anonymized, ADR-0010)

AC-062**Given** a user views the detail page of any artifact (topic, decision, action, risk, dependency, MoM) <br> **When** thApprovedMetevidencedTraceabilityTests (panel groups outgoing/incoming, "other" endpoint per direction, excludes inactive) + TraceabilityApiTests (`GET /api/traceability/{type}/{id}`) + P10e `TraceabilityPanel` FE suite (upstream/downstream groups, navigable links, axe-clean) + p17b-traceability.spec.ts (live real-stack: secretary logs in via genuine Keycloak PKCE, opens a Topic detail whose panel is honestly empty, then the created edge renders on it as a navigable `.tp-item-link` whose href targets the far artifact)
AC-063**Given** a Secretary creates a typed traceability edge from topic TOP-2026-040 (DerivedFrom) to decision DECN-2026-015 ApprovedMetevidencedTraceabilityApiTests (Secretary creates edge → both source + target panels show it) + TraceabilityTests (create audits `Relationship.Created`) + P10e create-dialog FE suite (dependency + generic-relationship, Chair/Sec-gated) + p17b-traceability.spec.ts (live real-stack: a typed edge created through the CreateRelationshipDialog → 201 → BOTH source and target panels reflect it with navigable links; the source panel refetches in place via query invalidation, no reload)
AC-064**Given** any authenticated committee member logs in <br> **When** the committee dashboard loads <br> **Then** it displaApprovedMetevidenceddashboardAgg.test (backlog by bucket + urgency, next meeting, action counts) + RoleDashboard.test (committee variant renders backlog total/next meeting/action tiles/last-5 decisions live; honest empty states)
AC-065**Given** a Secretary logs in <br> **When** the secretary dashboard loads <br> **Then** it shows count of topics in TriaApprovedMetevidenceddashboardAgg.test (overdue-beyond-threshold, SLA-breach sort) + RoleDashboard.test (secretary variant renders triage/MoMs/escalated counts + SLA list with aging)
AC-066**Given** the Chairman logs in <br> **When** the chairman dashboard loads <br> **Then** it shows votes awaiting chairmanApprovedMetevidenceddashboardAgg.test (deferred≥2, overdue-beyond-threshold) + RoleDashboard.test (chairman variant renders Closed votes / escalated risks / escalated actions / deferred≥2 with badges)
AC-067**Given** Webex is enabled and a committee-wide event occurs (e.g. agenda published) <br> **When** the notification dispApprovedMetevidencedWebexNotificationSinkTests (eligible→1 enqueue; per-recipient fan-out collapses to 1; disabled/non-eligible→0) + AdaptiveCardBuilderTests (v1.3, ≤80 KB, EN/AR, absolute deep-link)
AC-068**Given** the Webex API answers HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header <br> **When** the card-send job runs <br> **Then** itApprovedMetevidencedReschedule leg: WebexApiClientTests (Retry-After 30→30s) + WebexSendJobTests/WebexWebhookJobTests/WebexMeetingCreateJobTests (all three jobs reschedule on 429, no throw). Dead-letter: **WebexJobDeadLetterTests** drives a real in-memory Hangfire server — a job past its `[AutomaticRetry]` cap lands in the **Failed** state (dead-letter), asserted via `IMonitoringApi`.
AC-069**Given** an inbound Webex webhook <br> **When** its `x-spark-signature` HMAC is invalid <br> **Then** the request is reApprovedMetevidencedWebexWebhookApiTests (valid sig→200 + enqueue; invalid→401 + 0; event >5 min → dropped; no-timestamp → dropped) + WebexSignatureTests (SHA1/256/512 accept; tamper/wrong-secret/missing reject) + WebexWebhookJobTests (reprocessing the same recording attaches the identical reference — outcome-idempotent, so a within-window replay is harmless).
AC-070**Given** a recording-ready webhook for a correlated Webex meeting <br> **When** it is processed <br> **Then** the recorApprovedMetevidencedMeetingWebexWriterTests (attach by WebexMeetingId + `Meetings.RecordingAttached` audit; uncorrelated→false) + WebexWebhookJobTests (fetch→attach; skip null / no-meeting-id); **live**: webhook auto-registration (audit seq=46) + a synthetic signed `recordings/created` webhook → 200 → worker job → graceful drop
AC-071**Given** `Webex:Enabled` is false (air-gapped / v1 posture) <br> **When** any notification dispatches or the webhook enApprovedMetevidencedWebexWebhookApiTests (disabled→200 no-op) + WebexNotificationSinkTests (disabled→0 enqueue); adapter unregistered entirely when `Enabled=false`
AC-072**Given** an online meeting is scheduled with Webex enabled and a valid OAuth token <br> **When** the create job runs <bApprovedMetevidencedWebexMeetingCreateJobTests (token→create + write-back; no-token/no-meeting→skip) + WebexMeetingProvisionerTests (online+enabled→enqueue; else 0) + WebexTokenServiceTests (fresh/refresh/none)
AC-073**Given** a Secretary or Chairman uploads a recording file (allowed video type, ≤ 2 GB) to a meeting <br> **When** the uApprovedMetevidencedUploadRecording handler/validator + GetRecordingUrl handler tests + MeetingRecordingApiTests (401/403/400/404/200; upload→detail; presign→url) + **MinioFileStoreTests (Testcontainers-MinIO: real adapter — bucket create/skip, presign, exists found/object-missing/bucket-missing, delete)** + live (real upload 200 through nginx; presigned 200/206)
AC-074**Given** a meeting has a recording (uploaded file or Webex reference) <br> **When** a Secretary or Chairman deletes it ApprovedMetevidencedDeleteRecording handler tests (uploaded→delete object+clear+audit; webex→clear only; missing→throws) + MeetingRecordingApiTests (401/403/204→detail null) + live (delete → MinIO bucket empty)

PE-1262026-07-23Data repair of the 2.3.0-migrated package (2026-07-22/23): recovered the discarded roadmap as a narrative document, corrected 8 deferred-work statuses to the v1 register, rewrote the three handoff prompts to the v2 protocol, restored 155 work-item titles, backfilled 125 dated progress entries with three-valued date provenance, and created PH-4 under DEC-029/SC-001. Superseded by the 2.4.0 re-population recorded below.
PE-1272026-07-23Re-populated the package from docs/ with the 2.4.0 parser (identity DW crosswalk, full-text statements, ACs land Proposed, roadmap emitted as a document) and swapped it in for the 2.3.0-era package. Carried over: PH-4 + DEC-029 + SC-001, 19 slices, 129 progress entries, 13 defect records, 10 ADR-amendment trace edges, the deferred-work and work-item status truth-ups, and the AC-to-slice binding.
PE-1282026-07-23Status reconciliation after re-population, value-diffed against the prior package rather than assumed: deferred-work Done = historic D-13/15/16/18/19/21/23 (identity-keyed), Activated = D-02; 15 work items re-marked Deferred; PH-0/PH-1 re-marked Implemented; risk states reproduced by the 2.4.0 parser without repair.
PE-1292026-07-23Residuals after re-population, deliberately left open: the derived backlog still lists unevidenced work items as open (recorded as an open defect row); leaf work items carry no phase inheritance; the package-header name retains the interim -v2 suffix (cosmetic; no tool writes the packages row).
Scope changes (1 row)
iditerationauthorized bydescription
SC-0011DEC-029Add phase PH-4 "Cross-cutting hardening & release" and bind slices SL-016 through SL-019 to it. This introduces structure absent from every v1 artifact, so it is recorded as a typed scope change before the mutation it authorizes. Authorized by DEC-029. No requirement, acceptance criterion or work item changes scope as a result; the four slices already existed in the roadmap ladder and had already shipped. The change is representational only. Re-applied on the 2.4.0 re-population of 2026-07-23.

Registers

Freshness: iteration 1 · latest recorded activity: 2026-07-23

Design-ahead lead (Approved, not yet Implemented — an explicit, healthy state): 174 requirements; 29 decisions; 74 acceptance criteria

Requirements (218 rows) CSV
idkindtitlestatementprioritymvplifecycle_statusdispositiondisposition_reason_refsource_kindsource_spanintroduced_inretired_inlast_referenced
FR-001functionalThe system shall authenticate users via OIDC against Keycloak. No credentials are stored in ACMP.The system shall authenticate users via OIDC against Keycloak. No credentials are stored in ACMP.M1Approvedbriefbrief §6.1; ADR-00041
FR-002functionalThe system shall support an adapter for the org's existing internal auth service during Keycloak migration, with configuration to switch between providers without code change.The system shall support an adapter for the org's existing internal auth service during Keycloak migration, with configuration to switch between providers without code change.M1Implementedbriefdigest §1; ADR-00041
FR-003functionalAs any user, I want to switch the interface language between English and Arabic at any time without losing unsaved form data, so that I can work in my preferred language.As any user, I want to switch the interface language between English and Arabic at any time without losing unsaved form data, so that I can work in my preferred language.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-004functionalAs any user browsing in Arabic, I want the entire UI to render in full RTL layout (all text, icons, navigation, form controls, and data tables), so that the platform is comfortable and correct for AraAs any user browsing in Arabic, I want the entire UI to render in full RTL layout (all text, icons, navigation, form controls, and data tables), so that the platform is comfortable and correct for Arabic readers.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-005functionalAs any user, I want to toggle between light and dark themes, and have my preference remembered across sessions, so that I can work in comfortable lighting conditions.As any user, I want to toggle between light and dark themes, and have my preference remembered across sessions, so that I can work in comfortable lighting conditions.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-006functionalThe system shall store file attachments in an S3-compatible/Blob store via `IFileStore` abstraction; metadata (filename, size, MIME type, uploader, timestamp, entity link) is stored in SQL Server.The system shall store file attachments in an S3-compatible/Blob store via `IFileStore` abstraction; metadata (filename, size, MIME type, uploader, timestamp, entity link) is stored in SQL Server.M1ImplementedbriefREADME §A; ADR-00031
FR-007functionalAs an Administrator, I want health check endpoints for liveness and readiness, so that the Docker Compose deployment can verify the application is running correctly.As an Administrator, I want health check endpoints for liveness and readiness, so that the Docker Compose deployment can verify the application is running correctly.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-008functionalThe system shall expose an OpenAPI/Swagger document for all REST endpoints, accessible in development and staging environments.The system shall expose an OpenAPI/Swagger document for all REST endpoints, accessible in development and staging environments.M1Approvedbriefdigest §41
FR-009functionalThe system shall emit structured logs (Serilog) to the app-owned self-hosted Seq container; all log entries include correlation ID, user ID (masked per privacy policy), module, and operation. No depenThe system shall emit structured logs (Serilog) to the app-owned self-hosted Seq container; all log entries include correlation ID, user ID (masked per privacy policy), module, and operation. No dependency on the org's ELK/Seq.M1ApprovedbriefREADME §A; digest §41
FR-010functionalThe system shall emit distributed traces (OpenTelemetry) for all inbound HTTP requests and background jobs.The system shall emit distributed traces (OpenTelemetry) for all inbound HTTP requests and background jobs.M1ApprovedbriefREADME §A1
FR-011functionalAll background jobs shall be managed via Hangfire; job status, history, and failures are visible to Administrator.All background jobs shall be managed via Hangfire; job status, history, and failures are visible to Administrator.M1ImplementedbriefREADME §A; digest §11
FR-012functionalThe system shall run database migrations (EF Core code-first) automatically on startup in non-production environments and via explicit CLI command in production.The system shall run database migrations (EF Core code-first) automatically on startup in non-production environments and via explicit CLI command in production.M1Approvedbriefdigest §41
FR-013functionalThe system shall externalize all configuration (connection strings, secrets, URLs, feature flags) via environment variables and/or a secrets manager; no secrets in source code or container images.The system shall externalize all configuration (connection strings, secrets, URLs, feature flags) via environment variables and/or a secrets manager; no secrets in source code or container images.M1Approvedbriefdigest §4; derived: security1
FR-014functionalAs any user, I want to see clear, actionable validation error messages in my active language (EN or AR) when I submit invalid data, so that I can correct mistakes efficiently.As any user, I want to see clear, actionable validation error messages in my active language (EN or AR) when I submit invalid data, so that I can correct mistakes efficiently.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-015functionalAs any user, I want a confirmation prompt when I navigate away from a form with unsaved changes, so that I do not accidentally lose my work.As any user, I want a confirmation prompt when I navigate away from a form with unsaved changes, so that I do not accidentally lose my work.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-016functionalAs an Administrator, I want to create a user account by providing name, email, and stream membership, so that the person can log in via Keycloak and access the system with appropriate scope.As an Administrator, I want to create a user account by providing name, email, and stream membership, so that the person can log in via Keycloak and access the system with appropriate scope.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-017functionalAdministrator shall be able to create a user account with name, email, and stream membership; the user's global role is sourced from Keycloak group/realm-role claims and mapped to ACMP roles on login.Administrator shall be able to create a user account with name, email, and stream membership; the user's global role is sourced from Keycloak group/realm-role claims and mapped to ACMP roles on login. The user receives an in-app notification on first login (email deferred).M1Approvedbriefbrief §6.1; ADR-00041
FR-018functionalGlobal roles (Chairman, Secretary, Member, Reviewer, Auditor, Administrator, Submitter, Guest/Presenter) are sourced from Keycloak group/realm-role claims and mapped to ACMP roles on token validation;Global roles (Chairman, Secretary, Member, Reviewer, Auditor, Administrator, Submitter, Guest/Presenter) are sourced from Keycloak group/realm-role claims and mapped to ACMP roles on token validation; ACMP does not store or assign global roles independently. Administrator manages committee membership, stream scope, and per-topic assignments within ACMP.M1ApprovedbriefREADME §C; ADR-00041
FR-019functionalAs an Administrator, I want to assign a user to one or more streams, so that their topic access and contributions are correctly scoped to their area of responsibility.As an Administrator, I want to assign a user to one or more streams, so that their topic access and contributions are correctly scoped to their area of responsibility.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-020functionalAs an Administrator, I want to deactivate a user account, so that a departing team member can no longer log in while their historical votes, authorship, and actions remain intact and attributed.As an Administrator, I want to deactivate a user account, so that a departing team member can no longer log in while their historical votes, authorship, and actions remain intact and attributed.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-021functionalAs any authenticated user, I want to view a committee member directory listing all active users with their name, role, stream membership, and email, so that I can identify who is on the committee and As any authenticated user, I want to view a committee member directory listing all active users with their name, role, stream membership, and email, so that I can identify who is on the committee and contact them.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-022functionalAs a Secretary, I want to assign per-topic capabilities (Owner, Assignee, Presenter) to users independently of their global role, so that a Member can be made Presenter on a topic without a global rolAs a Secretary, I want to assign per-topic capabilities (Owner, Assignee, Presenter) to users independently of their global role, so that a Member can be made Presenter on a topic without a global role change.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-023functionalThe system shall support time-boxed Guest/Presenter access: a secretary can invite an external presenter with view-only access scoped to a specific meeting or topic, expiring automatically after the mThe system shall support time-boxed Guest/Presenter access: a secretary can invite an external presenter with view-only access scoped to a specific meeting or topic, expiring automatically after the meeting date.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.1; digest §31
FR-024functionalEvery user-facing page shall display only the navigation elements and actions the authenticated user's role is authorized to see; unauthorized actions are hidden, not merely disabled.Every user-facing page shall display only the navigation elements and actions the authenticated user's role is authorized to see; unauthorized actions are hidden, not merely disabled.M1Approvedbriefderived: RBAC/UX; brief §6.11
FR-025functionalAs a Submitter, Member, Secretary, or Chairman, I want to submit a new topic with title, description, type, urgency, source, affected streams, and optional target date, so that it enters the committeeAs a Submitter, Member, Secretary, or Chairman, I want to submit a new topic with title, description, type, urgency, source, affected streams, and optional target date, so that it enters the committee's intake queue.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-026functionalAs a topic submitter, I want the system to automatically assign my topic a canonical ID in the format TOP-YYYY-### when I submit it, so that I can reference and track it unambiguously.As a topic submitter, I want the system to automatically assign my topic a canonical ID in the format `TOP-YYYY-###` when I submit it, so that I can reference and track it unambiguously.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-027functionalAs a topic Owner or Secretary, I want to attach files (presentations, documents, diagrams, data sheets) to a topic, so that committee members can review supporting material before the meeting.As a topic Owner or Secretary, I want to attach files (presentations, documents, diagrams, data sheets) to a topic, so that committee members can review supporting material before the meeting.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-028functionalAs a topic Owner, I want to add free-text comments to a topic (timestamped and attributed), so that I can capture notes, updates, and clarifications in context without editing the core topic record.As a topic Owner, I want to add free-text comments to a topic (timestamped and attributed), so that I can capture notes, updates, and clarifications in context without editing the core topic record.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-029functionalAs a Secretary, I want to move a submitted topic into Triage, review its fields, and then accept it (assigning an Owner and confirming scope) or reject/defer it with a mandatory reason, so that only vAs a Secretary, I want to move a submitted topic into Triage, review its fields, and then accept it (assigning an Owner and confirming scope) or reject/defer it with a mandatory reason, so that only valid, well-scoped topics enter the active backlog.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-030functionalSecretary shall be able to convert a topic to a different type (e.g., convert a ResearchDiscovery to an ArchitectureDecision post-research), recording the conversion reason and creating a typed link bSecretary shall be able to convert a topic to a different type (e.g., convert a ResearchDiscovery to an ArchitectureDecision post-research), recording the conversion reason and creating a typed link between the original and converted artifact.S0ApprovedbriefREADME §E; brief §6.21
FR-031functionalAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to view the backlog as a sortable, filterable list showing title, type, urgency badge, status, owner, affected streams, and dates, so that I can quickly scan and manAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to view the backlog as a sortable, filterable list showing title, type, urgency badge, status, owner, affected streams, and dates, so that I can quickly scan and manage the queue.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-032functionalAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to view the backlog as a dense table with user-configurable columns (show/hide, reorder), so that power users can surface the fields most relevant to their workflow.As a Secretary or Chairman, I want to view the backlog as a dense table with user-configurable columns (show/hide, reorder), so that power users can surface the fields most relevant to their workflow.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-033functionalAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to view the backlog as a kanban board with status columns, where dragging a card between columns changes its status (with permission check), so that I can visually mAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to view the backlog as a kanban board with status columns, where dragging a card between columns changes its status (with permission check), so that I can visually manage flow.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-034functionalAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want a keyboard-accessible alternative to drag-and-drop for reordering topics (e.g., move-up/move-down controls), so that the interface is operable without a mouse and meAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want a keyboard-accessible alternative to drag-and-drop for reordering topics (e.g., move-up/move-down controls), so that the interface is operable without a mouse and meets accessibility requirements.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-035functionalAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to view the backlog as a calendar view showing topics by their scheduled meeting date, so that I can see how topics are spread across upcoming meetings.As a Secretary or Chairman, I want to view the backlog as a calendar view showing topics by their scheduled meeting date, so that I can see how topics are spread across upcoming meetings.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-036functionalThe backlog shall be displayable as a timeline/Gantt-lite view showing topic date bars (created → target → scheduled → decided) with pan/zoom.The backlog shall be displayable as a timeline/Gantt-lite view showing topic date bars (created → target → scheduled → decided) with pan/zoom.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.31
FR-037functionalAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to drag-and-drop topics to reprioritize them in the backlog, with priority stored as an explicit ordinal field, so that the committee's session order is always expliAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to drag-and-drop topics to reprioritize them in the backlog, with priority stored as an explicit ordinal field, so that the committee's session order is always explicit.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-038functionalAs a Secretary, I want to see aging indicators on topics that have remained in the same status beyond their urgency SLA (Critical: 3d, Urgent: 7d, Normal: 21d), so that stalled work is immediately visAs a Secretary, I want to see aging indicators on topics that have remained in the same status beyond their urgency SLA (Critical: 3d, Urgent: 7d, Normal: 21d), so that stalled work is immediately visible and I receive a background notification.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-039functionalAs any committee member, I want to view a topic's detail page showing all fields, status history (with actor and timestamp), linked artifacts, and a traceability graph excerpt, so that I have a compleAs any committee member, I want to view a topic's detail page showing all fields, status history (with actor and timestamp), linked artifacts, and a traceability graph excerpt, so that I have a complete picture of a topic's journey.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-040functionalAs a topic Owner or Secretary, I want topic editing to be permitted in early statuses (Draft, Submitted, Triage) and restricted (Secretary-only metadata edits) after Acceptance, so that the record canAs a topic Owner or Secretary, I want topic editing to be permitted in early statuses (Draft, Submitted, Triage) and restricted (Secretary-only metadata edits) after Acceptance, so that the record cannot be retroactively altered once the committee has engaged.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-041functionalThe system shall support topic templates (selectable by type on creation): template provides default description structure, required-field checklist, and suggested sections. Templates are managed in tThe system shall support topic templates (selectable by type on creation): template provides default description structure, required-field checklist, and suggested sections. Templates are managed in the Knowledge module.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.2; brief §6.131
FR-042functionalAs a Secretary, I want to mark an accepted topic as Prepared once its presentation materials and supporting research are complete, so that the scheduling step only considers ready topics.As a Secretary, I want to mark an accepted topic as Prepared once its presentation materials and supporting research are complete, so that the scheduling step only considers ready topics.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-043functionalAs a Secretary, I want to schedule a prepared topic to a specific meeting, moving it to Scheduled status, so that it appears on that meeting's agenda.As a Secretary, I want to schedule a prepared topic to a specific meeting, moving it to Scheduled status, so that it appears on that meeting's agenda.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-044functionalAs a Secretary, I want rejection and deferral reasons to be recorded as immutable events in the topic's history, so that the triage decision is always auditable and not erasable.As a Secretary, I want rejection and deferral reasons to be recorded as immutable events in the topic's history, so that the triage decision is always auditable and not erasable.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-045functionalSecretary shall be able to Reopen a Closed or Rejected topic with a recorded reason; reopened topics re-enter the triage workflow.Secretary shall be able to Reopen a Closed or Rejected topic with a recorded reason; reopened topics re-enter the triage workflow.S0ApprovedbriefREADME §E1
FR-046functionalAs a Secretary, I want to create an agenda for a scheduled meeting by selecting topics from the backlog, setting presentation order, assigning a time-box per item, and assigning a presenter, so that tAs a Secretary, I want to create an agenda for a scheduled meeting by selecting topics from the backlog, setting presentation order, assigning a time-box per item, and assigning a presenter, so that the meeting has a structured plan.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-047functionalAs a Secretary, I want to reorder agenda items via drag-and-drop with a keyboard-accessible alternative, so that the session order can be adjusted easily and accessibly.As a Secretary, I want to reorder agenda items via drag-and-drop with a keyboard-accessible alternative, so that the session order can be adjusted easily and accessibly.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-048functionalAs a Secretary, I want the system to automatically suggest carry-over items from the previous meeting's agenda that were not resolved, so that unfinished business is not silently dropped.As a Secretary, I want the system to automatically suggest carry-over items from the previous meeting's agenda that were not resolved, so that unfinished business is not silently dropped.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-049functionalAs a Secretary, I want to publish the agenda to committee members, triggering an in-app notification with agenda content and material links, so that members can prepare before the meeting.As a Secretary, I want to publish the agenda to committee members, triggering an in-app notification with agenda content and material links, so that members can prepare before the meeting.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-050functionalAs a Secretary, I want to create a meeting record with date, start/end time, meeting type (regular/extraordinary/emergency), mode (in-person/remote/hybrid), and a link to the published agenda, so thatAs a Secretary, I want to create a meeting record with date, start/end time, meeting type (regular/extraordinary/emergency), mode (in-person/remote/hybrid), and a link to the published agenda, so that the meeting is formally recorded.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-051functionalAs a Secretary, I want to mark each committee member as Present, Absent, or Remote for a given meeting, so that the attendance record is complete and can be used for quorum calculation.As a Secretary, I want to mark each committee member as Present, Absent, or Remote for a given meeting, so that the attendance record is complete and can be used for quorum calculation.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-052functionalAs a Secretary, I want to add live discussion notes per agenda item during a meeting (with auto-save on typing pause ≤2s), so that key points are captured in real time without interrupting the flow.As a Secretary, I want to add live discussion notes per agenda item during a meeting (with auto-save on typing pause ≤2s), so that key points are captured in real time without interrupting the flow.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-053functionalAs a Secretary, I want the system to generate a draft Minutes of Meeting (MoM) by compiling attendance, ordered agenda items with notes, decisions issued, and actions created into a structured MarkdowAs a Secretary, I want the system to generate a draft Minutes of Meeting (MoM) by compiling attendance, ordered agenda items with notes, decisions issued, and actions created into a structured Markdown document, so that I do not assemble it manually from scratch.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-054functionalAs a Secretary, I want to submit the draft MoM for review and approval; a Reviewer or Chairman reviews it, and once the Chairman approves, the MoM is locked and immutable with its version history presAs a Secretary, I want to submit the draft MoM for review and approval; a Reviewer or Chairman reviews it, and once the Chairman approves, the MoM is locked and immutable with its version history preserved, so that the official meeting record is tamper-evident.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-055functionalAs a Chairman or Secretary, I want the system to send an in-app notification to all committee members when the MoM is approved and published, containing a link to the published MoM, so that everyone iAs a Chairman or Secretary, I want the system to send an in-app notification to all committee members when the MoM is approved and published, containing a link to the published MoM, so that everyone is informed promptly.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-056functionalAs a Secretary, I want to attach a Webex meeting recording link or upload a recording file to the meeting record, so that future reference to the session is possible.As a Secretary, I want to attach a Webex meeting recording link or upload a recording file to the meeting record, so that future reference to the session is possible.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-057functionalThe system shall retrieve Webex meeting recording metadata (title, duration, download URL, play URL) via the Webex Recordings API and store it in the meeting record, triggered by a Hangfire job post-mThe system shall retrieve Webex meeting recording metadata (title, duration, download URL, play URL) via the Webex Recordings API and store it in the meeting record, triggered by a Hangfire job post-meeting.S0Implementedbriefdigest §5.31
FR-058functionalThe system shall retrieve Webex meeting transcript (speaker-attributed snippets) via the Webex Meeting Transcripts API and store the content linked to the meeting record, subject to Webex Assistant beThe system shall retrieve Webex meeting transcript (speaker-attributed snippets) via the Webex Meeting Transcripts API and store the content linked to the meeting record, subject to Webex Assistant being enabled by the host.S0Approvedbriefdigest §5.31
FR-059functionalStored transcripts shall be indexed in SQL Server FTS and searchable by keyword across all meetings (access restricted to Chairman, Secretary, Auditor).Stored transcripts shall be indexed in SQL Server FTS and searchable by keyword across all meetings (access restricted to Chairman, Secretary, Auditor).S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.51
FR-060functionalThe system shall extract AI-candidate action items and decision summaries from a stored transcript and present them to the Secretary as draft proposals requiring explicit human approval; no AI-extractThe system shall extract AI-candidate action items and decision summaries from a stored transcript and present them to the Secretary as draft proposals requiring explicit human approval; no AI-extracted content enters the record without approval.C0Approvedbriefbrief §6.5; P-051
FR-061functionalAs a Secretary, I want to view a meeting calendar showing past and upcoming meetings with status badges, so that I can track the schedule at a glance.As a Secretary, I want to view a meeting calendar showing past and upcoming meetings with status badges, so that I can track the schedule at a glance.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-062functionalAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to create a committee decision record linked to a topic, capturing outcome (from canonical list), rationale, conditions (if conditionally approved), alternatives conAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to create a committee decision record linked to a topic, capturing outcome (from canonical list), rationale, conditions (if conditionally approved), alternatives considered, authority actor, and effective date, so that the decision is complete and traceable.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-063functionalAs a Secretary, I want the outcome field to enforce selection from the canonical list (Approved, ConditionallyApproved, Rejected, MoreInfoRequired, FeedbackProvided, EnhancementsRequired, DesignChangeAs a Secretary, I want the outcome field to enforce selection from the canonical list (Approved, ConditionallyApproved, Rejected, MoreInfoRequired, FeedbackProvided, EnhancementsRequired, DesignChangesRequired, ResearchRequired, Deferred, Escalated, Converted) with no free-text override, so that outcomes are consistently categorized.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-064functionalA decision record shall capture alternatives considered (structured list: alternative name + reason not chosen) to preserve rationale.A decision record shall capture alternatives considered (structured list: alternative name + reason not chosen) to preserve rationale.M1Approvedbriefbrief §6.61
FR-065functionalAs a Secretary, I want an issued decision to be immutable — corrections require creating a new superseding decision with a back-link to the prior — so that the record of governance cannot be silently As a Secretary, I want an issued decision to be immutable — corrections require creating a new superseding decision with a back-link to the prior — so that the record of governance cannot be silently altered.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-066functionalOnce a decision record status is Issued (i.e., voting closed and chairman has acted), the decision content (outcome, rationale, alternatives, conditions) is immutable; corrections require creating a nOnce a decision record status is Issued (i.e., voting closed and chairman has acted), the decision content (outcome, rationale, alternatives, conditions) is immutable; corrections require creating a new superseding decision.M1ImplementedbriefADR-0009; P-061
FR-067functionalAs a Chairman or Secretary, I want to link a decision to a downstream artifact (Action, Risk) before marking it Issued, with at least one downstream link required, so that every decision drives followAs a Chairman or Secretary, I want to link a decision to a downstream artifact (Action, Risk) before marking it Issued, with at least one downstream link required, so that every decision drives follow-through.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-068functionalChairman shall be able to promote a committee decision to an ADR: a new ADR record (ADR-…) is created pre-filled from the decision record; the decision and ADR are bidirectionally linked.Chairman shall be able to promote a committee decision to an ADR: a new ADR record (ADR-…) is created pre-filled from the decision record; the decision and ADR are bidirectionally linked.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.11; README §E1
FR-069functionalAs any committee member, I want to view a filterable decision history list (by outcome, topic, stream, date range, chairman), so that I can research past governance decisions efficiently.As any committee member, I want to view a filterable decision history list (by outcome, topic, stream, date range, chairman), so that I can research past governance decisions efficiently.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-070functionalAs a Secretary, I want to configure a vote on a topic specifying the eligible voter list, voting options, quorum threshold, and whether abstentions are allowed, so that the formal ballot is correctly As a Secretary, I want to configure a vote on a topic specifying the eligible voter list, voting options, quorum threshold, and whether abstentions are allowed, so that the formal ballot is correctly set up.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-071functionalAs a Secretary, I want the system to prevent closing a vote unless the quorum threshold is met (eligible voters present ≥ quorum count), so that no decision is ratified without sufficient participatioAs a Secretary, I want the system to prevent closing a vote unless the quorum threshold is met (eligible voters present ≥ quorum count), so that no decision is ratified without sufficient participation.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-072functionalAs a Member or Chairman, I want to cast my attributed vote (one vote per session, no double voting), so that my position is on record.As a Member or Chairman, I want to cast my attributed vote (one vote per session, no double voting), so that my position is on record.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-073functionalVoting is always attributed in v1: all individual voter choices are recorded and visible to Auditor and Chairman; aggregate totals are also displayed. (Vote anonymity is out of scope for v1.)Voting is always attributed in v1: all individual voter choices are recorded and visible to Auditor and Chairman; aggregate totals are also displayed. (Vote anonymity is out of scope for v1.)M1ImplementedbriefADR-00101
FR-074functionalAs a Chairman, I want to record my final action on a closed vote — Confirm (ratify majority), Override (select different outcome with mandatory reason), or Abstain-from-override — with my action recorAs a Chairman, I want to record my final action on a closed vote — Confirm (ratify majority), Override (select different outcome with mandatory reason), or Abstain-from-override — with my action recorded explicitly and immutably, so that the committee's record reflects my authority decision.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-075functionalAs an Auditor or Chairman, I want all vote records (each voter's choice, timestamp, chairman action, quorum check result) to be immutable and stored in the append-only audit log after vote close, so tAs an Auditor or Chairman, I want all vote records (each voter's choice, timestamp, chairman action, quorum check result) to be immutable and stored in the append-only audit log after vote close, so that the ballot cannot be altered retroactively.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-076functionalThe system shall support a conflict-of-interest flag per voter per vote: a voter may declare a conflict; the declaration is recorded without automatically excluding the voter (exclusion is a chairman The system shall support a conflict-of-interest flag per voter per vote: a voter may declare a conflict; the declaration is recorded without automatically excluding the voter (exclusion is a chairman decision).S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.71
FR-077functionalAs a Secretary, I want the vote status to follow the strict lifecycle Configured → Open → Closed → Ratified with no backward transitions, so that the ballot process is procedurally correct.As a Secretary, I want the vote status to follow the strict lifecycle Configured → Open → Closed → Ratified with no backward transitions, so that the ballot process is procedurally correct.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-078functionalAs an eligible voter, I want to receive an in-app notification when a vote is opened on a topic I am eligible for, so that I can cast my ballot without missing the window.As an eligible voter, I want to receive an in-app notification when a vote is opened on a topic I am eligible for, so that I can cast my ballot without missing the window.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-079functionalAs a Secretary, Chairman, or topic Owner, I want to create an action item linked to a topic, decision, or risk with a mandatory owner and due date, so that every follow-through commitment has an accouAs a Secretary, Chairman, or topic Owner, I want to create an action item linked to a topic, decision, or risk with a mandatory owner and due date, so that every follow-through commitment has an accountable person and a deadline.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-080functionalThe system shall prevent creation of an action without an owner and a due date; these are required fields enforced at the API and UI layer.The system shall prevent creation of an action without an owner and a due date; these are required fields enforced at the API and UI layer.M1ApprovedbriefP-13; brief §6.81
FR-081functionalAs an action Owner, I want to update my action's status (Open → InProgress → Blocked → Completed) and add timestamped progress notes, so that the committee can track implementation without chasing me As an action Owner, I want to update my action's status (Open → InProgress → Blocked → Completed) and add timestamped progress notes, so that the committee can track implementation without chasing me in meetings.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-082functionalAs any committee member, I want overdue actions (due date past, status not Completed/Verified/Cancelled) to be visually flagged automatically without any user action required, so that stalled work is As any committee member, I want overdue actions (due date past, status not Completed/Verified/Cancelled) to be visually flagged automatically without any user action required, so that stalled work is always visible.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-083functionalAs an action Owner, I want to receive an in-app reminder N days before my action is due (configurable, default 3 days), so that I can plan completion without missing the deadline.As an action Owner, I want to receive an in-app reminder N days before my action is due (configurable, default 3 days), so that I can plan completion without missing the deadline.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-084functionalAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want the system to send an escalation notification when an action is overdue beyond a configurable threshold (default 2 days past due), so that chronic delays are surfaceAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want the system to send an escalation notification when an action is overdue beyond a configurable threshold (default 2 days past due), so that chronic delays are surfaced for intervention.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-085functionalAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to mark a completed action as Verified (recorded with actor and timestamp), and the verifier must not be the action's owner, so that self-certification of completionAs a Secretary or Chairman, I want to mark a completed action as Verified (recorded with actor and timestamp), and the verifier must not be the action's owner, so that self-certification of completion is prevented (SoD-1).M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-086functionalAs a Secretary, I want to cancel an action with a mandatory cancellation reason, and the cancelled action remains in the topic's history and is never deleted, so that the audit trail is complete.As a Secretary, I want to cancel an action with a mandatory cancellation reason, and the cancelled action remains in the topic's history and is never deleted, so that the audit trail is complete.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-087functionalAs any committee member, I want to view an actions dashboard showing all open, blocked, and overdue actions across all topics (filterable by owner, stream, topic, due date, status), so that I have a lAs any committee member, I want to view an actions dashboard showing all open, blocked, and overdue actions across all topics (filterable by owner, stream, topic, due date, status), so that I have a live view of implementation health.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
