FR-088functionalAs any committee member, I want the topic detail page to show all actions linked to that topic with their current status, so that I can assess outstanding commitments without leaving the topic contextAs any committee member, I want the topic detail page to show all actions linked to that topic with their current status, so that I can assess outstanding commitments without leaving the topic context.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-089functionalAs a Secretary, Chairman, or Member, I want to create a risk record linked to a topic or action, specifying likelihood, impact, owner, and an optional mitigation plan, so that risks are formally captuAs a Secretary, Chairman, or Member, I want to create a risk record linked to a topic or action, specifying likelihood, impact, owner, and an optional mitigation plan, so that risks are formally captured and visible.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-090functionalAs a Secretary, I want the system to prevent closing a risk without a recorded mitigation plan or an explicit Accepted status with acceptance rationale, so that risks are not silently abandoned.As a Secretary, I want the system to prevent closing a risk without a recorded mitigation plan or an explicit Accepted status with acceptance rationale, so that risks are not silently abandoned.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-091functionalAs a risk Owner, I want to update my risk's status through the lifecycle (Open → Mitigating → Closed; or Accepted/Escalated), so that the committee can track mitigation progress.As a risk Owner, I want to update my risk's status through the lifecycle (Open → Mitigating → Closed; or Accepted/Escalated), so that the committee can track mitigation progress.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-092functionalWhen a risk is Escalated, a notification shall be sent to Secretary and Chairman with the risk summary.When a risk is Escalated, a notification shall be sent to Secretary and Chairman with the risk summary.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.91
FR-093functionalAs any committee member, I want the topic detail page to show all risks linked to that topic with current status and a likelihood/impact indicator, so that risk exposure is visible in context.As any committee member, I want the topic detail page to show all risks linked to that topic with current status and a likelihood/impact indicator, so that risk exposure is visible in context.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-094functionalAs a Secretary, Chairman, or Member, I want to create a typed dependency edge (Blocks, DependsOn, RelatesTo) between two entities (topic, action, system), so that cross-topic and cross-stream blockingAs a Secretary, Chairman, or Member, I want to create a typed dependency edge (Blocks, DependsOn, RelatesTo) between two entities (topic, action, system), so that cross-topic and cross-stream blocking relationships are formally recorded.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-095functionalAs any committee member, I want the system to visually flag dependency edges that cross stream boundaries, so that cross-stream dependencies are immediately visible and can be coordinated.As any committee member, I want the system to visually flag dependency edges that cross stream boundaries, so that cross-stream dependencies are immediately visible and can be coordinated.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-096functionalThe system shall perform an impact analysis query: given entity X, return all entities transitively blocked by or dependent on X via SQL graph traversal; result is displayed as a list and optionally rThe system shall perform an impact analysis query: given entity X, return all entities transitively blocked by or dependent on X via SQL graph traversal; result is displayed as a list and optionally rendered as a Tarseem dependency diagram.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.10; ADR-00081
FR-097functionalA dependency graph view (rendered via Tarseem dependency family) shall be available on the topic detail page and the dependency module view, showing the local dependency neighborhood of a selected entA dependency graph view (rendered via Tarseem dependency family) shall be available on the topic detail page and the dependency module view, showing the local dependency neighborhood of a selected entity.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.10; ADR-00061
FR-098functionalAs any committee member, I want the topic detail page to show inbound and outbound dependency edges, so that I understand what this topic blocks and what it depends on.As any committee member, I want the topic detail page to show inbound and outbound dependency edges, so that I understand what this topic blocks and what it depends on.M1Approvedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-099functionalSecretary or Chairman shall be able to create an ADR (in-app ADR-… ID) using the MADR-lite template: title, status, context, decision, consequences, alternatives (structured list), date.Secretary or Chairman shall be able to create an ADR (in-app ADR-… ID) using the MADR-lite template: title, status, context, decision, consequences, alternatives (structured list), date.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.11; digest §5.51
FR-100functionalADR status lifecycle shall be: Draft → Proposed → Approved → (Superseded | Deprecated). No backward transitions from Approved.ADR status lifecycle shall be: Draft → Proposed → Approved → (Superseded | Deprecated). No backward transitions from Approved.S0ApprovedbriefREADME §E; brief §6.111
FR-101functionalA superseded ADR shall link to its superseding ADR; both records are preserved and immutable after the supersession event.A superseded ADR shall link to its superseding ADR; both records are preserved and immutable after the supersession event.S0ApprovedbriefADR-0009; brief §6.111
FR-102functionalThe ADR repository shall display a searchable list of all ADRs, filterable by status, date, author, and affected stream; full-text search on ADR content via SQL FTS.The ADR repository shall display a searchable list of all ADRs, filterable by status, date, author, and affected stream; full-text search on ADR content via SQL FTS.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.111
FR-103functionalAn ADR shall be linkable to a committee decision (DECN-…), topic (TOP-…), and architecture invariant (AIV-…) in the traceability graph; these links shall appear on the ADR detail page and on the linkeAn ADR shall be linkable to a committee decision (DECN-…), topic (TOP-…), and architecture invariant (AIV-…) in the traceability graph; these links shall appear on the ADR detail page and on the linked entity's detail page.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.11; ADR-00081
FR-104functionalThe ADR detail page shall render the ADR content in formatted Markdown; the raw Markdown is downloadable.The ADR detail page shall render the ADR content in formatted Markdown; the raw Markdown is downloadable.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.111
FR-105functionalSecretary shall be able to manage ADR templates (TPL-…): create, edit, set as default for ADR type; templates are stored in the Knowledge module.Secretary shall be able to manage ADR templates (TPL-…): create, edit, set as default for ADR type; templates are stored in the Knowledge module.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.131
FR-106functionalSecretary or Chairman shall be able to create an Architecture Invariant (AIV-…): category (Security/Performance/Data/Interoperability/Other [unverified — validate categories with committee]), scope (sSecretary or Chairman shall be able to create an Architecture Invariant (AIV-…): category (Security/Performance/Data/Interoperability/Other [unverified — validate categories with committee]), scope (single-stream/multi-stream/platform/org-wide), statement (required), rationale (required), owner (required).S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.121
FR-107functionalInvariant status lifecycle shall be: Draft → Proposed → Active → (Retired | Superseded).Invariant status lifecycle shall be: Draft → Proposed → Active → (Retired | Superseded).S0ApprovedbriefREADME §E; brief §6.121
FR-108functionalThe system shall support recording a violation event against an active invariant: violation description, discovering entity (topic, audit, incident), severity, and a link to the topic or action that wThe system shall support recording a violation event against an active invariant: violation description, discovering entity (topic, audit, incident), severity, and a link to the topic or action that will remediate it.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.121
FR-109functionalThe invariant list shall display all active invariants with violation count; violations are filterable by category, scope, and severity.The invariant list shall display all active invariants with violation count; violations are filterable by category, scope, and severity.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.121
FR-110functionalAn invariant exception request workflow (submit → Secretary review → Chairman approval) shall be supported as an in-app process.An invariant exception request workflow (submit → Secretary review → Chairman approval) shall be supported as an in-app process.C0Approvedbriefbrief §6.121
FR-111functionalSecretary shall be able to create a Research Mission record (RMS-…): title, description, linked topic, Keystone package reference (URL or path), status.Secretary shall be able to create a Research Mission record (RMS-…): title, description, linked topic, Keystone package reference (URL or path), status.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.14; ADR-0007; digest §5.21
FR-112functionalThe system shall import a Keystone package's structured artifacts (findings, recommendations, decisions, risks, acceptance criteria) from a referenced Keystone package and map them to ACMP domain entiThe system shall import a Keystone package's structured artifacts (findings, recommendations, decisions, risks, acceptance criteria) from a referenced Keystone package and map them to ACMP domain entities: Finding (FND-…), Recommendation (REC-…), Risk (RSK-…).S0ApprovedbriefADR-0007; digest §5.2; deferred out of P15 → future (D-05, 2026-07-12)1
FR-113functionalEach imported Finding (FND-…) and Recommendation (REC-…) shall be a first-class artifact: individually viewable, linkable in the traceability graph, and associable with decisions and actions.Each imported Finding (FND-…) and Recommendation (REC-…) shall be a first-class artifact: individually viewable, linkable in the traceability graph, and associable with decisions and actions.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.14; ADR-00081
FR-114functionalThe Research Mission detail page shall display: the Keystone package reference, import status, all imported findings/recommendations/risks, and linked ACMP artifacts (topics, decisions, actions).The Research Mission detail page shall display: the Keystone package reference, import status, all imported findings/recommendations/risks, and linked ACMP artifacts (topics, decisions, actions).S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.141
FR-115functionalA Research Mission shall be linkable to a topic (as the output of a ResearchDiscovery or ResearchRequired decision); this link shall appear in the traceability graph.A Research Mission shall be linkable to a topic (as the output of a ResearchDiscovery or ResearchRequired decision); this link shall appear in the traceability graph.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.14; ADR-00081
FR-116functionalSecretary shall be able to create and manage wiki/knowledge-base pages (DOC-…) in Markdown with: title, category, content, and cross-links to other pages or ACMP artifacts.Secretary shall be able to create and manage wiki/knowledge-base pages (DOC-…) in Markdown with: title, category, content, and cross-links to other pages or ACMP artifacts.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.141
FR-117functionalWiki pages shall be versioned: each save creates an immutable version; previous versions are viewable and diffable.Wiki pages shall be versioned: each save creates an immutable version; previous versions are viewable and diffable.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.14; derived: audit1
FR-118functionalWiki content shall be indexed in SQL Server FTS and searchable alongside topics, decisions, and ADRs in the global search.Wiki content shall be indexed in SQL Server FTS and searchable alongside topics, decisions, and ADRs in the global search.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.141
FR-119functionalSecretary shall be able to create, edit, and delete templates (TPL-…) for artifact types: Topics (by type), MoM, ADR, and Research Mission. Templates are Markdown with placeholder fields.Secretary shall be able to create, edit, and delete templates (TPL-…) for artifact types: Topics (by type), MoM, ADR, and Research Mission. Templates are Markdown with placeholder fields.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.131
FR-120functionalTemplates shall be selectable at artifact creation time; the template's Markdown content pre-fills the description/content field and is editable by the user.Templates shall be selectable at artifact creation time; the template's Markdown content pre-fills the description/content field and is editable by the user.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.131
FR-121functionalSecretary or Member shall be able to create a diagram (DGM-…) by submitting a JSON spec conforming to the Tarseem v1.0 schema; the spec is stored as the version-controlled source of truth in SQL ServeSecretary or Member shall be able to create a diagram (DGM-…) by submitting a JSON spec conforming to the Tarseem v1.0 schema; the spec is stored as the version-controlled source of truth in SQL Server (JSON column).S0ApprovedbriefADR-0006; digest §5.11
FR-122functionalThe system shall render a diagram by submitting the JSON spec to the Tarseem sidecar (containerized render endpoint) via a Hangfire background job; the generated artifacts (SVG, PNG, PDF, draw.io, PPTThe system shall render a diagram by submitting the JSON spec to the Tarseem sidecar (containerized render endpoint) via a Hangfire background job; the generated artifacts (SVG, PNG, PDF, draw.io, PPTX) are stored via `IFileStore` with the spec hash recorded.S0ApprovedbriefADR-0006; digest §5.11
FR-123functionalOn diagram spec update, the system shall queue a re-render job; the previous artifact version is retained and accessible in diagram version history.On diagram spec update, the system shall queue a re-render job; the previous artifact version is retained and accessible in diagram version history.S0ApprovedbriefADR-00061
FR-124functionalEach generated artifact shall record the Tarseem engine version and spec hash used to produce it, enabling future reproducibility and diff.Each generated artifact shall record the Tarseem engine version and spec hash used to produce it, enabling future reproducibility and diff.S0Approvedbriefdigest §5.1; ADR-00061
FR-125functionalTarseem render errors shall be surfaced to the diagram author with the structured error list (code, path, message, hint) returned by `tarseem.generate()`; no silent failures.Tarseem render errors shall be surfaced to the diagram author with the structured error list (code, path, message, hint) returned by `tarseem.generate()`; no silent failures.S0Approvedbriefdigest §5.11
FR-126functionalDiagrams shall be attachable to topics (TOP-…), ADRs (ADR-…), and decisions (DECN-…) via the traceability relationship model.Diagrams shall be attachable to topics (TOP-…), ADRs (ADR-…), and decisions (DECN-…) via the traceability relationship model.S0ApprovedbriefADR-0008; brief §6.151
FR-127functionalAll available export formats (SVG, PNG, PDF, draw.io, PPTX) shall be downloadable from the diagram detail page.All available export formats (SVG, PNG, PDF, draw.io, PPTX) shall be downloadable from the diagram detail page.S0Approvedbriefdigest §5.11
FR-128functionalDiagram version history shall display: spec diff between versions, render timestamp, artifact hash, and engine version.Diagram version history shall display: spec diff between versions, render timestamp, artifact hash, and engine version.S0ApprovedbriefADR-00061
FR-129functionalAll outbound notifications shall be dispatched via the `INotificationChannel` abstraction. The **in-app notification center** is the sole concrete implementation in v1. The Webex adapter (Phase 2) andAll outbound notifications shall be dispatched via the `INotificationChannel` abstraction. The **in-app notification center** is the sole concrete implementation in v1. The Webex adapter (Phase 2) and an Email adapter (deferred until SMTP relay available) are planned future implementations. No notification code hard-codes a specific channel; no org notification platform is used (CON-001).M1ImplementedbriefADR-00051
FR-130functionalThe system shall send an **in-app notification** for each event in the notification catalog: topic status change, agenda published, vote opened, vote closed, MoM published, action reminder, action oveThe system shall send an **in-app notification** for each event in the notification catalog: topic status change, agenda published, vote opened, vote closed, MoM published, action reminder, action overdue, action escalation, risk escalation (v1). Webex Adaptive Card (v1.3 compliant, ≤80KB) notifications are Phase 2; email notifications are deferred.M1ImplementedbriefADR-0005; brief §6.171
FR-131functionalEach notification shall include a deep link to the relevant artifact in ACMP (e.g., clicking a vote-open notification takes the recipient directly to the voting UI for that topic).Each notification shall include a deep link to the relevant artifact in ACMP (e.g., clicking a vote-open notification takes the recipient directly to the voting UI for that topic).M1Implementedbriefbrief §6.171
FR-132functionalOutbound integration/notification dispatch shall back off and retry transient failures via the background-job/outbox mechanism; delivery failures are logged and surfaced in the admin dashboard. (WebexOutbound integration/notification dispatch shall back off and retry transient failures via the background-job/outbox mechanism; delivery failures are logged and surfaced in the admin dashboard. (Webex-specific HTTP 429 + Retry-After handling lands with the Webex adapter in Phase 2.)M1Implementedbriefdigest §5.31
FR-133functionalThe system shall support a user notification preferences page (per-user, per-event-type: on/off) for all non-mandatory notifications.The system shall support a user notification preferences page (per-user, per-event-type: on/off) for all non-mandatory notifications.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.171
FR-134functionalA Hangfire job shall generate and send a daily/weekly digest of pending actions, upcoming meetings, and open votes to opted-in users.A Hangfire job shall generate and send a daily/weekly digest of pending actions, upcoming meetings, and open votes to opted-in users.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.171
FR-135functionalAs any authenticated committee member, I want a committee dashboard showing backlog summary by status/urgency, the next scheduled meeting with agenda link, open action counts by status, overdue actionAs any authenticated committee member, I want a committee dashboard showing backlog summary by status/urgency, the next scheduled meeting with agenda link, open action counts by status, overdue action count, and the last 5 decisions issued, so that I get an immediate health snapshot on login.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-136functionalAs a Secretary, I want my dashboard to show topics in triage queue, pending MoM approvals, overdue actions awaiting escalation, and aging topics beyond SLA, so that I can immediately identify what neeAs a Secretary, I want my dashboard to show topics in triage queue, pending MoM approvals, overdue actions awaiting escalation, and aging topics beyond SLA, so that I can immediately identify what needs my attention.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-137functionalAs a Chairman, I want my dashboard to show votes pending my approval, escalated risks, escalated actions, and topics deferred ≥2 times, so that I can focus on the items that require my authority.As a Chairman, I want my dashboard to show votes pending my approval, escalated risks, escalated actions, and topics deferred ≥2 times, so that I can focus on the items that require my authority.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-138functionalThe system shall provide a per-stream report: all topics, decisions, actions, and risks affecting a given stream, filterable by date range and status.The system shall provide a per-stream report: all topics, decisions, actions, and risks affecting a given stream, filterable by date range and status.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.161
FR-139functionalThe system shall provide a decision history report: all decisions in a date range, filterable by outcome and stream, exportable to CSV.The system shall provide a decision history report: all decisions in a date range, filterable by outcome and stream, exportable to CSV.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.161
FR-140functionalThe system shall provide an action completion rate trend chart (weekly/monthly): open vs. closed vs. overdue over time.The system shall provide an action completion rate trend chart (weekly/monthly): open vs. closed vs. overdue over time.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.161
FR-141functionalThe system shall provide a KPI/health dashboard with: average topic-to-decision days by type, action SLA compliance %, backlog age distribution, and vote-to-ratification time.The system shall provide a KPI/health dashboard with: average topic-to-decision days by type, action SLA compliance %, backlog age distribution, and vote-to-ratification time.C0Approvedbriefbrief §6.16; `docs/domain/metrics-kpi-catalog.md`1
FR-142functionalAll tabular reports shall be exportable to CSV; charts shall be exportable to PNG.All tabular reports shall be exportable to CSV; charts shall be exportable to PNG.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.161
FR-143functionalAs any committee member, I want a global search bar accessible from every page that searches across topics, decisions, and MoMs (by title, description, and content), with results grouped by artifact tAs any committee member, I want a global search bar accessible from every page that searches across topics, decisions, and MoMs (by title, description, and content), with results grouped by artifact type showing ID, title, excerpt, status, and a deep link, so that I can find any record quickly.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-144functionalGlobal search results shall be grouped by artifact type and display: ID, title, matched excerpt, status, and a deep link to the artifact.Global search results shall be grouped by artifact type and display: ID, title, matched excerpt, status, and a deep link to the artifact.M1Approvedbriefbrief §6.181
FR-145functionalAs any committee member who works in Arabic, I want global search to correctly process Arabic queries (Arabic word-breaking), so that I get relevant results when I search in Arabic.As any committee member who works in Arabic, I want global search to correctly process Arabic queries (Arabic word-breaking), so that I get relevant results when I search in Arabic.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-146functionalAs any committee member, I want every artifact (topic, decision, action, risk, dependency, MoM) to display a traceability panel showing all upstream and downstream typed relationships, so that I can nAs any committee member, I want every artifact (topic, decision, action, risk, dependency, MoM) to display a traceability panel showing all upstream and downstream typed relationships, so that I can navigate the governance chain from any point.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-147functionalAs a Secretary, I want to create, read, and delete typed traceability edges between any two artifacts (DerivedFrom, Supersedes, Implements, Resolves, References, Blocks, DependsOn, RelatesTo), so thatAs a Secretary, I want to create, read, and delete typed traceability edges between any two artifacts (DerivedFrom, Supersedes, Implements, Resolves, References, Blocks, DependsOn, RelatesTo), so that the governance lineage is explicitly modeled.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-148functionalThe system shall perform a transitive impact analysis query: given artifact X, return all artifacts reachable via any relationship type within a configurable depth limit (default 3 hops); results dispThe system shall perform a transitive impact analysis query: given artifact X, return all artifacts reachable via any relationship type within a configurable depth limit (default 3 hops); results displayed as a navigable list.S0ApprovedbriefADR-0008; brief §6.181
FR-149functionalThe system shall generate a traceability matrix for a topic (TOP-…): a table of the topic and all linked artifacts by type and relationship, exportable to CSV.The system shall generate a traceability matrix for a topic (TOP-…): a table of the topic and all linked artifacts by type and relationship, exportable to CSV.C0Approvedbriefbrief §6.181
FR-150functionalAs an Auditor or Administrator, I want every state transition, field update, and user action on governed entities (topics, decisions, votes, actions, risks, dependencies, MoMs) to be recorded automatiAs an Auditor or Administrator, I want every state transition, field update, and user action on governed entities (topics, decisions, votes, actions, risks, dependencies, MoMs) to be recorded automatically in an append-only audit log, so that the complete history of committee activity is available for compliance review.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-151functionalEach audit log entry shall record: entity type, entity ID, action type, actor user ID, timestamp (UTC), before-state (JSON), after-state (JSON), and correlation ID.Each audit log entry shall record: entity type, entity ID, action type, actor user ID, timestamp (UTC), before-state (JSON), after-state (JSON), and correlation ID.M1ApprovedbriefADR-00091
FR-152functionalAs an Auditor or Administrator, I want audit log entries to be immutable — no API, UI, or database-level process can update or delete an audit row in normal operation — so that the log is a tamper-eviAs an Auditor or Administrator, I want audit log entries to be immutable — no API, UI, or database-level process can update or delete an audit row in normal operation — so that the log is a tamper-evident record.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-153functionalAs an Auditor or Administrator, I want to search the audit log by entity type, entity ID, actor, action type, and date range (paginated results), so that I can investigate specific events efficiently.As an Auditor or Administrator, I want to search the audit log by entity type, entity ID, actor, action type, and date range (paginated results), so that I can investigate specific events efficiently.M1Implementedbriefv1:register-row1
FR-154functionalThe audit log shall be exportable to CSV or JSON for a date range and entity filter; this export is accessible only to Auditor and Administrator.The audit log shall be exportable to CSV or JSON for a date range and entity filter; this export is accessible only to Auditor and Administrator.S0Approvedbriefbrief §6.181
FR-155functionalData retention policy: all records are retained; retention is **configurable** so legal can set periods later; **no automatic purge in v1**. Votes, issued decisions, ADRs, and published minutes are imData retention policy: all records are retained; retention is **configurable** so legal can set periods later; **no automatic purge in v1**. Votes, issued decisions, ADRs, and published minutes are immutable. Specific retention periods (e.g., audit log years) to be set by org compliance/legal team [unverified — validate with org].S0Approvedbriefderived: gov compliance; `docs/domain/audit-and-records.md`1
NFR-001non-functionalPage load time (P95) for any page rendered with full data (topic list, dashboard, meeting detail) shall not exceed 2 seconds on a standard office network connection (≥10 Mbps). **Target:** P95 ≤ 2 000Page load time (P95) for any page rendered with full data (topic list, dashboard, meeting detail) shall not exceed 2 seconds on a standard office network connection (≥10 Mbps). **Target:** P95 ≤ 2 000 ms.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Performance; README §A1
NFR-002non-functionalAPI response time (P95) for list/query endpoints (backlog list, search results, audit log query) with up to 10 000 topic records shall not exceed 1 second. **Target:** P95 ≤ 1 000 ms.API response time (P95) for list/query endpoints (backlog list, search results, audit log query) with up to 10 000 topic records shall not exceed 1 second. **Target:** P95 ≤ 1 000 ms.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Performance; FR-0021
NFR-003non-functionalAPI response time (P95) for individual artifact detail retrieval (topic, decision, meeting) shall not exceed 500 ms. **Target:** P95 ≤ 500 ms.API response time (P95) for individual artifact detail retrieval (topic, decision, meeting) shall not exceed 500 ms. **Target:** P95 ≤ 500 ms.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Performance; FR-0031
NFR-004non-functionalFull-text search query (SQL FTS) over topic titles, descriptions, and decision rationale with up to 10 000 records shall return results in ≤ 800 ms P95. **Target:** P95 ≤ 800 ms.Full-text search query (SQL FTS) over topic titles, descriptions, and decision rationale with up to 10 000 records shall return results in ≤ 800 ms P95. **Target:** P95 ≤ 800 ms.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Performance; ADR-00111
NFR-005non-functionalTarseem diagram render (via sidecar Hangfire job) for a diagram spec of typical complexity (≤50 nodes, ≤100 edges) shall complete within 30 seconds of job enqueue. **Target:** P95 ≤ 30 s (end-to-end fTarseem diagram render (via sidecar Hangfire job) for a diagram spec of typical complexity (≤50 nodes, ≤100 edges) shall complete within 30 seconds of job enqueue. **Target:** P95 ≤ 30 s (end-to-end from enqueue to artifact stored).Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Performance; ADR-00061
NFR-006non-functionalMeeting notes auto-save (debounce on typing pause) shall complete a server-side persist within 2 seconds of the debounce trigger. **Target:** ≤ 2 000 ms round-trip from debounce trigger to server ACK.Meeting notes auto-save (debounce on typing pause) shall complete a server-side persist within 2 seconds of the debounce trigger. **Target:** ≤ 2 000 ms round-trip from debounce trigger to server ACK.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Performance; FR-0521
NFR-007non-functionalBackground job execution (Hangfire): action-overdue detection job shall process all open actions and send notifications within 5 minutes of scheduled run time. **Target:** Processing lag ≤ 5 min from Background job execution (Hangfire): action-overdue detection job shall process all open actions and send notifications within 5 minutes of scheduled run time. **Target:** Processing lag ≤ 5 min from scheduled start.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Performance; ADR-00141
NFR-008non-functionalThe system shall support up to 20 committee user accounts in v1 without degradation in authentication or authorization latency. Right-sized for on-prem, low-traffic deployment. **Target:** 20 user accThe system shall support up to 20 committee user accounts in v1 without degradation in authentication or authorization latency. Right-sized for on-prem, low-traffic deployment. **Target:** 20 user accounts; auth check ≤ 200 ms P95.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Scale and Capacity; README §A1
NFR-009non-functionalThe system shall support up to 500 topics per year (2 500 total over a 5-year operational life) without schema migration, index degradation, or query plan regression. **Target:** 2 500 topic records; The system shall support up to 500 topics per year (2 500 total over a 5-year operational life) without schema migration, index degradation, or query plan regression. **Target:** 2 500 topic records; list query P95 ≤ 1 s (see NFR-002).Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Scale and Capacity; README §A1
NFR-010non-functionalThe system shall support 5 active streams with no hard-coded stream limit; stream count is configuration-driven. **Target:** 5 streams at go-live; configurable to ≥20.The system shall support 5 active streams with no hard-coded stream limit; stream count is configuration-driven. **Target:** 5 streams at go-live; configurable to ≥20.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Scale and Capacity1
NFR-011non-functionalFile attachment storage shall support individual file uploads up to 100 MB; cumulative attachment storage shall be bounded only by the provisioned blob storage capacity (not ACMP application code). **File attachment storage shall support individual file uploads up to 100 MB; cumulative attachment storage shall be bounded only by the provisioned blob storage capacity (not ACMP application code). **Target:** Single file ≤ 100 MB; no application-level cap on total storage.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Scale and Capacity; ADR-00141
NFR-012non-functionalThe audit log shall support append-only writes at ≥ 100 events/second sustained for 60 seconds without data loss or lock contention on the primary transaction. **Target:** ≥ 100 audit log inserts/s unThe audit log shall support append-only writes at ≥ 100 events/second sustained for 60 seconds without data loss or lock contention on the primary transaction. **Target:** ≥ 100 audit log inserts/s under sustained load.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Scale and Capacity; ADR-00091
NFR-013non-functionalThe SQL Server database shall support columnstore-indexed reporting queries (dashboard aggregations) over 3 years of data (estimated 10 000–50 000 rows across governed entities) with result times ≤ 3 The SQL Server database shall support columnstore-indexed reporting queries (dashboard aggregations) over 3 years of data (estimated 10 000–50 000 rows across governed entities) with result times ≤ 3 seconds. **Target:** Dashboard query P95 ≤ 3 000 ms with 50 000-row dataset.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Scale and Capacity; ADR-00031
NFR-014non-functionalThe system shall be available **24×7** with a monthly availability target of **≥ 99.9%**, achieved via simple redundancy and nightly backups (no HA cluster, no horizontal scaling). Right-sized for on-The system shall be available **24×7** with a monthly availability target of **≥ 99.9%**, achieved via simple redundancy and nightly backups (no HA cluster, no horizontal scaling). Right-sized for on-prem, ≤20 users. **The bundled, self-hosted Keycloak and SQL Server are now within ACMP's own availability and backup scope (ADR-0015)** — not external dependencies. **Target:** ≥ 99.9% monthly uptime; ≤ ~44 min downtime/month.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Availability and Reliability; ADR-0015; README §A1
NFR-015non-functionalScheduled maintenance windows (for upgrades, backups, or ops tasks) are acceptable with ≥ 24 h advance notice to committee members; planned windows do not count against availability SLA. **Target:** MScheduled maintenance windows (for upgrades, backups, or ops tasks) are acceptable with ≥ 24 h advance notice to committee members; planned windows do not count against availability SLA. **Target:** Maintenance windows documented in runbook; notification template for committee members.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Availability and Reliability1
NFR-016non-functionalA single application process failure (unhandled exception, OOM) shall not result in data loss; the system shall recover from restart without manual intervention within 2 minutes. **Target:** Recovery A single application process failure (unhandled exception, OOM) shall not result in data loss; the system shall recover from restart without manual intervention within 2 minutes. **Target:** Recovery time after process restart ≤ 2 min; zero data loss for committed transactions.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Availability and Reliability1
NFR-017non-functionalHangfire background job failures shall be retried automatically up to 3 times with exponential backoff (default Hangfire retry policy); persistent failures shall alert Administrator via self-hosted SeHangfire background job failures shall be retried automatically up to 3 times with exponential backoff (default Hangfire retry policy); persistent failures shall alert Administrator via self-hosted Seq alert rule. **Target:** Retry on failure; alert after 3 consecutive failures.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Availability and Reliability; ADR-00141
NFR-018non-functionalThe application shall meet OWASP ASVS 5.0 Level 2 requirements for all applicable chapters. **Target:** 100% of L2 mandatory requirements pass.The application shall meet OWASP ASVS 5.0 Level 2 requirements for all applicable chapters. **Target:** 100% of L2 mandatory requirements pass.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/security-controls.md; OWASP ASVS 5.01
NFR-019non-functionalAll inter-service communication (application ↔ SQL Server, application ↔ Tarseem sidecar, application ↔ Keycloak) shall use TLS 1.2 or higher; TLS 1.0 and 1.1 shall be disabled. **Target:** TLS ≥ 1.2 All inter-service communication (application ↔ SQL Server, application ↔ Tarseem sidecar, application ↔ Keycloak) shall use TLS 1.2 or higher; TLS 1.0 and 1.1 shall be disabled. **Target:** TLS ≥ 1.2 on all connections.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/security-controls.md; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Security1
NFR-020non-functionalAuthentication tokens (JWT from Keycloak) shall be validated on every API request; requests without a valid token return HTTP 401; requests with insufficient role/scope return HTTP 403. No endpoint isAuthentication tokens (JWT from Keycloak) shall be validated on every API request; requests without a valid token return HTTP 401; requests with insufficient role/scope return HTTP 403. No endpoint is unauthenticated except health checks and the OIDC callback. **Target:** 0 unauthenticated non-health endpoints; 401/403 on invalid/insufficient token.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/security-controls.md; ADR-00041
NFR-021non-functionalAll user input shall be validated server-side; SQL parameters shall use parameterized queries or EF Core LINQ only; raw SQL string concatenation with user input is prohibited. **Target:** 0 SQL injectAll user input shall be validated server-side; SQL parameters shall use parameterized queries or EF Core LINQ only; raw SQL string concatenation with user input is prohibited. **Target:** 0 SQL injection vectors detected by SAST scan.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/security-controls.md; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Security1
NFR-022non-functionalThe application shall implement CSRF protection for all state-changing requests (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) using the ASP.NET Core anti-forgery token or SameSite cookie policy. **Target:** 0 CSRF vulneThe application shall implement CSRF protection for all state-changing requests (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) using the ASP.NET Core anti-forgery token or SameSite cookie policy. **Target:** 0 CSRF vulnerabilities per OWASP ZAP scan.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/security-controls.md; OWASP ASVS V41
NFR-023non-functionalSession tokens shall expire after 8 hours of inactivity [unverified — confirm with org security policy]; refresh tokens shall not extend sessions beyond 24 hours without re-authentication. **Target:**Session tokens shall expire after 8 hours of inactivity [unverified — confirm with org security policy]; refresh tokens shall not extend sessions beyond 24 hours without re-authentication. **Target:** Session idle timeout ≤ 8 h; absolute max ≤ 24 h.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/security-controls.md; ADR-00041
NFR-024non-functionalAll application secrets (database connection strings, Keycloak client secret, S3/Blob credentials, API keys) shall be managed via environment variables or a secrets manager; no secrets shall appear inAll application secrets (database connection strings, Keycloak client secret, S3/Blob credentials, API keys) shall be managed via environment variables or a secrets manager; no secrets shall appear in source code, build artifacts, or container images. **Target:** 0 secrets in source code or images (validated by secret scanning).Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/security-controls.md; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Security1
NFR-025non-functionalTranscript and recording data shall be accessible only to roles explicitly authorized (Chairman, Secretary, Auditor); all access to transcript content shall generate an audit log entry. **Target:** RoTranscript and recording data shall be accessible only to roles explicitly authorized (Chairman, Secretary, Auditor); all access to transcript content shall generate an audit log entry. **Target:** Role-gated access; 100% of transcript reads logged.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/security-threat-model.md; ADR-00091
NFR-026non-functionalThe application shall be protected against OWASP LLM01 (Prompt Injection) in all AI-extraction features: all LLM inputs from meeting transcripts shall be treated as untrusted data; LLM-generated outpuThe application shall be protected against OWASP LLM01 (Prompt Injection) in all AI-extraction features: all LLM inputs from meeting transcripts shall be treated as untrusted data; LLM-generated outputs shall never be committed to the system record without explicit human approval action. **Target:** 0 AI-generated content auto-committed; every AI candidate has status=Candidate until human approves.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/security-threat-model.md; OWASP LLM Top 101
NFR-027non-functionalMeeting recording files and transcripts are sensitive data; they shall be stored in the object store (self-hosted MinIO) with access-controlled pre-signed URLs (time-limited, ≤ 1 h expiry); the URL isMeeting recording files and transcripts are sensitive data; they shall be stored in the object store (self-hosted MinIO) with access-controlled pre-signed URLs (time-limited, ≤ 1 h expiry); the URL is never embedded in logs or notifications. **Target:** Pre-signed URLs expire ≤ 1 h; URLs absent from logs.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Privacy; ADR-00141
NFR-028non-functionalStructured logs and OTel traces shall not record personally identifiable information (PII) beyond user ID (pseudonymous); full names, email addresses, and vote content shall not appear in log payloadsStructured logs and OTel traces shall not record personally identifiable information (PII) beyond user ID (pseudonymous); full names, email addresses, and vote content shall not appear in log payloads. **Target:** 0 PII fields in log/trace output.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Privacy1
NFR-029non-functionalVoting is always attributed in v1 (vote anonymity is out of scope for v1, per ADR-0010). Individual voter choices are recorded in the audit log and accessible to Auditor and Chairman; the results viewVoting is always attributed in v1 (vote anonymity is out of scope for v1, per ADR-0010). Individual voter choices are recorded in the audit log and accessible to Auditor and Chairman; the results view displays both individual choices and aggregate totals. **Target:** 100% of vote records are attributed; no anonymization pathway in v1.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Privacy; ADR-00101
NFR-030non-functionalAll user interface components shall conform to WCAG 2.2 Level AA (W3C Recommendation, October 2023). **Target:** 0 WCAG 2.2 AA failures per automated + manual audit.All user interface components shall conform to WCAG 2.2 Level AA (W3C Recommendation, October 2023). **Target:** 0 WCAG 2.2 AA failures per automated + manual audit.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0012; WCAG 2.2 AA1
NFR-031non-functionalAll interactive elements (buttons, links, form fields, DnD handles) shall be keyboard accessible; drag-and-drop operations shall have a keyboard-accessible alternative (move up/down controls). **TargeAll interactive elements (buttons, links, form fields, DnD handles) shall be keyboard accessible; drag-and-drop operations shall have a keyboard-accessible alternative (move up/down controls). **Target:** 100% of interactive elements reachable and operable via keyboard.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0012; WCAG 2.2 AA1
NFR-032non-functionalAll non-decorative images, icons, and data visualizations (charts, diagrams) shall have appropriate alternative text or ARIA labels in both EN and AR. **Target:** 0 non-decorative images without alt tAll non-decorative images, icons, and data visualizations (charts, diagrams) shall have appropriate alternative text or ARIA labels in both EN and AR. **Target:** 0 non-decorative images without alt text per axe-core scan.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0012; WCAG 2.2 AA1
NFR-033non-functionalColor contrast ratio for all text on background and UI component states (normal, hover, focused, disabled) shall meet WCAG 2.2 AA minimum (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text and UI components). *Color contrast ratio for all text on background and UI component states (normal, hover, focused, disabled) shall meet WCAG 2.2 AA minimum (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text and UI components). **Target:** Contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 (body), ≥ 3:1 (large/UI).Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0012; WCAG 2.2 AA1
NFR-034non-functionalStatus indicators (topic urgency, action overdue, risk severity) shall not rely on color alone; they shall also use text labels and/or icons with accessible names. **Target:** 0 color-only status indiStatus indicators (topic urgency, action overdue, risk severity) shall not rely on color alone; they shall also use text labels and/or icons with accessible names. **Target:** 0 color-only status indicators per WCAG 1.4.1.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0012; WCAG 2.2 (1.4.1)1
NFR-035non-functionalAll UI strings shall be externalized in `react-i18next` resource files; no hardcoded display strings in component code. **Target:** 0 hardcoded EN strings in component JSX/TSX.All UI strings shall be externalized in `react-i18next` resource files; no hardcoded display strings in component code. **Target:** 0 hardcoded EN strings in component JSX/TSX.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0012; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Localization1
NFR-036non-functionalThe Arabic locale shall trigger full RTL layout via the `dir="rtl"` attribute on `<html>` and CSS logical properties throughout; no `left`/`right` CSS properties shall appear in component stylesheets.The Arabic locale shall trigger full RTL layout via the `dir="rtl"` attribute on `<html>` and CSS logical properties throughout; no `left`/`right` CSS properties shall appear in component stylesheets. **Target:** 0 `padding-left`, `margin-right`, etc. in component CSS when RTL active.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-00121
NFR-037non-functionalAll date, time, and number formats shall use locale-appropriate formatting: Gregorian calendar in both EN and AR (v1 only; Hijri display is a future option, not in v1). **Target:** Gregorian dates renAll date, time, and number formats shall use locale-appropriate formatting: Gregorian calendar in both EN and AR (v1 only; Hijri display is a future option, not in v1). **Target:** Gregorian dates rendered correctly in both locales; no raw ISO strings visible.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0012; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Localization1
NFR-038non-functionalTarseem diagram output shall render Arabic text correctly (HarfBuzz shaping, RTL geometry mirror) when topic language is AR or diagram content includes Arabic labels. **Target:** 0 garbled Arabic textTarseem diagram output shall render Arabic text correctly (HarfBuzz shaping, RTL geometry mirror) when topic language is AR or diagram content includes Arabic labels. **Target:** 0 garbled Arabic text or incorrect glyph ordering in diagram exports.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0012; ADR-00061
NFR-039non-functionalThe EN↔AR terminology glossary (README §G, localized in design handoff) shall be the single source of canonical bilingual terms; no term shall have more than one AR translation within the application.The EN↔AR terminology glossary (README §G, localized in design handoff) shall be the single source of canonical bilingual terms; no term shall have more than one AR translation within the application. **Target:** 0 terminology inconsistencies detected in the i18n resource files.Must1ApprovedbriefREADME §G; ADR-00121
NFR-040non-functionalThe audit log table shall have no `UPDATE` or `DELETE` triggers, constraints, or ORM operations defined; it is insert-only at the application layer. **Target:** 0 UPDATE/DELETE statements targeting thThe audit log table shall have no `UPDATE` or `DELETE` triggers, constraints, or ORM operations defined; it is insert-only at the application layer. **Target:** 0 UPDATE/DELETE statements targeting the audit log table in application code.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0009; docs/domain/audit-and-records.md1
NFR-041non-functionalVotes (individual vote records) and issued decisions (DECN-…) shall be immutable after their respective immutability events (vote close, decision issue); no API endpoint shall permit field mutation onVotes (individual vote records) and issued decisions (DECN-…) shall be immutable after their respective immutability events (vote close, decision issue); no API endpoint shall permit field mutation on these records after the event. **Target:** 0 API endpoints permitting mutation of closed vote or issued decision fields.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0009; docs/domain/audit-and-records.md1
NFR-042non-functionalEvery state transition on a governed entity shall generate a corresponding audit log entry within the same database transaction; no state transition without an audit log entry is a system defect. **TaEvery state transition on a governed entity shall generate a corresponding audit log entry within the same database transaction; no state transition without an audit log entry is a system defect. **Target:** 100% of state transitions have a corresponding audit log entry within the same TX.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0009; docs/domain/audit-and-records.md1
NFR-043non-functionalEvery inbound HTTP request shall generate an OTel trace with spans for: HTTP handler, DB query, Hangfire job dispatch, and external HTTP calls (Webex, Tarseem sidecar). **Target:** 100% of requests trEvery inbound HTTP request shall generate an OTel trace with spans for: HTTP handler, DB query, Hangfire job dispatch, and external HTTP calls (Webex, Tarseem sidecar). **Target:** 100% of requests traced (sample rate 100% for this traffic volume).Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0014; README §A1
NFR-044non-functionalStructured log entries (Serilog) shall include: `CorrelationId`, `UserId` (pseudonymous), `Module`, `Operation`, `ElapsedMs`, and `LogLevel`; minimum log level in production is `Information`. All logsStructured log entries (Serilog) shall include: `CorrelationId`, `UserId` (pseudonymous), `Module`, `Operation`, `ElapsedMs`, and `LogLevel`; minimum log level in production is `Information`. All logs shipped to the app-owned self-hosted Seq container. **Target:** All log entries contain required fields.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0014; README §A1
NFR-045non-functionalApplication health check endpoints shall respond within 1 second and return: application status, database connectivity, Hangfire connectivity, Seq connectivity, MinIO connectivity, and Tarseem sidecarApplication health check endpoints shall respond within 1 second and return: application status, database connectivity, Hangfire connectivity, Seq connectivity, MinIO connectivity, and Tarseem sidecar status (Phase 2). **Target:** Health check response ≤ 1 000 ms; includes all configured sub-checks.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0014; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Observability1
NFR-046non-functionalAlerts shall be configured in self-hosted Seq for: application error rate > 5 errors/min, Hangfire failed jobs > 3 in 10 min, health check failure ≥ 3 consecutive, and audit log write failure (any). *Alerts shall be configured in self-hosted Seq for: application error rate > 5 errors/min, Hangfire failed jobs > 3 in 10 min, health check failure ≥ 3 consecutive, and audit log write failure (any). **Target:** Alert fires within 60 s of threshold crossing.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0014; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Observability1
NFR-047non-functionalEach canonical module (Membership, Topics, Meetings, Decisions, Actions, Risks, Dependencies, Governance, Research, Knowledge, Diagrams, Notifications, Reporting, Search&Traceability, Audit&Records, PEach canonical module (Membership, Topics, Meetings, Decisions, Actions, Risks, Dependencies, Governance, Research, Knowledge, Diagrams, Notifications, Reporting, Search&Traceability, Audit&Records, Platform) shall be implemented as a distinct project or folder boundary; no module shall reference another module's internal types — only its public contract interface or MediatR command/query. **Target:** 0 cross-module internal type references detected by ArchUnit or dependency analysis rule.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0001; ADR-0002; docs/domain/repository-structure.md1
NFR-048non-functionalUnit test coverage for domain logic (entity state machines, business rules, validation) shall be ≥ 80% line coverage. **Target:** ≥ 80% line coverage on domain layer projects.Unit test coverage for domain logic (entity state machines, business rules, validation) shall be ≥ 80% line coverage. **Target:** ≥ 80% line coverage on domain layer projects.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0016; docs/validation/test-strategy.md1
NFR-049non-functionalIntegration test coverage shall include at least one end-to-end happy-path test and one failure-path test per canonical workflow (topic intake, agenda creation, meeting + MoM, vote + decision, action Integration test coverage shall include at least one end-to-end happy-path test and one failure-path test per canonical workflow (topic intake, agenda creation, meeting + MoM, vote + decision, action lifecycle). **Target:** ≥ 1 happy-path + 1 failure-path integration test per workflow (5 workflows × 2 = ≥ 10 integration tests).Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0016; docs/validation/test-strategy.md1
NFR-050non-functionalDatabase schema migrations shall be backward-compatible with the running application version (expand-contract pattern); no migration shall drop a column or table that the current application version sDatabase schema migrations shall be backward-compatible with the running application version (expand-contract pattern); no migration shall drop a column or table that the current application version still reads. **Target:** 0 breaking schema changes in migration without a corresponding application code change in the same PR.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Maintainability; docs/domain/repository-structure.md1
NFR-051non-functionalAll third-party dependencies shall be declared in `package.json` / `.csproj` with pinned or semver-constrained versions; Dependabot or equivalent shall be configured to alert on known CVEs within 48 hAll third-party dependencies shall be declared in `package.json` / `.csproj` with pinned or semver-constrained versions; Dependabot or equivalent shall be configured to alert on known CVEs within 48 hours. **Target:** 0 unpinned wildcard dependencies; CVE alerts ≤ 48 h.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Maintainability; docs/domain/security-controls.md1
NFR-052non-functionalThe application shall be fully containerized (Docker); a `docker-compose.yml` shall run the complete local development stack (app, SQL Server, self-hosted Seq, self-hosted MinIO, Tarseem sidecar [PhasThe application shall be fully containerized (Docker); a `docker-compose.yml` shall run the complete local development stack (app, SQL Server, self-hosted Seq, self-hosted MinIO, Tarseem sidecar [Phase 2]) with a single `docker compose up` command. **Target:** Single-command local startup ≤ 5 min on a standard developer machine (8-core, 16 GB RAM).Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0013; README §A1
NFR-053non-functionalAll application configuration shall be externalized; switching deployment environments (dev/staging/prod) requires only environment variable changes, not code changes or image rebuilds. **Target:** 0 All application configuration shall be externalized; switching deployment environments (dev/staging/prod) requires only environment variable changes, not code changes or image rebuilds. **Target:** 0 environment-specific values hardcoded in source code or Dockerfile.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0013; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Portability1
NFR-054non-functionalContainer images shall be built from a minimal base image (e.g., `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:8.0-alpine` or equivalent distroless); image size shall not exceed 500 MB. **Target:** Image size ≤ 50Container images shall be built from a minimal base image (e.g., `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:8.0-alpine` or equivalent distroless); image size shall not exceed 500 MB. **Target:** Image size ≤ 500 MB.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0013; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Portability1
NFR-055non-functionalThe system shall not require Kubernetes, a service mesh, or a message broker to run; Docker Compose or equivalent single-host container orchestration is the supported deployment model in v1. **Target:The system shall not require Kubernetes, a service mesh, or a message broker to run; Docker Compose or equivalent single-host container orchestration is the supported deployment model in v1. **Target:** 0 Kubernetes-specific manifests or broker dependencies in v1 deployment artifacts.Must1ApprovedbriefADR-0013; CON-0011
NFR-056non-functionalRecovery Point Objective (RPO): in the event of a catastrophic failure, data loss shall not exceed 4 hours (i.e., database backups occur at least every 4 hours during business days). **Target:** RPO ≤Recovery Point Objective (RPO): in the event of a catastrophic failure, data loss shall not exceed 4 hours (i.e., database backups occur at least every 4 hours during business days). **Target:** RPO ≤ 4 h.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/deployment.md; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Backup and DR1
NFR-057non-functionalRecovery Time Objective (RTO): the system shall be restorable to a functional state within 8 business hours of a declared disaster. **Target:** RTO ≤ 8 h.Recovery Time Objective (RTO): the system shall be restorable to a functional state within 8 business hours of a declared disaster. **Target:** RTO ≤ 8 h.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/deployment.md; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Backup and DR1
NFR-058non-functionalDatabase backups shall be stored in a location separate from the primary database server; backups shall be tested for restorability at least quarterly. **Target:** Backups on separate storage; quarterDatabase backups shall be stored in a location separate from the primary database server; backups shall be tested for restorability at least quarterly. **Target:** Backups on separate storage; quarterly restore test.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/deployment.md; docs/requirements/non-functional.md §Backup and DR1
NFR-059non-functionalAll records (including audit log data) are **retained indefinitely in v1; no automatic purge**. Retention periods are **configurable** so legal/compliance can set them later. Immutable records (votes,All records (including audit log data) are **retained indefinitely in v1; no automatic purge**. Retention periods are **configurable** so legal/compliance can set them later. Immutable records (votes, decisions, ADRs, MoM) are never purged. Specific retention periods (e.g., years) will be determined by org compliance/legal [unverified — validate with org]. **Target:** No automatic purge in v1; configurable retention; immutable records protected.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/audit-and-records.md; ADR-00091
NFR-060non-functionalFile attachments (presentations, recordings, diagrams) are **retained; no automatic purge in v1**. Retention periods are configurable and will be set by legal/ops. No automated archival job deletes atFile attachments (presentations, recordings, diagrams) are **retained; no automatic purge in v1**. Retention periods are configurable and will be set by legal/ops. No automated archival job deletes attachments in v1. **Target:** Configurable retention; no automatic purge in v1.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/domain/audit-and-records.md; ADR-00141
NFR-061non-functionalThe web application shall support the latest two major versions of: Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, and Apple Safari as of the release date. No Internet Explorer support. **Target:** FThe web application shall support the latest two major versions of: Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, and Apple Safari as of the release date. No Internet Explorer support. **Target:** Functional on Chrome N/N-1, Edge N/N-1, Firefox N/N-1, Safari N/N-1.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Compatibility1
NFR-062non-functionalThe application shall be usable on tablet devices (iPad or equivalent, landscape orientation, ≥1024 px viewport width) with no horizontal scroll and no overlapping UI elements. **Target:** 0 UI defectThe application shall be usable on tablet devices (iPad or equivalent, landscape orientation, ≥1024 px viewport width) with no horizontal scroll and no overlapping UI elements. **Target:** 0 UI defects on 1024 px viewport (tablet landscape).Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Compatibility; ADR-00121
NFR-063non-functionalThe application is not required to support mobile phone viewports (< 768 px) in v1; a "not optimized for mobile" notice is acceptable on narrow viewports. **Target:** Mobile (< 768 px) displays a gracThe application is not required to support mobile phone viewports (< 768 px) in v1; a "not optimized for mobile" notice is acceptable on narrow viewports. **Target:** Mobile (< 768 px) displays a graceful not-optimized notice; no broken layout.Must1Approvedbriefdocs/requirements/non-functional.md §Compatibility1
Constraints (15 rows) CSV
idtitlestatementlifecycle_statusdispositiondisposition_reason_refsource_kindsource_spanlast_referenced
CON-001**Self-contained**: no org Hangfire / ELK / Seq / notification platform. ACMP bundles its own — all runtime dependencies bundled (incl. self-hosted Keycloak + SQL Server) per ADR-0015; the only extern**Self-contained**: no org Hangfire / ELK / Seq / notification platform. ACMP bundles its own — all runtime dependencies bundled (incl. self-hosted Keycloak + SQL Server) per ADR-0015; the only external dependency is Webex (Phase 2, via pluggable adapter).Approved
CON-002**Mandated technology stack**: Backend must be .NET 8 (LTS) / ASP.NET Core. Frontend must be React 18 + TypeScript + Vite. Database must be Microsoft SQL Server. No deviations without an explicit ADR.**Mandated technology stack**: Backend must be .NET 8 (LTS) / ASP.NET Core. Frontend must be React 18 + TypeScript + Vite. Database must be Microsoft SQL Server. No deviations without an explicit ADR. (Implication: No Go, Python (except Tarseem sidecar), Java, Postgres, MongoDB, or other stacks for the ACMP application itself.)Approved
CON-003**On-prem VM + Docker Compose; no Kubernetes**: Deployment is Docker Compose on one or two VMs. No Kubernetes, Helm, service mesh, or container orchestration platform. (Implication: Resource limits, s**On-prem VM + Docker Compose; no Kubernetes**: Deployment is Docker Compose on one or two VMs. No Kubernetes, Helm, service mesh, or container orchestration platform. (Implication: Resource limits, secret management, and scaling are all VM/Compose-level concerns. No K8s features (ConfigMaps, Secrets, Ingress controllers) may be assumed.)Approved
CON-004**Scale ceiling ≤20 total users; right-size everything**: No HA cluster, no horizontal scaling, no heavy performance engineering. 99.9% availability via simple standby VM + nightly backups. (Implicati**Scale ceiling ≤20 total users; right-size everything**: No HA cluster, no horizontal scaling, no heavy performance engineering. 99.9% availability via simple standby VM + nightly backups. (Implication: Do not engineer for 1,000 concurrent users. Reject any architectural addition (Redis, message broker, CDN) whose sole justification is scalability beyond 20 users.)Approved
CON-005**No message brokers in ACMP's own infrastructure**: No RabbitMQ, Kafka, or similar broker as part of ACMP's own runtime. Background work is handled by app-owned Hangfire on SQL. (Implication: Hangfir**No message brokers in ACMP's own infrastructure**: No RabbitMQ, Kafka, or similar broker as part of ACMP's own runtime. Background work is handled by app-owned Hangfire on SQL. (Implication: Hangfire + SQL outbox is the messaging layer. Async patterns are task-queue only, not event streaming.)Approved
CON-006**No email in v1**: Email notifications are deferred until an SMTP relay is available. In-app notification center is the sole channel in PH-1. (Implication: `INotificationChannel` abstraction must be **No email in v1**: Email notifications are deferred until an SMTP relay is available. In-app notification center is the sole channel in PH-1. (Implication: `INotificationChannel` abstraction must be in place so email adapter can be added without modifying dispatch code. Alert users only via in-app center and (PH-2) Webex.)Approved
CON-007**No public self-registration or anonymous access**: All access is provisioned by Administrator/Secretary via Keycloak. No public-facing registration endpoint. (Implication: `POST /api/register` does **No public self-registration or anonymous access**: All access is provisioned by Administrator/Secretary via Keycloak. No public-facing registration endpoint. (Implication: `POST /api/register` does not exist. OIDC callback is the only entry point.)Approved
CON-008**EN and AR are both first-class, not additive**: Full bilingual support and RTL layout from the first PH-1 release. Not a v2 retrofit. (Implication: Every UI component must be built with logical CSS **EN and AR are both first-class, not additive**: Full bilingual support and RTL layout from the first PH-1 release. Not a v2 retrofit. (Implication: Every UI component must be built with logical CSS from day 1. Every user-visible string must exist in both locale files before the feature ships. RTL regression must pass in CI.)Approved
CON-009**Keycloak is the sole IdP; roles sourced from Keycloak claims**: ACMP does not store or assign global roles. It reads claims on token validation and maps to canonical roles. (Implication: ACMP cannot**Keycloak is the sole IdP; roles sourced from Keycloak claims**: ACMP does not store or assign global roles. It reads claims on token validation and maps to canonical roles. (Implication: ACMP cannot have a "role management" screen that changes Keycloak data. Role assignment is done in Keycloak; ACMP reads the result.)Approved
CON-010**Single committee; no multi-committee generalization**: v1 is built for exactly one committee. No tenant IDs, no committee selection, no committee-scoped routing. (Implication: Reject any data-model **Single committee; no multi-committee generalization**: v1 is built for exactly one committee. No tenant IDs, no committee selection, no committee-scoped routing. (Implication: Reject any data-model change that adds a committee entity as a root tenant discriminator unless a new explicit decision reverses this.)Approved
CON-011**Voting always attributed in v1; no anonymous voting**: Every voter's choice is recorded and visible to Auditor and Chairman. Anonymous voting mode is out of scope for v1. (Implication: The `anonymit**Voting always attributed in v1; no anonymous voting**: Every voter's choice is recorded and visible to Auditor and Chairman. Anonymous voting mode is out of scope for v1. (Implication: The `anonymity` flag on the vote configuration (FR-070 references it) is an informational field only in v1; the system always records individual votes. Full anonymity support is a future decision.)Approved
CON-012**Gregorian dates only; no Hijri in v1**: All date storage, display, and SLA calculations use Gregorian calendar. (Implication: No Hijri calendar library, no dual-calendar display, no Hijri date input**Gregorian dates only; no Hijri in v1**: All date storage, display, and SLA calculations use Gregorian calendar. (Implication: No Hijri calendar library, no dual-calendar display, no Hijri date input. Localized Gregorian formatting (right-to-left digit order in AR) is acceptable.)Approved
CON-013**Keystone is optional**: The Research module must work fully standalone (manual entry). Keystone import is an enhancement. No hard dependency on Keystone availability. (Implication: Research Mission **Keystone is optional**: The Research module must work fully standalone (manual entry). Keystone import is an enhancement. No hard dependency on Keystone availability. (Implication: Research Mission record can be created without a Keystone package reference. The Keystone import tool is a PH-2 feature, not a gate for the Research module — build since deferred out of P15 → future per D-05, 2026-07-12; optionality unchanged.)Approved
CON-014**Webex is Phase 2, adapter-only**: No Webex calls in PH-1 code. All Webex integration is behind the `INotificationChannel` and `IWebexRecordingAdapter` abstractions. (Implication: PH-1 code must comp**Webex is Phase 2, adapter-only**: No Webex calls in PH-1 code. All Webex integration is behind the `INotificationChannel` and `IWebexRecordingAdapter` abstractions. (Implication: PH-1 code must compile and run without Webex credentials configured. The adapter registers as no-op when Webex config is absent.)Approved
CON-015**AI extraction is Phase 3; manual always available**: No AI features in PH-1 or PH-2. When delivered in PH-3, AI extraction is off by default and requires Admin activation with data-residency confirm**AI extraction is Phase 3; manual always available**: No AI features in PH-1 or PH-2. When delivered in PH-3, AI extraction is off by default and requires Admin activation with data-residency confirmation. (Implication: No LLM client code in PH-1 or PH-2 production code. Phase 3 LLM integration is behind a feature flag.)Approved
