INV-001Do not replace the approved technology stack (.NET 8 / ASP.NET Core / EF Core / SQL Server / React + TypeScript / Vite) without a new ADR.Do not replace the approved technology stack (.NET 8 / ASP.NET Core / EF Core / SQL Server / React + TypeScript / Vite) without a new ADR.ADR-0001/0002/0003/0012; code reviewApproved
INV-002No distributed architecture — no microservices, message brokers (RabbitMQ/Kafka), service mesh, Kubernetes, or a second database — without a demonstrated, measured need recorded in an ADR. The defaultNo distributed architecture — no microservices, message brokers (RabbitMQ/Kafka), service mesh, Kubernetes, or a second database — without a demonstrated, measured need recorded in an ADR. The default is a **modular monolith**.ADR-0001; architecture tests; code reviewApproved
INV-003Do not depend on the organization's runtime infrastructure (CON-001). ACMP self-hosts and **bundles all runtime deps** — incl. self-hosted Keycloak (ACMP-owned realm) + SQL Server — in its own Docker Do not depend on the organization's runtime infrastructure (CON-001). ACMP self-hosts and **bundles all runtime deps** — incl. self-hosted Keycloak (ACMP-owned realm) + SQL Server — in its own Docker Compose stack; zero external runtime services in v1. Only external dependency = Webex (Phase 2).ADR-0013/0014/0015; deployment config reviewApproved
INV-004Do not bypass authorization. Every endpoint and command goes through policy-based authorization (role + ABAC). Enforce least privilege and segregation of duties (verifier ≠ action owner; chairman is nDo not bypass authorization. Every endpoint and command goes through policy-based authorization (role + ABAC). Enforce least privilege and segregation of duties (verifier ≠ action owner; chairman is never sole vote counter).ASP.NET policies; CapabilityHandler (ABAC); testsApproved
INV-005Do not create unaudited changes to governance records. Every state change emits an `AuditEvent`. **Votes, issued decisions, approved ADRs, and published minutes are immutable** — superseded, never ediDo not create unaudited changes to governance records. Every state change emits an `AuditEvent`. **Votes, issued decisions, approved ADRs, and published minutes are immutable** — superseded, never edited or deleted. Hash-chain votes/decisions/audit.ADR-0009; append-only audit; hash chain; testsApproved
INV-006Do not treat AI-extracted content as authoritative. Any AI-suggested minutes/decisions/actions (Phase 3) are **candidates** until a human reviews and approves them. Treat transcripts/briefs as untrustDo not treat AI-extracted content as authoritative. Any AI-suggested minutes/decisions/actions (Phase 3) are **candidates** until a human reviews and approves them. Treat transcripts/briefs as untrusted input (OWASP LLM01).ADR-0007 principle; human approval gateApproved
INV-007Do not store secrets in source control. Use externalized config + Docker secrets / `.env` (git-ignored). No connection strings, tokens, or keys in code or committed files.Do not store secrets in source control. Use externalized config + Docker secrets / `.env` (git-ignored). No connection strings, tokens, or keys in code or committed files.secret scanning; code review; `.gitignore`Approved
INV-008Do not implement a feature without tests and acceptance criteria. Each feature ships with unit + integration tests and satisfies its `AC-###`.Do not implement a feature without tests and acceptance criteria. Each feature ships with unit + integration tests and satisfies its `AC-###`.DoD; CI coverage gate; acceptance auditApproved
INV-009Do not break Arabic or RTL. No hardcoded user-facing strings — everything via i18n (EN + AR). Use CSS logical properties + `dir`. Every screen renders correctly in RTL. Dates are Gregorian (localized Do not break Arabic or RTL. No hardcoded user-facing strings — everything via i18n (EN + AR). Use CSS logical properties + `dir`. Every screen renders correctly in RTL. Dates are Gregorian (localized formatting).i18n parity check; RTL visual regression; reviewApproved
INV-010Do not duplicate Tarseem or Keystone functionality. Diagrams are rendered by Tarseem (JSON spec is the source of truth); research packages come from the optional Keystone workflow. No in-app diagram eDo not duplicate Tarseem or Keystone functionality. Diagrams are rendered by Tarseem (JSON spec is the source of truth); research packages come from the optional Keystone workflow. No in-app diagram engine or research methodology without a documented ADR.ADR-0006/0007; code reviewApproved
INV-011Do not mask assumptions or open decisions. Inferences are recorded as `ASM-###`; unresolved decisions as `OQ-###` (use the recommended default but flag it). Never silently decide a flagged open questiDo not mask assumptions or open decisions. Inferences are recorded as `ASM-###`; unresolved decisions as `OQ-###` (use the recommended default but flag it). Never silently decide a flagged open question.assumption/open-question registers; reviewApproved
INV-012Do not overengineer. Right-size for an on-prem, low-traffic, ≤20-user internal tool. No premature abstraction, speculative generality, or enterprise patterns the problem doesn't demand.Do not overengineer. Right-size for an on-prem, low-traffic, ≤20-user internal tool. No premature abstraction, speculative generality, or enterprise patterns the problem doesn't demand.code review; architecture testsApproved
INV-013Do not commit directly to `main`. Every phase/slice ships on a short-lived branch → PR → green CI → review (GO) → squash-merge → delete branch → sync local `main`. `main` stays green and deployable.Do not commit directly to `main`. Every phase/slice ships on a short-lived branch → PR → green CI → review (GO) → squash-merge → delete branch → sync local `main`. `main` stays green and deployable.branch protection; CI; git workflowApproved
INV-014Do not let the UI drift from the reference design. Any screen with a matching `.dc.html` in [`ACMP product context/`](../../ACMP%20product%20context/) matches it exactly (read directly, not via MCP) —Do not let the UI drift from the reference design. Any screen with a matching `.dc.html` in [`ACMP product context/`](../../ACMP%20product%20context/) matches it exactly (read directly, not via MCP) — tokens/anatomy/states/iconography/RTL/light-dark/copy/AA — composed from the shared design system. No-reference screens compose from the design system + the IA spec and are flagged.design-parity ledger; visual regression; reviewApproved
Assumptions (16 rows) CSV
idtitlestatementrisk_if_wronglifecycle_statusdispositiondisposition_reason_refsource_kindsource_spanlast_referenced
ASM-001~~The org's Keycloak instance will be accessible to ACMP from the deployment VM at all times; ACMP does not need to manage a separate IdP.~~ **RESOLVED — FALSE (ADR-0015):** the org has no Keycloak. M~~The org's Keycloak instance will be accessible to ACMP from the deployment VM at all times; ACMP does not need to manage a separate IdP.~~ **RESOLVED — FALSE (ADR-0015):** the org has no Keycloak. Mitigation realized: Keycloak is now provisioned as a bundled service in the ACMP Compose stack with an ACMP-owned realm (the "scope expansion" path was taken).If false: ACMP needs an internal fallback login path or Keycloak must be provisioned as part of the Compose stack — a scope expansion. **This is now the adopted design (ADR-0015).**Approved
ASM-002Keycloak group/realm-role claims can be configured to carry ACMP's canonical role names (or a mapping can be agreed) without code change to Keycloak itself.Keycloak group/realm-role claims can be configured to carry ACMP's canonical role names (or a mapping can be agreed) without code change to Keycloak itself.If false: ACMP must support a custom claim-mapping configuration layer (already planned in BL-019) or Keycloak admin deploys a custom OIDC mapper.Proposed
ASM-003All committee users (≤20) have access to the deployment VM's network (internal network or VPN); no public internet exposure required.All committee users (≤20) have access to the deployment VM's network (internal network or VPN); no public internet exposure required.If false: TLS termination + network exposure policy must be designed and approved, widening the security perimeter review.Proposed
ASM-004SQL Server will be provisioned on the same network segment as the ACMP Docker Compose stack, with a connection string accessible via environment variable. ACMP owns its own SQL Server instance and schSQL Server will be provisioned on the same network segment as the ACMP Docker Compose stack, with a connection string accessible via environment variable. ACMP owns its own SQL Server instance and schema.If false: shared SQL Server requires schema isolation and resource governance configuration, adding operational risk.Proposed
ASM-005MinIO can be provisioned as a Docker container in the ACMP Compose stack (or as a separately provisioned S3-compatible endpoint) with adequate storage volume mounted from the host.MinIO can be provisioned as a Docker container in the ACMP Compose stack (or as a separately provisioned S3-compatible endpoint) with adequate storage volume mounted from the host.If false: a different S3-compatible provider must be configured behind `IFileStore`; no code change required (abstraction layer), but ops setup changes.Proposed
ASM-006The committee operates a single committee with a stable, known membership; the canonical roles (Chairman, Secretary, Member, etc.) map cleanly to real people. There will be no need to model sub-commitThe committee operates a single committee with a stable, known membership; the canonical roles (Chairman, Secretary, Member, etc.) map cleanly to real people. There will be no need to model sub-committees or working groups in v1.If false: multi-committee model is a significant scope expansion (OOS, explicitly excluded by settled decision).Proposed
ASM-007Dates are Gregorian only; no Hijri calendar support is required in v1 (settled decision, README §A).Dates are Gregorian only; no Hijri calendar support is required in v1 (settled decision, README §A).If false: Hijri calendar is a Phase 3+ item requiring a calendar library and locale configuration that is not currently planned.Proposed
ASM-008The platform will be used by ≤20 total users; no horizontal scaling, no HA cluster, no heavy performance engineering is required. 99.9% availability is achievable via a simple standby VM + nightly bacThe platform will be used by ≤20 total users; no horizontal scaling, no HA cluster, no heavy performance engineering is required. 99.9% availability is achievable via a simple standby VM + nightly backup (settled, README §A).If false: a load balancer, multiple app instances, and session-state externalization are needed — an architectural change.Proposed
ASM-009The organization does not require a public-facing portal or external partner access in v1; ACMP is an internal-only tool.The organization does not require a public-facing portal or external partner access in v1; ACMP is an internal-only tool.If false: a separate security-reviewed external-access layer is required (OOS-12).Proposed
ASM-010The Secretary is the primary product owner and has authority to make functional scope decisions during delivery without escalating each to a governance board.The Secretary is the primary product owner and has authority to make functional scope decisions during delivery without escalating each to a governance board.If false: decision latency will slow sprints; a lightweight approval process must be defined.Proposed
ASM-011The urgency SLA thresholds (Critical: 3 days in status, Urgent: 7 days, Normal: 21 days) are acceptable starting values; the committee will adjust them via configuration after PH-1 pilot. [Marked `[unThe urgency SLA thresholds (Critical: 3 days in status, Urgent: 7 days, Normal: 21 days) are acceptable starting values; the committee will adjust them via configuration after PH-1 pilot. [Marked `[unverified]` in FR-038; must be validated with committee.]If false: SLA thresholds must be redesigned before PH-1 go-live; aging indicator calibration changes.Proposed
ASM-012The committee is willing to adopt the canonical decision outcome vocabulary (11 outcomes in README §E) as-is, possibly with bilingual labels; no additional custom outcomes are required.The committee is willing to adopt the canonical decision outcome vocabulary (11 outcomes in README §E) as-is, possibly with bilingual labels; no additional custom outcomes are required.If false: the outcome enum is expanded; no code architecture change but schema + UI changes required.Proposed
ASM-013Webex is the only external meeting/collaboration platform in use by the committee; no Teams or Zoom integration is needed.Webex is the only external meeting/collaboration platform in use by the committee; no Teams or Zoom integration is needed.If false: a second meeting-platform adapter must be designed, widening Phase 2 scope.Proposed
ASM-014The AC-029 downstream-link gate (P8d, OQ-045) proxies "≥1 downstream artifact" as **≥1 ActionItem sourced from the decision** (`SourceType=Decision, SourceId`), and **exempts the supersession successoThe AC-029 downstream-link gate (P8d, OQ-045) proxies "≥1 downstream artifact" as **≥1 ActionItem sourced from the decision** (`SourceType=Decision, SourceId`), and **exempts the supersession successor** from the gate. Rationale: the Risk module + typed-edge Traceability (ADR-0008) land in P10, so Actions is the only linkable artifact today; and a supersession successor is drafted+issued atomically with zero links, so gating it would deadlock every correction (the successor corrects a prior that already passed the gate). `SourceId` is trusted input — no cross-module existence check.If false (e.g. a decision must link a Risk with no Action, or supersession must itself re-satisfy the gate): the gate predicate widens when P10 adds Risk/typed edges, and the supersession path would need its own link-carry-forward or a draft-then-issue split. No schema change; the `IActionLinkDirectory` predicate + handler check are the only touch-points.Approved
ASM-015**P10c Traceability backbone assumptions.** (1) *No physical Artifact registry* — relationship edges self-describe via value snapshots `(Type, Id, Key, Title)` on both ends; matches docs/domain/search**P10c Traceability backbone assumptions.** (1) *No physical Artifact registry* — relationship edges self-describe via value snapshots `(Type, Id, Key, Title)` on both ends; matches docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §2.1 SQL and reconciles ADR-0008's "registry" prescription via **ADR-0019** (amends 0008). (2) *AC-029 widened to a **curated** downstream set* — an edge satisfies the gate iff the decision is source of `recorded-as`/`resolves` or target of `implements`; upstream/lineage edges (`decided-by`/`derived-from`/`supersedes`) are excluded so a decision cannot be self-satisfied by its own topic. Superset of the Action-only gate → AC-029 stays Met. (3) *Title is a create-time snapshot* — may go stale on rename (accepted; deep-link fetches live data; keys never go stale). (4) *Owner-AiO edge-create deferred* — v1 create is Chairman/Secretary RBAC only (no ABAC handler for arbitrary artifact types; no UI). (5) *Panel shows explicit edges only* — projection of existing soft-refs (Decision→Topic, Action→source, Risk→subject) into edges is a later slice, and when built should be **read-time contract composition, not write-through** (one source of truth; keeps the upstream `decided-by` edge out of the AC-029 predicate). (6) *Edge endpoint types are NOT validated against the docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §2.2 per-RelType matrix* — the create validator checks `IsInEnum` + source≠target only, not that (e.g.) `RecordedAs` runs Decision→ADR. Trusted-actor input (Chairman/Secretary only, mirrors ASM-014's `SourceId` posture). **Consequence:** a malformed edge (e.g. `Decision --RecordedAs--> Meeting`) can satisfy the AC-029 gate, since `DecisionHasDownstreamEdgeAsync` matches on source-type + RelType without checking the far-endpoint type. Acceptable at ≤20 on-prem users; the full 16-RelType type matrix is deferred.If false: (1) a registry + per-module edge-writes would be needed (ADR-0001 cost); (2) the downstream RelType set is re-curated; (3) a read-time title-resolution seam is added; (4) an artifact-generic ABAC resource handler is built; (5) projection is added as its own slice; (6) the §2.2 per-RelType endpoint-type matrix is added to the validator. All are additive — no rework of the P10c edge schema or the Shared `ITraceabilityLinks` port.Approved
ASM-016**P10d Dependencies backbone assumptions.** (1) *Read-time composition, NO write-through mirror edge (OQ-046)* — a `Dependency` is stored ONCE (Dependencies module); it is NOT mirrored into the Tracea**P10d Dependencies backbone assumptions.** (1) *Read-time composition, NO write-through mirror edge (OQ-046)* — a `Dependency` is stored ONCE (Dependencies module); it is NOT mirrored into the Traceability `Relationship` table. docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §1.34/§1.1 previously implied write-through sync; per-module DbContexts share no transaction, so a mirror would risk dual-write drift for no benefit. The unified graph composes at read-time via the Dependencies module's artifact-scoped query. **Consequence:** the docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §4.1 recursive-CTE impact traversal will NOT see dependency edges — **P10f must UNION the Dependencies table** (or compose in memory). Two-model boundary affirmed (docs/domain/domain-model.md §A.3; ADR-0008/0019). (2) *Kind = `{DependsOn,BlockedBy,Blocks,RelatesTo}`* — the design register's 4 chips; `Impacts` (docs/domain/domain-model.md) dropped as unused, `Blocks` added (FR-094 + design). (3) *`IsCrossStream` not modelled* — `(derived)` per docs/domain/domain-model.md, computed read-time in P10e from real stream *sets* (FR-095 needs a Topics+Membership stream-resolution contract, owned by P10e); P10d stores no flag. (4) *`Note` = plain `string?`* not `LocalizedString` (single free-text Notes box; user annotation ≠ system label; mirrors `Relationship.Notes`). (5) *Endpoint types not validated against a per-Kind matrix* (trusted actor, mirrors ASM-015 pt6). (6) *Member/Reviewer `Dependency.Create` AiO dormant* — the endpoint is resourceless so `CapabilityHandler` grants only Chairman/Secretary; AiO is deferred because **there is no FE caller yet**, NOT because auth cannot express it (a topic-sourced create satisfies the existing `ITopicCapabilityResolver` once P10e supplies `ITopicScopedResource`; only `action→action` is genuinely resolver-blocked). (7) *Resolve/Remove reuse `Policies.DependencyCreate`* (no `Dependency.Manage` policy — same trusted actors, YAGNI).If false: (1) a Dependencies→Traceability write port + outbox/reconciliation would be needed (ADR-0001 cost), or unify the two aggregates (reverses ADR-0008 — rejected, see progress-log P10d); (2) the Kind enum is re-curated; (3) a stream-resolution seam is built earlier; (4) a bilingual Note is modelled; (5) the per-Kind endpoint matrix is added; (6) an `ITopicScopedResource` is wired at the create endpoint (topic-sourced) + an artifact-generic ABAC handler (action-sourced); (7) a `Dependency.Manage` policy is split out. All additive — no rework of the P10d schema.Approved
Dependencies (11 rows) CSV
idtitlestatementownerlifecycle_statusdispositiondisposition_reason_refsource_kindsource_spanlast_referenced
DEP-001**Keycloak instance**: OIDC-compliant; ACMP registered as a client; group/realm-role claims carrying ACMP role names; test credentials for claim-mapping spike.**Keycloak instance**: OIDC-compliant; ACMP registered as a client; group/realm-role claims carrying ACMP role names; test credentials for claim-mapping spike.Approved
DEP-002**SQL Server instance**: Dedicated instance (or named database on a shared instance with exclusive schema) allocated to ACMP; connection string accessible from Docker Compose.**SQL Server instance**: Dedicated instance (or named database on a shared instance with exclusive schema) allocated to ACMP; connection string accessible from Docker Compose.Approved
DEP-003**MinIO provisioning**: S3-compatible endpoint (self-hosted MinIO container in Compose stack); storage volume mounted from host with adequate capacity.**MinIO provisioning**: S3-compatible endpoint (self-hosted MinIO container in Compose stack); storage volume mounted from host with adequate capacity.Approved
DEP-004**Development VM and Docker Compose environment**: A VM accessible to the team with Docker Engine installed; able to pull or cache required base images.**Development VM and Docker Compose environment**: A VM accessible to the team with Docker Engine installed; able to pull or cache required base images.Approved
DEP-005**Self-hosted Seq container**: Seq is included in the ACMP Docker Compose stack; no external Seq license required for development (Seq has a free single-user license; production may require commercial**Self-hosted Seq container**: Seq is included in the ACMP Docker Compose stack; no external Seq license required for development (Seq has a free single-user license; production may require commercial license for team logging).Approved
DEP-006**Tarseem v1.0 render sidecar**: Python ≥3.10 + pip + Chromium + Node/elkjs inside a container; `tarseem doctor` passes; internal HTTP endpoint (FastAPI wrapper) callable from Hangfire job.**Tarseem v1.0 render sidecar**: Python ≥3.10 + pip + Chromium + Node/elkjs inside a container; `tarseem doctor` passes; internal HTTP endpoint (FastAPI wrapper) callable from Hangfire job.Approved
DEP-007**Webex bot/integration account + Webex licensing**: OAuth2 integration account registered; scopes for meetings/recordings/transcripts/messaging granted; Webex Adaptive Cards enabled on the org's Webe**Webex bot/integration account + Webex licensing**: OAuth2 integration account registered; scopes for meetings/recordings/transcripts/messaging granted; Webex Adaptive Cards enabled on the org's Webex deployment; Webex Assistant enabled for transcripts (Control Hub admin decision).Approved
DEP-008**Keystone package schema stability**: Keystone v1.0 manifest schema and artifact types must be stable for the import tool (BL-116) to parse reliably.**Keystone package schema stability**: Keystone v1.0 manifest schema and artifact types must be stable for the import tool (BL-116) to parse reliably.Approved
DEP-009**SMTP relay**: An org SMTP relay or approved cloud mail service (with data-processing agreement if cloud) available to the ACMP container network.**SMTP relay**: An org SMTP relay or approved cloud mail service (with data-processing agreement if cloud) available to the ACMP container network.Approved
DEP-010**LLM endpoint for AI extraction (Phase 3)**: An on-prem or org-approved cloud LLM endpoint reachable from ACMP container; data-residency review completed; endpoint supports transcript chunk inputs.**LLM endpoint for AI extraction (Phase 3)**: An on-prem or org-approved cloud LLM endpoint reachable from ACMP container; data-residency review completed; endpoint supports transcript chunk inputs.Approved
DEP-011**CI/CD system** (GH Actions self-hosted runner / GitLab CI / Azure DevOps — OQ-CI): A CI pipeline that can build Docker images, run tests, and optionally push to a private registry from the developme**CI/CD system** (GH Actions self-hosted runner / GitLab CI / Azure DevOps — OQ-CI): A CI pipeline that can build Docker images, run tests, and optionally push to a private registry from the development VM.Approved
Open questions (58 rows) CSV
idtitlequestionresolutionresolved_bylifecycle_statusdispositiondisposition_reason_refsource_kindsource_spanlast_referenced
OQ-001Should Guest and Presenter be two distinct roles or one role with a `canPresent` attribute?Should Guest and Presenter be two distinct roles or one role with a `canPresent` attribute?Single `Guest/Presenter` role with an `isPresenter` relationship attribute on the invitation; relationship-based ABAC handles the distinctionDeferred
OQ-002Can a user with the Reviewer role cast a vote on a topic they are reviewing?Can a user with the Reviewer role cast a vote on a topic they are reviewing?No — Reviewers annotate but do not vote; eligibility is configured per vote from the Member/Chairman/Secretary poolDeferred
OQ-003What MFA policy and session idle-timeout should Keycloak enforce?What MFA policy and session idle-timeout should Keycloak enforce?MFA required for Chairman + Secretary, optional for others; 60-minute idle timeout; set in ACMP's own Keycloak realm, no code changeDeferred
OQ-004Should Submitter-created topics be the same Topic entity or a separate TopicRequest converted after triage?Should Submitter-created topics be the same Topic entity or a separate TopicRequest converted after triage?Merged — one Topic entity; Submitter creates a Draft, Secretary triages directly; avoids entity duplicationDeferred
OQ-005Are the aging thresholds (Critical 3d, Urgent 7d, Normal 21d) correct?Are the aging thresholds (Critical 3d, Urgent 7d, Normal 21d) correct?Keep Critical 3d / Urgent 7d / Normal 21d until the committee reviews after the PH-1 pilot; thresholds configurable at deployDeferred
OQ-006Should topic `Source` be free-text or an enum, and what values?Should topic `Source` be free-text or an enum, and what values?Enum + Other escape (CommitteeMember, StreamRequest, OperationalIncident, SecurityFinding, RegulatoryRequirement, Innovation, ExternalPartner, Other); enables reporting; Secretary validates list before PH-1Deferred
OQ-007What are the canonical Architecture Invariant categories?What are the canonical Architecture Invariant categories?Security, Performance, Data, Interoperability, Compliance, Other — enum with Other escape; Secretary confirms before the PH-2 buildDeferred
OQ-008Is the committee moving to bi-weekly meetings or staying weekly?Is the committee moving to bi-weekly meetings or staying weekly?Bi-weekly default; cadence is operational config, not hard-coded; SLA thresholds are independent of cadenceDeferred
OQ-009Should multiple users edit the draft MoM simultaneously?Should multiple users edit the draft MoM simultaneously?No — single editor at a time (last-write-wins with a dirty-lock warning); OOS-07 excludes real-time collab; revisit PH-3 if demandedDeferred
OQ-010Are there meeting types beyond regular / extraordinary / emergency?Are there meeting types beyond regular / extraordinary / emergency?Regular, Extraordinary, Emergency as the initial enum with an Other escape; Secretary confirms before the PH-1 buildDeferred
OQ-011Should a Webex Transcript be a first-class artifact type or a blob attachment?Should a Webex Transcript be a first-class artifact type or a blob attachment?First-class artifact (`TRS-YYYY-###`, traceable, independently searchable) for PH-2; a blob makes search and traceability harderDeferred
OQ-012Serve the React SPA via nginx or ASP.NET Core static files?Serve the React SPA via nginx or ASP.NET Core static files?Resolved (2026-06-25): a separate nginx `web` container proxying `/api`→api ships in v1, overriding the earlier ASP.NET-static default (docs/domain/repository-structure.md §8)Approved
OQ-013Which OIDC client library should the React app use?Which OIDC client library should the React app use?`oidc-client-ts` + `react-oidc-context` — active, TypeScript-first, native auth-code + PKCE against Keycloak, not Auth0-brandedDeferred
OQ-014GUID (newsequentialid) or BIGINT IDENTITY as the clustered key for governed entities?GUID (newsequentialid) or BIGINT IDENTITY as the clustered key for governed entities?BIGINT IDENTITY clustered key + GUID alternate unique key for external refs; human-readable `TOP-YYYY-###` remain separate display IDsDeferred
OQ-015Store ballots as a JSON column or a fully normalized table?Store ballots as a JSON column or a fully normalized table?Normalized table (VoteSession, VoteOption, VoteCast rows); per-row immutability and hash-chain integrity are easier to enforce and auditDeferred
OQ-016Should the notification center poll the server or receive WebSocket/SSE push?Should the notification center poll the server or receive WebSocket/SSE push?Short polling at a 30-second interval in PH-1; OOS-15 rejects WebSocket infrastructure; revisit PH-3 if users report stalenessDeferred
OQ-017Is the 8-type relationship enum final, or should admins define custom types?Is the 8-type relationship enum final, or should admins define custom types?Fixed enum of 8 types in v1 (add an Other escape if needed); custom types add UI, migration risk, and semantic dilution; re-evaluate PH-3Deferred
OQ-018What max node cap protects the transitive impact-analysis query?What max node cap protects the transitive impact-analysis query?Depth limit of 3 hops + a 200-node total cap; on overflow, return partial results with a "graph truncated" warningDeferred
OQ-019Should the OpenAPI/Swagger UI be enabled in production?Should the OpenAPI/Swagger UI be enabled in production?Disabled in production (available in dev and staging only per FR-008); auditors use the staging Swagger or a downloaded specDeferred
OQ-020Keep the standby VM warm or cold?Keep the standby VM warm or cold?Warm standby if budget permits, else cold standby with a documented ≤8h restore procedure; Secretary confirms the availability budgetDeferred
OQ-021Include a mobile (phone) breakpoint in PH-1, or tablet + desktop only?Include a mobile (phone) breakpoint in PH-1, or tablet + desktop only?Tablet + desktop only (≥768px) in PH-1; committee use is desk/laptop/tablet; mobile deferred to PH-3 if a need is demonstratedDeferred
OQ-022Which charting library should dashboards use, and is it RTL-validated?Which charting library should dashboards use, and is it RTL-validated?Resolved (2026-07-05, ADR-0022): no chart library — charts are CSS primitives (`rpt-*` renderers); RTL via logical properties + `dir`, verified by live VRApproved
OQ-023Generate export PDFs server-side or browser-side?Generate export PDFs server-side or browser-side?Tarseem for diagram PDF; browser `window.print()` for report PDF in PH-1/PH-2; avoids a server-side PDF library; evaluate QuestPDF in PH-3 if neededDeferred
OQ-024What TLS / cipher / mTLS policy applies to the HTTPS endpoint?What TLS / cipher / mTLS policy applies to the HTTPS endpoint?TLS 1.2+ on the public endpoint (1.3 preferred if all clients support it); no mTLS in v1 for internal Compose-network servicesDeferred
OQ-025Require dual-control or audit + alert for high-sensitivity operations?Require dual-control or audit + alert for high-sensitivity operations?Audit log + anomaly alert in v1; dual-control is too heavy for a ≤20-user system; immutable log + hash chain + Auditor notice suffice; review if classification escalatesDeferred
OQ-026Should ACMP scan uploaded files for malware before storage?Should ACMP scan uploaded files for malware before storage?MIME-type + extension whitelist validation in v1 (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, PNG, JPG, SVG, MP4, MP3, ZIP, JSON); ClamAV Compose sidecar if the org requires scanningDeferred
OQ-027Which SAST/DAST tools run in CI, and do failing scans block merges?Which SAST/DAST tools run in CI, and do failing scans block merges?Semgrep OSS SAST on every PR (block on high severity); OWASP ZAP baseline on staging (alert, not block in v1); dependency scan via `dotnet list package --vulnerable` + `npm audit`Deferred
OQ-028Use a strict nonce/hash CSP or a domain allowlist?Use a strict nonce/hash CSP or a domain allowlist?`script-src 'self'` (no unsafe-inline, no unsafe-eval) for the Vite-built SPA + a report-uri endpoint; switch to nonce only if inline scripts become requiredDeferred
OQ-029Does the org require a formal pen-test sign-off before production deploy?Does the org require a formal pen-test sign-off before production deploy?Internal security review by the designated reviewer before PH-1 go-live; schedule a formal pen-test as a PH-1 gate if org policy requires itDeferred
OQ-030Which CI system runs the automated test + build pipeline?Which CI system runs the automated test + build pipeline?GitHub Actions self-hosted runner if outbound GitHub is allowed, else GitLab CI if the org uses GitLab; decide before PH-0 repo initializationDeferred
OQ-031Push and pull images via a private registry or a public one?Push and pull images via a private registry or a public one?Public registry with digest pinning for dev/staging; a private on-prem mirror is strongly recommended for production; coordinate with org IT in PH-0Deferred
OQ-032Must NuGet and npm packages be mirrored to an internal feed?Must NuGet and npm packages be mirrored to an internal feed?Direct internet access assumed; set up a NuGet + npm mirror in PH-0 if the VM is air-gapped or policy requires it (blocking if not addressed early)Deferred
OQ-033What initial KPI dashboard threshold values apply with no historical baseline?What initial KPI dashboard threshold values apply with no historical baseline?Admin-configurable thresholds; show KPI trend without RAG status until the committee sets thresholds after 90 days of live dataDeferred
OQ-034Is SQL Server's Arabic word-breaker adequate for committee terminology?Is SQL Server's Arabic word-breaker adequate for committee terminology?**Resolved (P15f, 2026-07-15):** spike on the FTS-enabled deploy image (`mssql-server-fts`) → Arabic word-breaker (lcid 1025) present; FREETEXT micro-recall **82.9% ≥ 80%** over 20 committee-term queries (misses Arabic *derivation* which the provider's LIKE booster recovers). **Engine = SQL Server FTS + LIKE booster (single datastore; INV-002 not triggered, no OpenSearch, no ADR).** Evidence: `SearchProvidersFtsTests` (real-stack, AC-061), ph0-validation §7.Implemented
OQ-035Confirm the Urgency SLA thresholds (validation item for FR-038).Confirm the Urgency SLA thresholds (validation item for FR-038).Critical 3d, Urgent 7d, Normal 21d; configurable; adjust post-PH-1 pilot (duplicate of OQ-005)Deferred
OQ-036Confirm the Architecture Invariant category enum (validation item for FR-106).Confirm the Architecture Invariant category enum (validation item for FR-106).Security, Performance, Data, Interoperability, Compliance, Other (duplicate of OQ-007)Deferred
OQ-037Which LLM endpoint powers PH-3 AI extraction, and is data residency confirmed?Which LLM endpoint powers PH-3 AI extraction, and is data residency confirmed?Not determined; the feature is off by default; the org must confirm the LLM provider + data-residency before Admin can activate; no code is blockedDeferred
OQ-038Where does the self-hosted Keycloak keep its own operational store?Where does the self-hosted Keycloak keep its own operational store?Dedicated Postgres-for-Keycloak container (confirm via a PH-0 spike); app data stays SQL-only (ADR-0003); backup/restore must cover the chosen storeDeferred
OQ-039Should ACMP's Keycloak later broker/federate to an organizational IdP?Should ACMP's Keycloak later broker/federate to an organizational IdP?No upstream federation in v1; keep the ACMP realm broker-capable so a future org IdP can be added without reworkDeferred
OQ-040Which SQL Server edition runs the bundled production instance?Which SQL Server edition runs the bundled production instance?Evaluate at deploy phase P18: start with Express but verify columnstore + FTS availability and that the 10GB/DB + memory caps fit ≤20 users; escalate to Standard if limits bindDeferred
OQ-041Does production need a self-hosted CI runner + package/registry mirrors?Does production need a self-hosted CI runner + package/registry mirrors?Provision a self-hosted runner before prod cutover (P18); GitHub-hosted runners stay fine for PH-1 CI; ties to OQ-031 and OQ-032Deferred
OQ-042Should ACMP build any in-app user-invite / provisioning affordance?Should ACMP build any in-app user-invite / provisioning affordance?Resolved (2026-06-27): deep-link to the Keycloak admin console only (`_blank`, `rel="noopener"`), carrying no in-app account-creation form; honors ADR-0015Approved
OQ-043Do mutable aggregate roots carry a RowVersion optimistic-concurrency token?Do mutable aggregate roots carry a RowVersion optimistic-concurrency token?Resolved (2026-06-30): added RowVersion + `IsRowVersion()` config + migrations + `DbUpdateConcurrencyException`→409 mapping + a concurrency test; recorded in ADR-0018Approved
OQ-044Should marking notifications read emit AuditEvents?Should marking notifications read emit AuditEvents?Resolved (2026-07-01): audit the bulk `read-all` sweep only (`Notifications.AllRead`, emitted only when ≥1 item flips); single mark-read stays un-auditedApproved
OQ-045Enforce the AC-029 downstream-link-required-to-issue gate once Actions exist?Enforce the AC-029 downstream-link-required-to-issue gate once Actions exist?Resolved (2026-07-02, P8d): a handler precondition in `IssueDecisionHandler` via the Actions-owned `IActionLinkDirectory`; supersession is auto-exempt; AC-029 → MetApproved
OQ-046Mirror each Dependency as a Relationship edge, or compose at read time?Mirror each Dependency as a Relationship edge, or compose at read time?Resolved (2026-07-03, P10d): read-time composition — store the Dependency once, merge both modules at read time, no mirror edge; recorded via ASM-016 + docs/domain/search-and-traceability.md §1.1Approved
OQ-047How should non-Topic artifacts carry a stream for the impact graph's cross-stream signal?How should non-Topic artifacts carry a stream for the impact graph's cross-stream signal?Default adopted (2026-07-03, P10f): Topic-scope via an `ITopicStreamReader` seam; FR-095 recorded partial; the inherit model stays open pending the multi/zero-topic semantic (ADR-0020)Deferred
OQ-048Does the Invariant aggregate need a `Kind` field?Does the Invariant aggregate need a `Kind` field?Resolved (2026-07-04, P11c): drop Kind — model Category + Scope only per docs/domain/standards-and-best-practices.md §A + FR-106 + the design form; a docs/domain/entity-lifecycles.md §9 correction is owedApproved
OQ-049Should a topic be able to leave `Prepared` (un-prepare / defer-from-Prepared) before it is scheduled?Should a topic be able to leave `Prepared` (un-prepare / defer-from-Prepared) before it is scheduled?Default adopted (2026-07-09, D-15 Tier 3): no un-prepare transition in v1 — `DeferTopic` allows Triage/Accepted/Scheduled/InCommittee but not Prepared, so a mis-prepared topic stays Prepared until it is scheduled (then it can be Deferred). Accept the gap; add a Prepared→Accepted un-prepare only if operators hit stuck-Prepared topics in practiceDeferred
OQ-050Should non-topic-scoped governance artifacts (ADR, Invariant, ResearchMission) support owner-based allow-if-owner (AiO), or is A-only (Chairman/Secretary) the accepted behavior?Should non-topic-scoped governance artifacts (ADR, Invariant, ResearchMission) support owner-based allow-if-owner (AiO), or is A-only (Chairman/Secretary) the accepted behavior?Default (2026-07-13, from P15a): accept **A-only**. The matrix marks `Research.Manage` (#26), ADR (#18) and Invariant (#21) AiO for Member/Reviewer, but the shared `CapabilityHandler` resolves AiO only for an `ITopicScopedResource` (a per-topic relationship). These aggregates are not topic-scoped, so Member/Reviewer are denied create/manage — consistent across ADR/Invariant/Research; `OwnerUserId` is attribution, not authz. Revisit only if the committee needs Member-owned governance authoring; realizing it is a **cross-cutting authz change** (a resource/owner ABAC handler affecting all three), not a per-module fixDeferred
OQ-051`Template.TargetType` value set — FR-119 `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Research Mission}` vs domain-model §403 sketch `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Document, Action}`?`Template.TargetType` value set — FR-119 `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Research Mission}` vs domain-model §403 sketch `{Topic, ADR, MoM, Document, Action}`?Resolved (2026-07-14, P15d-2): adopt **FR-119's set** `{Topic, Adr, MinutesOfMeeting, ResearchMission}` — the functional requirement is the contract (OWASP LLM01), and §403 is an illustrative sketch that even contradicts itself (§404 omitted Action, neither line listed ResearchMission). Document/Action have no FR → YAGNI; the enum serializes as its string name (int-stored, no CHECK) so any value is addable later with **no migration**. domain-model §402-404 reconciled in the same PR; member spelling mirrors Traceability `ArtifactType`Approved
OQ-052Should Draft/Archived wiki pages appear in the tree for all readers, or be filtered by role/status?Should Draft/Archived wiki pages appear in the tree for all readers, or be filtered by role/status?Default (2026-07-14, P15e): the wiki backend returns every status to every reader (reads are committee-wide), but the design draws no distinction. Adopted: tree shows **Published to everyone**, **Draft only to managers** (Chairman/Secretary, dot-marked), **Archived excluded** from the tree. A selected page still opens by direct key regardless. Revisit if members need to see drafts or browse archived pagesDeferred
OQ-053AC-012 presumes the action owner *attempts* to verify and is blocked; the UI instead **prevents** the attempt (hides Verify from owner/completer). Is prevention an acceptable satisfaction of the SoD-1AC-012 presumes the action owner *attempts* to verify and is blocked; the UI instead **prevents** the attempt (hides Verify from owner/completer). Is prevention an acceptable satisfaction of the SoD-1 criterion?Interpretation adopted (2026-07-17, P17b): **yes** — SoD-1 is satisfied by prevention. The owner never sees Verify (proven live, p17b-immutability.spec.ts) and the API 403 is the backstop (ActionsApiTests). AC-012 → Met with this note. Revisit only if an explicit owner-attempt-then-403 UX is requiredDeferred
OQ-054AC-022 presumes a second cast is rejected in-UI with "You have already voted"; the SPA instead routes a re-vote to `/change` (Fork 1, editable-until-close), so that rejection **never occurs in-productAC-022 presumes a second cast is rejected in-UI with "You have already voted"; the SPA instead routes a re-vote to `/change` (Fork 1, editable-until-close), so that rejection **never occurs in-product**. Is the divergence accepted?Recorded as a product↔criterion divergence (2026-07-17, P17b, operator-ruled): the one-ballot invariant holds via the API 409 + DB unique index (VotesApiTests); the in-UI experience is edit-in-place, not rejection (proven live). AC-022 → Met with the divergence foregrounded. **The audit note must NOT claim the UI rejects a second ballot — it does not.** Revisit if a hard in-UI second-cast block is ever requiredDeferred
OQ-055AC-025's tally and chair-action legs have no mutating endpoint (only the ballot leg does). Is immutable-by-absence an acceptable satisfaction for those legs?AC-025's tally and chair-action legs have no mutating endpoint (only the ballot leg does). Is immutable-by-absence an acceptable satisfaction for those legs?Interpretation adopted (2026-07-17, P17b): **yes** — the ballot leg is proven live (POST `/change` on a closed vote → 409, p17b-immutability.spec.ts); the tally + chair-action legs are immutable because no endpoint mutates a closed vote's frozen tally or its counter of record. AC-025 → Met. Revisit only if those legs gain a mutating surfaceDeferred
OQ-056AC-026 (forward-only lifecycle) has no UI or API path to attempt a backward transition, so there is no drivable live leg. Is domain enforcement + absence-of-affordance an acceptable satisfaction?AC-026 (forward-only lifecycle) has no UI or API path to attempt a backward transition, so there is no drivable live leg. Is domain enforcement + absence-of-affordance an acceptable satisfaction?Interpretation adopted (2026-07-17, P17b): **yes** — the forward-only lifecycle is domain-enforced (VoteTests) and no endpoint exposes a regression transition, so the criterion is met by the absence of any backward affordance (immutable-by-absence). AC-026 → Met. Revisit only if a backward transition is ever addedDeferred
OQ-057AC-027 (decision immutability) is satisfied in-UI by the read-only detail exposing no edit surface, rather than by an edit attempt being rejected. Is the no-edit-surface interpretation accepted?AC-027 (decision immutability) is satisfied in-UI by the read-only detail exposing no edit surface, rather than by an edit attempt being rejected. Is the no-edit-surface interpretation accepted?Interpretation adopted (2026-07-17, P17b): **yes** — the decision detail renders as static prose with zero editable fields (proven live, p17b-immutability.spec.ts) and the domain rejects any mutation (DecisionTests); corrections go through Supersede (a new record). AC-027 → Met. Revisit only if in-place decision editing is ever introducedDeferred
OQ-058The design splits decision issuance across two surfaces — the "Record a decision" create form (ACMP Create Flows & Dialogs) and the closed-vote "Record override" control (ACMP Decision, Voting & ADR) The design splits decision issuance across two surfaces — the "Record a decision" create form (ACMP Create Flows & Dialogs) and the closed-vote "Record override" control (ACMP Decision, Voting & ADR) — but the shipped `RecordDecisionDialog` merges record + issue into ONE meeting-workspace dialog, omits the design create-form's Approving authority / Effective date / Affected systems (no backend field — the chair is attributed at issue, `IssuedAt` is server-set), adds Title/Statement/Alternatives (backend-required, present on the read page but abbreviated out of the create form), and folds the separate immutability confirm into an inline warning. Is the reconciliation accepted, or is a two-surface build owed?Reconciliation adopted (2026-07-18, P17b): **one dialog** — in-meeting the committee records + ratifies in one place, so record→issue is a single chairman-gated dialog launched from the agenda item (D-15 show-and-enforce; the SoD-3 403 surfaces inline). Design-only fields dropped rather than inventing backend fields (implement-as-specified, OWASP LLM01); the filled form + "Record & issue" primary is the deliberate immutability confirm. AC-015/016 + the F-03 leg proven live (p17b-decision-issue.spec.ts). A design-doc update to a single record→issue dialog is owed. Revisit only if operators need a standalone Draft-only record form for the secretaryDeferred

DEC-001Deployment model — on-prem VM(s) + Docker Compose; no Kubernetes, service mesh, or cloud PaaSDeployment model — on-prem VM(s) + Docker Compose; no Kubernetes, service mesh, or cloud PaaSRight-sized for an on-prem, low-traffic ≤20-user footprint (CON-003)ApprovedADR-0001
DEC-002Self-contained constraint — ACMP bundles its own background processing, observability, and notification channels; no org Hangfire, ELK, Seq, or notification platformSelf-contained constraint — ACMP bundles its own background processing, observability, and notification channels; no org Hangfire, ELK, Seq, or notification platformZero external runtime dependency in v1 (CON-001)Approved
DEC-003Object storage — self-hosted MinIO (S3-compatible) as an ACMP-owned container; `IFileStore` abstraction in code; metadata in SQL ServerObject storage — self-hosted MinIO (S3-compatible) as an ACMP-owned container; `IFileStore` abstraction in code; metadata in SQL ServerS3-compatible storage without a cloud dependency (DEP-003)ApprovedADR-0003
DEC-004Background jobs — app-owned Hangfire on ACMP's own SQL schema; dashboard for Administrator; SQL outbox for durabilityBackground jobs — app-owned Hangfire on ACMP's own SQL schema; dashboard for Administrator; SQL outbox for durabilityDurable jobs without the org's HangfireApproved
DEC-005Email in v1 — none; the in-app notification center is the sole channel; `INotificationChannel` keeps adding email a zero-code changeEmail in v1 — none; the in-app notification center is the sole channel; `INotificationChannel` keeps adding email a zero-code changeNo confirmed SMTP relay yet (CON-006)ApprovedADR-0005
DEC-006Identity / roles — Keycloak (OIDC, auth-code + PKCE) is the IdP; global roles sourced only from Keycloak claims; no self-registrationIdentity / roles — Keycloak (OIDC, auth-code + PKCE) is the IdP; global roles sourced only from Keycloak claims; no self-registrationACMP stores or assigns no roles (CON-007, CON-009)ApprovedADR-0004
DEC-007Voting attribution — always attributed in v1; every voter's choice is recorded and visible to Auditor and ChairmanVoting attribution — always attributed in v1; every voter's choice is recorded and visible to Auditor and ChairmanNo anonymous voting (CON-011)ApprovedADR-0010
DEC-008Stream visibility — all members read topics across all streams; write, create, and edit are restricted by role and ownershipStream visibility — all members read topics across all streams; write, create, and edit are restricted by role and ownershipRead-broad, write-scoped defaultApproved
DEC-009Single committee — exactly one committee; no multi-committee, tenant discriminator, or sub-committee generalization in v1Single committee — exactly one committee; no multi-committee, tenant discriminator, or sub-committee generalization in v1No multi-tenant need at this scale (CON-010)Approved
DEC-010Webex integration phase — Webex is Phase 2 (notification card adapter + recording/transcript retrieval); no Webex calls in PH-1 codeWebex integration phase — Webex is Phase 2 (notification card adapter + recording/transcript retrieval); no Webex calls in PH-1 codePhased scope (CON-014)ApprovedADR-0005
DEC-011Recordings and transcripts — PH-1 Secretary pastes a link or uploads manually; PH-2 uses the Webex Recordings + Transcripts APIsRecordings and transcripts — PH-1 Secretary pastes a link or uploads manually; PH-2 uses the Webex Recordings + Transcripts APIsManual-first, phased delivery (D-02)Approved
DEC-012AI extraction — Phase 3 only, manual first, always human-reviewed; off by default; Admin activation requires data-residency confirmationAI extraction — Phase 3 only, manual first, always human-reviewed; off by default; Admin activation requires data-residency confirmationNo unreviewed AI content enters the record (RISK-012, CON-015)ApprovedADR-0007
DEC-013Retention policy — keep all records, no auto-purge in v1; retention configurable; votes, decisions, ADRs, and minutes are immutableRetention policy — keep all records, no auto-purge in v1; retention configurable; votes, decisions, ADRs, and minutes are immutableLegal/compliance can set periods later (FR-155)Approved
DEC-014Availability target — 24×7 / 99.9% via standby VM + nightly backups; no HA cluster, no horizontal scalingAvailability target — 24×7 / 99.9% via standby VM + nightly backups; no HA cluster, no horizontal scalingMeets the SLA at ≤20-user scale (ASM-008)Approved
DEC-015Calendar / dates — Gregorian only, no Hijri in v1; localized Gregorian formatting in AR is acceptableCalendar / dates — Gregorian only, no Hijri in v1; localized Gregorian formatting in AR is acceptableSimplicity at v1 scope (CON-012)Approved
DEC-016Keystone integration — optional; the Research module works fully standalone; Keystone import is a Phase 2 enhancement, never a hard dependencyKeystone integration — optional; the Research module works fully standalone; Keystone import is a Phase 2 enhancement, never a hard dependencyNo hard external dependency (CON-013)ApprovedADR-0007
DEC-017Scale ceiling — on-prem, low traffic, ≤20 total users; reject architectural additions whose only justification is scaling beyond 20Scale ceiling — on-prem, low traffic, ≤20 total users; reject architectural additions whose only justification is scaling beyond 20Right-size everything (CON-004)Approved
DEC-018Audit hash chain — SHA-256 chained hash over vote and issued-decision audit recordsAudit hash chain — SHA-256 chained hash over vote and issued-decision audit recordsEnables independent integrity verification by Auditor (BL-067)ApprovedADR-0009
DEC-019Tarseem integration phase — the diagram sidecar is Phase 2; the JSON spec is the version-controlled source of truth; a broken render never blocksTarseem integration phase — the diagram sidecar is Phase 2; the JSON spec is the version-controlled source of truth; a broken render never blocksPhased; spec-as-source-of-truthApprovedADR-0006
DEC-020Primary datastore — Microsoft SQL Server only (transactional + columnstore reporting + FTS); no second database in v1Primary datastore — Microsoft SQL Server only (transactional + columnstore reporting + FTS); no second database in v1One ACMP-owned storeApprovedADR-0003
DEC-021Backend stack — .NET 8 (LTS), ASP.NET Core, REST, Clean Architecture per module, vertical-slice MediatR handlers, EF CoreBackend stack — .NET 8 (LTS), ASP.NET Core, REST, Clean Architecture per module, vertical-slice MediatR handlers, EF CoreSettled stack (CON-002)ApprovedADR-0002
DEC-022Frontend stack — React 18 + TypeScript + Vite; react-i18next (EN/AR); RTL via CSS logical properties + `dir`; light/dark; `@dnd-kit` DnDFrontend stack — React 18 + TypeScript + Vite; react-i18next (EN/AR); RTL via CSS logical properties + `dir`; light/dark; `@dnd-kit` DnDSettled stack (CON-002, CON-008)ApprovedADR-0012
DEC-023Architecture style — modular monolith, single deployable; in-process contracts only; no cross-module DB access; microservices rejected for v1Architecture style — modular monolith, single deployable; in-process contracts only; no cross-module DB access; microservices rejected for v1Right-sized module boundariesApprovedADR-0001
DEC-024Search in v1 — SQL Server Full-Text Search; an app-owned self-hosted search (e.g. OpenSearch) only if FTS outgrows scale, never the org's ELKSearch in v1 — SQL Server Full-Text Search; an app-owned self-hosted search (e.g. OpenSearch) only if FTS outgrows scale, never the org's ELKSelf-contained searchApprovedADR-0011
DEC-025Traceability model — explicit typed relationship model (directed edges over shared Artifact identity); graph traversal in SQLTraceability model — explicit typed relationship model (directed edges over shared Artifact identity); graph traversal in SQLTyped edges give semantic precisionApprovedADR-0008
DEC-026Observability — Serilog + self-hosted Seq container (ACMP-owned) + OpenTelemetry traces/metrics; no dependency on the org's ELK/SeqObservability — Serilog + self-hosted Seq container (ACMP-owned) + OpenTelemetry traces/metrics; no dependency on the org's ELK/SeqSelf-contained observabilityApproved
DEC-027Optimistic concurrency — every mutable aggregate root carries `RowVersion`; a stale write throws `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` → API returns 409Optimistic concurrency — every mutable aggregate root carries `RowVersion`; a stale write throws `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` → API returns 409Chosen over last-writer-wins and pessimistic locking; implemented 2026-06-30 (resolves OQ-043)ApprovedADR-0018
DEC-028**P14 (Tarseem diagram sidecar + Diagrams surface) is deferred indefinitely** — removed from the active build-slice ladder. ACMP ships with **no in-product diagram renderer**; diagrams stay externally**P14 (Tarseem diagram sidecar + Diagrams surface) is deferred indefinitely** — removed from the active build-slice ladder. ACMP ships with **no in-product diagram renderer**; diagrams stay externally authored and attached as files (the D-11 Phase-1 handling, already in place). The `IDiagramRenderer` seam is **not** built**Operator decision 2026-07-17:** P14 is not wanted in the near future. It is safe to drop from the ladder because the capability is **additive** and already has a working manual path (D-11), so nothing else depends on it: PH-2 is otherwise complete and **P17→P19 proceed without it**. Recorded as a `DEC-` and not an ADR because it is a **scheduling** decision, not an architecturally significant one — no settled decision changes (OOS-02 "no in-app diagram engine" stands; the unbuilt `IDiagramRenderer` seam is untouched). **Schedules `DEC-019`** (Tarseem = Phase 2) without superseding it: the sidecar shape, spec-as-source-of-truth and never-blocks clauses all stand, so DEC-019 is *annotated*, not reopened — the same treatment DEC-016 got for the D-05 / P15 Keystone-import deferralApprovedADR-0006
DEC-029Model the cross-cutting slices P16–P19 as their own phase PH-4Create a fifth phase, PH-4 "Cross-cutting hardening & release", and assign slices SL-016 through SL-019 (P16 security hardening, P17 testing hardening, P18 deployment, P19 final audit and release readiness) to it. Operator decision of 2026-07-22, re-applied on the 2.4.0 re-population of 2026-07-23.The roadmap classifies P16–P19 as "cross-cutting" and gives them no phase home, but the schema declares slices.phase_id NOT NULL, so every slice must reference a phase. The operator chose explicit modelling so the cross-cutting nature stays visible rather than being silently absorbed into PH-2. RECONCILIATION, recorded here so it is never read as drift: the roadmap classifies P16–P19 as cross-cutting under no phase; this package models them as PH-4 solely to satisfy the NOT NULL foreign key; the two records are consistent, not conflicting. A reader comparing the phase count against the roadmap's 4-row phase table is seeing this decision, not damage.Approvedinferredoperator decision 2026-07-22; see SC-001
Adrs (33 rows) CSV
idtitlecontextdecisionconsequenceslifecycle_statusdispositiondisposition_reason_refsuperseded_bysource_kindsource_spanlast_referenced
ADR-0001ADR-0001: Modular Monolith as Macro-ArchitectureACMP is a focused, auditable, bilingual governance tool for a single Architecture Committee. Scale is on-prem, ≤20 total users, low traffic, single delivery team. A macro-architecture decision is required before module boundaries, inter-module communication rules, and deployment can be specified. The wrong choice here drives every downstream structural decision.Chosen option: "Modular monolith", because the scale (≤20 users, single team, on-prem VM) makes microservices operational overhead unjustifiable, and a layered monolith provides no enforced isolation and would couple unrelated domains (audit, voting, search) in ways that invite future pain. Modules map one-to-one with bounded contexts; they communicate only via in-process public contracts (interfaces / MediatR notifications) and never read each other's tables directly. ### Consequences - Good: minimal operational overhead; single deployment unit (Docker Compose); one EF Core migration set; full-stack debugging without distributed tracing between services; well-understood pattern in .NET; can extract a module to a service later if usage changes. - Bad / trade-off: no network-level isolation between modules (a bug in one module can crash all); if scale increases dramatically (>20×), decomposition requires non-trivial refactoring. Discipline is required to enforce module boundaries — violations are not caught by the compiler without a custom Roslyn analyzer or ArchUnit-equivalent.Approved
ADR-0002ADR-0002: .NET 8 + ASP.NET Core + Clean Architecture + Vertical Slice + EF Core + RESTGiven the modular-monolith decision (ADR-0001), the backend technology stack and intra-module structural pattern must be chosen. The platform handles sensitive governance data (votes, decisions, audit) and must be maintainable long-term by a single .NET team within an organization whose broader tech estate is .NET.Chosen option: ".NET 8 + ASP.NET Core + Clean Architecture per module + vertical-slice (MediatR) + EF Core + REST", because it aligns with org expertise, is LTS-stable, and provides layered testability (domain logic isolated from EF Core and HTTP) while keeping each use case self-contained. MediatR pipeline behaviors handle cross-cutting concerns (validation, logging, authorization, outbox) uniformly without repeating them in controllers. ### Consequences - Good: domain and application layers are fully unit-testable without a running database; EF Core migrations are automated and reviewable; MediatR pipeline behaviors enforce consistent cross-cutting logic; REST is universally understood by API consumers; LTS lifecycle gives multi-year support stability. - Bad / trade-off: MediatR adds an indirection layer that beginners find opaque; Clean Architecture enforces strict layer boundaries which produce more files per feature than a simple CRUD approach; EF Core's change tracker can be a footgun if developers mix tracked/untracked entities carelessly — requires code-review attention.Approved
ADR-0003ADR-0003: Microsoft SQL Server as the Single DatastoreACMP needs a datastore for transactional data (topics, votes, decisions, actions, audit), search (full-text across topics/docs/transcripts), reporting/dashboard analytics, and traceability graph traversal. The question is whether one datastore is sufficient or whether a second (search engine, analytics DB, graph DB) is needed from the start.Chosen option: "SQL Server only", because at ≤20 users and low traffic, SQL Server's FTS, columnstore indexes, native JSON support, and recursive CTE graph traversal are demonstrably sufficient without a second datastore. Adding OpenSearch or any other engine introduces a consistency boundary (dual-write or CDC), an additional container, and additional backup complexity — costs that are not justified by any measured requirement. If FTS is outgrown (evidence: unacceptably slow queries or search quality complaints at scale), the platform will stand up its own self-hosted OpenSearch container (app-owned, never the org's ELK cluster). [Basis: §5.6 — airbyte/influxdata/medium comparisons; "From ElasticSearch back to SQL Server" (mauridb)] ### Consequences - Good: one backup/restore target; one migration pipeline; one monitoring obligation; no dual-write complexity; simpler disaster-recovery; native JSON column support handles Tarseem spec storage and flexible metadata. - Bad / trade-off: SQL Server FTS is weaker than Elasticsearch on fuzzy matching, typo tolerance, and autocomplete. Arabic word-breaking quality must be validated (see Validation). If search requirements grow, migration to a hybrid approach (SQL + self-hosted OpenSearch) requires adding a dual-write outbox or CDC — this is non-trivial to retrofit.Approved
ADR-0004ADR-0004: Keycloak (OIDC) as Identity ProviderACMP is a high-sensitivity governance tool. It needs SSO (committee members must not manage a separate ACMP password), role-based authorization (Chairman, Secretary, Member, Reviewer, Auditor, Administrator, Submitter, Guest/Presenter), and controlled onboarding (no self-registration). The organization is mid-migration from an internal auth service to Keycloak.Chosen option: "Keycloak OIDC (authorization-code + PKCE)", because the organization mandates SSO via Keycloak, ACMP must not own an IdP, and Keycloak's group/realm-role claim mechanism cleanly maps to the ACMP canonical role set without building a custom authorization server. PKCE ensures the authorization-code flow is secure for SPA/BFF usage. ### Consequences - Good: no password management in ACMP; SSO for committee members; Keycloak's MFA, session management, and account lifecycle are inherited; roles are centrally managed by Keycloak admins; ACMP token validation is a standard ASP.NET Core JWT middleware configuration. - Bad / trade-off: ACMP has a runtime dependency on the Keycloak server being available (if Keycloak is down, ACMP login fails). Mitigate: token validation is local (JWT signature + expiry check); existing sessions continue until token expiry. Role changes in Keycloak take effect at next token refresh — there is a window of up to token TTL where stale roles are honoured. Keycloak group/role schema changes must be coordinated with ACMP role-mapping configuration.Approved
ADR-0005ADR-0005: Notification Channel Abstraction (INotificationChannel)ACMP needs to notify committee members of meeting reminders, action due-dates, vote openings, and status changes. The notification landscape is uncertain: no SMTP relay is available in v1, the organization has a centralized notification platform (Email/SMS/Firebase) that ACMP must not depend on (CON-001), and Webex integration is desirable but complex (see §5.3 — rate limits, Webex Assistant requirement for transcripts, Adaptive Cards). A design that hard-codes any single channel creates coupling that will be expensive to change.Chosen option: "`INotificationChannel` abstraction with in-app notification center as the v1 channel", because it eliminates external runtime dependencies in v1, meets the delivery deadline, and allows Webex and future channels to be added as pluggable adapters in Phase 2 without touching notification business logic. The in-app center provides a persistent, auditable notification history that Webex push alone would not. ### Consequences - Good: v1 has zero external notification dependencies; notification logic is testable with a mock channel; in-app center doubles as a notification history/audit trail; adding Webex in Phase 2 is a new `INotificationChannel` implementation + Hangfire job, not a refactor; abstraction prevents org-platform coupling. - Bad / trade-off: committee members who expect push notifications (email, Webex) in v1 will find the in-app center less immediate — this is a known gap and a product decision (Phase 2 addresses it). The in-app center requires the user to open ACMP to see notifications; there is no out-of-band delivery in v1.Approved
ADR-0006ADR-0006: Tarseem as Containerized Render Sidecar (JSON Spec = Source of Truth)ACMP topics, ADRs, and architectural decisions require architecture diagrams (C4, flowchart, sequence, deployment, ER, etc.) as first-class artifacts, not just file attachments. The question is whether to build a diagram engine, adopt a third-party SaaS (Lucidchart, draw.io cloud), or integrate an existing engine already in the organization's orbit.Chosen option: "Tarseem as containerized render sidecar; JSON spec as the version-controlled source of truth", because Tarseem is the only available engine covering the required diagram families with Arabic/RTL support, a machine-readable agent surface, and a deterministic spec-hash-traceable output. The sidecar pattern keeps Tarseem's Python + Chromium dependency isolated from the .NET application container. The JSON spec stored in SQL is the canonical artifact; generated SVG/PNG/PDF/draw.io/PPTX are derived outputs attached via the relationship model. ### Consequences - Good: no diagram engine to build; 11 diagram families available on day one of Phase 2; Arabic/RTL diagrams work without workarounds; spec-diff enables diagram history and change review; generated artifacts carry spec hash for traceability; sidecar isolation means Tarseem failures do not crash the .NET app; `tarseem doctor` provides a health-check endpoint. - Bad / trade-off: adds a Python + Chromium container to the Docker Compose stack (image size, startup time, Chromium memory for raster); Tarseem is v1.0.0 (released 2026-06-17 [unverified for latest patch]) — adopting at v1.0 means being an early adopter; schema version lock at v1.0 means migration tooling (`tarseem migrate`) must be used if schema evolves. ACMP must implement a thin internal HTTP wrapper (not a public API) around `tarseem.generate()`.Approved
ADR-0007ADR-0007: Keystone as Optional Companion (Not Embedded, Not a Hard Dependency)Keystone (`github.com/A-H-911/keystone`) is a Claude Code plugin that turns a project description into an execution-ready planning and handoff package (research missions, findings, recommendations, requirements, risks, decisions, traceability matrix). The ACMP domain includes a Research module for `ResearchDiscovery` topics. The question is how tightly Keystone integrates with ACMP: embedded service, hard dependency, or optional enhancement.Chosen option: "Keystone OPTIONAL — Research module fully standalone; import Keystone outputs; adopt ID scheme", because Keystone is a facilitated authoring workflow, not a runtime service, and the Research module must be independently usable. When a team runs a Keystone package for a `ResearchDiscovery` topic, ACMP imports the structured manifest (requirements, decisions, risks, acceptance criteria, traceability matrix) as first-class domain artifacts (Research Mission → Finding → Recommendation entities) and stores a reference/link to the Keystone package. ACMP adopts Keystone's identifier scheme and quality-gate philosophy platform-wide. ### Consequences - Good: Research module launches on day one without any Keystone dependency; Keystone becomes a value-add for teams doing rigorous research discovery; imported artifacts are first-class (queryable, traceable, linked to topics); identifier scheme consistency across the platform and any Keystone packages produced; no Anthropic/Claude Code runtime coupling. - Bad / trade-off: import mapping (Keystone output schema → ACMP domain entities) must be defined and maintained; if Keystone's output schema changes between versions, the import adapter needs updating. Teams using Keystone plus ACMP need a clear documented workflow (run Keystone → export manifest → import into ACMP topic) — this is a process gap, not a technical one.Approved
ADR-0008ADR-0008: Typed Directed Relationship Edges as the Traceability ModelACMP must support end-to-end traceability across its domain entities: Topics link to Meetings, Decisions, Actions, ADRs, Risks, Dependencies, Diagrams, Documents, Research Missions, and Architecture Invariants. Impact analysis ("what is affected if this system changes?") requires traversing these links. The design question is whether to use a shared identity scheme over a flat artifact table, a dedicated graph database, or explicit typed relationship edges in SQL.Chosen option: "Typed directed `Relationship` edges over a shared `Artifact` identity table, traversed via recursive CTEs in SQL Server", because it preserves relationship semantics (governance requires knowing *how* entities are related, not just *that* they are), scales to the entity count without combinatorial table explosion, supports impact analysis via SQL traversal, and avoids a second datastore. The `Artifact` table acts as a polymorphic identity registry — each domain entity registers an artifact record on creation; the `Relationship` table links artifact IDs with a typed edge. ### Consequences - Good: uniform traversal API across all entity types; relationship types are queryable and reportable; new entity types join the traceability graph by registering in `Artifact` (no schema change to `Relationship`); bidirectional traversal via `SourceArtifactId` / `TargetArtifactId` index; SQL Server graph tables or recursive CTEs support impact analysis without a graph DB. - Bad / trade-off: polymorphic identity adds a level of indirection (every entity write must also write an `Artifact` row — enforced at the repository layer); cyclic relationship detection must be implemented at application level (SQL recursive CTEs can cycle); relationship type set must be curated and versioned (a proliferating type vocabulary becomes a governance problem in itself).Approved
ADR-0009ADR-0009: Append-Only Audit Log with Immutability and Hash-Chain IntegrityACMP is the system of record for architectural governance: votes, decisions, ADRs, and published minutes have legal and regulatory weight. A governance platform that allows silent editing of these records is unfit for purpose. An audit strategy must be chosen that supports accountability, provides tamper evidence, and satisfies records-management requirements — while remaining operable without cryptographic PKI infrastructure on an on-prem VM.Chosen option: "Append-only audit log + immutability enforcement at the application layer + hash-chain on the highest-sensitivity record categories (votes, decisions, audit log itself)", because it provides tamper evidence within the existing SQL Server infrastructure without external dependencies, and the immutability rule is enforced both at the application layer (no UPDATE/DELETE paths on immutable entities) and by DB constraints (no `UPDATE` privilege on the audit table for the app DB user). ### Consequences - Good: any post-hoc modification of a vote or decision is detectable via hash-chain verification; the audit log provides a complete reconstruction timeline; immutability is enforced at multiple layers (application + DB permissions); no external dependency; hash-chain verification can be run as a scheduled integrity check. - Bad / trade-off: immutable records require a "supersede" workflow rather than "edit" for corrections (e.g., correcting a vote that was cast in error requires a chairman-authorized override event, not an edit) — this adds UX complexity but is the correct governance behaviour. The hash-chain covers tamper detection by insiders with application access; a DBA with direct SQL access and truncate rights could still destroy the chain — mitigate by restricting DB permissions and enabling SQL Server audit at the server level.Approved
ADR-0010ADR-0010: Always-Attributed Voting ModelThe committee makes decisions via voting followed by chairman final approval. The voting model must specify: who may vote (eligible voters), what constitutes a quorum, how abstentions are handled, whether votes are attributed to individuals or anonymous, and how the chairman's authority interacts with the vote outcome. Getting this wrong produces either accountability gaps (anonymous votes in a governance body) or a model too complex to implement correctly.Chosen option: "Always attributed voting — eligible voters, quorum, abstentions, chairman approval/override by name", because accountability is the primary governance requirement: architectural decisions must be traceable to the individuals who made them, and the chairman's authority must be recorded explicitly (not inferred). In v1 there is no anonymity and no ranked-choice — these can be revisited with evidence of a requirement. ### Consequences - Good: full attribution means the audit log records who voted which way; the chairman's approval or override is a named, timestamped, immutable event; quorum enforcement prevents invalid decisions; abstentions are treated as a deliberate position (not just absence); conflict-of-interest recusals are expressible. - Bad / trade-off: some committee members may feel attribution inhibits honest voting (the "reluctant dissenter" problem). This is a governance culture decision, not a technical one — the brief mandates attribution, and it is the correct stance for an architecture committee. If anonymous voting is ever required (e.g., for sensitive personnel matters), it must be introduced as a separate vote type with explicit scope, not as a default.Approved
ADR-0011ADR-0011: SQL Server Full-Text Search in v1; Self-Hosted OpenSearch if OutgrownACMP needs full-text search across topics (title, description, scope), documents (meeting minutes, ADRs, architecture invariants), and (in later phases) meeting transcripts. Search quality requirements include English and Arabic text matching. The decision is which search technology to adopt for v1 and what the upgrade path looks like.Chosen option: "SQL Server FTS for v1; escalate to self-hosted OpenSearch only with measured evidence of SQL Server being insufficient", because SQL Server FTS avoids a second datastore in v1 without sacrificing acceptable search quality at this scale. If search is outgrown (evidenced by slow queries, poor Arabic recall, or user complaints at meaningful scale), the platform adds an app-owned OpenSearch container and a dual-write outbox — never the org's ELK. [Basis: §5.6 of `.context/brief-digest.md`] ### Consequences - Good: no second datastore in v1; no dual-write complexity; one backup target; SQL Server already running for transactional data; FTS index maintenance is automatic via SQL Server. - Bad / trade-off: Arabic FTS quality is a known risk (SQL Server Arabic word-breaker quality is [unverified] versus a tuned OpenSearch Arabic analyzer); fuzzy/typo-tolerant search is not available in v1; autocomplete requires a workaround (prefix queries or a dedicated suggestions table); if the FTS-to-OpenSearch migration is deferred too long, the dual-write retrofit may be more painful.Approved
ADR-0012ADR-0012: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite FrontendACMP requires a web frontend that is bilingual (EN/AR), supports RTL layout, is accessible, and provides rich interactions (drag-and-drop backlog/agenda prioritization, kanban views, real-time notification updates). The technology stack must be compatible with the .NET/REST backend (ADR-0002), supportable by the existing team, and deployable as a static asset bundle served by the ACMP server or a separate nginx container.Chosen option: "React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + react-i18next + RTL logical CSS + `@dnd-kit`", because it is the most productive and well-supported stack for the requirements, the RTL approach (CSS logical properties + `dir` attribute) is framework-agnostic and correct for a bilingual app, and `@dnd-kit` provides the accessible DnD needed for keyboard users without the maintenance risk of unmaintained libraries. ### Consequences - Good: fast development iteration with Vite HMR; strong TypeScript ecosystem; `react-i18next` with `dir="rtl"` on the root element and CSS logical properties handles EN/AR layout flip without per-component hacks; `@dnd-kit` keyboard-accessibility satisfies WCAG 2.2 AA DnD requirements; React 18 concurrent features allow prioritised rendering (notifications update without blocking the UI); light/dark tokens via CSS custom properties are trivial to implement. - Bad / trade-off: bundle size must be monitored (React + Vite is smaller than Angular but larger than a vanilla approach — acceptable for an internal tool); TypeScript type discipline requires upfront investment in shared type definitions (API response DTOs); RTL testing requires explicit test runs with `dir="rtl"` — easy to miss RTL layout bugs without a dedicated RTL test pass.Approved
ADR-0013ADR-0013: Self-Contained On-Premises Deployment (CON-001)The organization operates a shared runtime infrastructure: Hangfire (background jobs), ELK+Seq (observability), centralized notification platform (Email/SMS/Firebase), shared databases. ACMP is a high-sensitivity governance tool — its availability, data integrity, and audit trail must not depend on the operational state or upgrade schedule of shared infrastructure. The deployment model must be specified explicitly to avoid accidental coupling.Chosen option: "On-prem VM + Docker Compose; all ACMP infrastructure bundled as its own containers", because CON-001 is a hard constraint, Docker Compose is the correct orchestration tier for this scale, and bundling every runtime dependency (**including SQL Server and Keycloak, per ADR-0015**) in the Docker Compose stack makes the platform self-contained, reproducible, and operationally simple. ### Consequences - Good: ACMP is deployable on any VM that can run Docker without org infrastructure dependencies; upgrades of shared org infrastructure do not affect ACMP; the deployment is fully reproducible from the `docker-compose.yml` + env files; availability target is achievable with Docker Compose restart policies and VM-level redundancy; no K8s operational expertise needed. - Bad / trade-off: *(Amended by ADR-0015 — SQL Server and Keycloak are now **bundled** in the Compose stack, not external.)* ACMP has a startup dependency on both; Compose `depends_on` + health checks order startup. If Keycloak is down, ACMP login fails (mitigated: JWT validation is local; existing sessions continue). If SQL Server is down, ACMP is fully unavailable — addressed by ACMP's own nightly backup + warm-standby (now ACMP's operational responsibility, since both are bundled).Approved
