WBS-1.2WBS-1.2 EF Core project setup: DbContext, migrations infrastructure, apply-on-startup / CLI, rollback doc. Realizes FR-001, FR-003–FR-015. (BL-002 / EPIC-01).WBS-1Approved
WBS-1.3WBS-1.3 Configuration management: secrets via env vars; no secrets in image; IOptions<> binding per module. Realizes FR-001, FR-003–FR-015. (BL-003 / EPIC-01).WBS-1Approved

WBS-19.4WBS-19.4 Wiki page entity + migration: DOC-…; Markdown content, category, versioned. Realizes FR-111–FR-120. (BL-118 / EPIC-19).WBS-19Approved
WBS-19.5WBS-19.5 Wiki editor UI: Markdown editor with preview; version history; diff view; add to FTS index. Realizes FR-111–FR-120. (BL-119 / EPIC-19).WBS-19Approved
WBS-19.6WBS-19.6 Template entity + migration: TPL-…; Markdown template with placeholder fields; artifact type tag. Realizes FR-111–FR-120 (incl. FR-041 topic templates). (BL-120 / EPIC-19).WBS-19Approved
WBS-19.7WBS-19.7 Template selection at artifact creation: pre-fills description/content field; editable. Realizes FR-111–FR-120 (incl. FR-041 topic templates). (BL-121 / EPIC-19).WBS-19Approved
WBS-2Identity & Access ManagementPH-1LApproved
WBS-2.1WBS-2.1 OIDC authorization-code + PKCE flow: Keycloak integration; token validation middleware; silent renew; logout. Realizes FR-001–FR-002, FR-016, FR-018, FR-022, FR-024. (BL-017 / EPIC-02).WBS-2Approved
WBS-2.2WBS-2.2 Auth service adapter: config-driven switch between Keycloak and legacy org auth. Realizes FR-001–FR-002, FR-016, FR-018, FR-022, FR-024. (BL-018 / EPIC-02).WBS-2Approved
WBS-2.3WBS-2.3 Keycloak claim parser: group/realm-role claims → ACMP canonical roles. Realizes FR-001–FR-002, FR-016, FR-018, FR-022, FR-024. (BL-019 / EPIC-02).WBS-2Approved
WBS-2.4WBS-2.4 ASP.NET Core policy-based authorization: role policies per endpoint; 403 on unauthorized. Realizes FR-001–FR-002, FR-016, FR-018, FR-022, FR-024. (BL-020 / EPIC-02).WBS-2Approved
WBS-2.5WBS-2.5 Per-topic ABAC capability resolver: Owner/Assignee/Presenter relationship checks. Realizes FR-001–FR-002, FR-016, FR-018, FR-022, FR-024. (BL-021 / EPIC-02).WBS-2Approved
WBS-2.6WBS-2.6 Role-aware navigation: frontend hides unauthorized nav items and action buttons. Realizes FR-001–FR-002, FR-016, FR-018, FR-022, FR-024. (BL-027 / EPIC-02).WBS-2Approved
WBS-20AI & Advanced AnalyticsPH-3MApproved
WBS-20.1WBS-20.1 Transcript FTS index: SQL Server FTS on transcript content; search restricted to Chairman/Secretary/Auditor. Realizes FR-059–FR-060. (BL-201 / EPIC-20).WBS-20Approved
WBS-20.2WBS-20.2 AI candidate extraction: Secretary-triggered; configurable LLM endpoint; draft candidates; approval gate; off by default. Realizes FR-059–FR-060. (BL-202 / EPIC-20).WBS-20Approved
WBS-20.3WBS-20.3 AI extraction feature flag + privacy gate: Admin activation only; data-residency confirmation stored; activation log. Realizes FR-059–FR-060. (BL-203 / EPIC-20).WBS-20Approved
WBS-20.4WBS-20.4 Email adapter: INotificationChannel via SMTP; zero change to dispatch call site; activated by config. Realizes FR-130 (email items). (BL-204 / EPIC-20).WBS-20Approved
WBS-20.5WBS-20.5 KPI/health dashboard: avg topic-to-decision days, action SLA %, backlog age distribution, vote-to-ratification time; configurable thresholds. Realizes FR-141 (PH-3). (BL-205 / EPIC-20).WBS-20Approved
WBS-3Membership & User ManagementPH-1MApproved
WBS-3.1WBS-3.1 User entity + migration: name, email, streams, deactivated flag; seeded on first-login from OIDC claims. Realizes FR-016–FR-021. (BL-022 / EPIC-03).WBS-3Approved
WBS-3.2WBS-3.2 Administrator: create user (name, email, stream assignment); no self-registration endpoint. Realizes FR-016–FR-021. (BL-023 / EPIC-03).WBS-3Approved
WBS-3.3WBS-3.3 Stream entity + migration; stream assignment multi-select; stream filter context propagated. Realizes FR-016–FR-021. (BL-024 / EPIC-03).WBS-3Approved
WBS-3.4WBS-3.4 User deactivation: blocks login; historical records intact and attributed. Realizes FR-016–FR-021. (BL-025 / EPIC-03).WBS-3Approved
WBS-3.5WBS-3.5 Member directory UI: paginated active users with name, role, streams, email. Realizes FR-016–FR-021. (BL-026 / EPIC-03).WBS-3Approved
WBS-3.6WBS-3.6 Guest/Presenter time-boxed invitation: view-only scoped to meeting/topic; auto-expire. Realizes FR-023 (PH-2). (BL-126 / EPIC-03).WBS-3Approved
WBS-4Topic Intake & LifecyclePH-1XLApproved
WBS-4.1WBS-4.1 Topic entity + migration: all fields; TOP-YYYY-### ID generation. Realizes FR-025–FR-029, FR-038–FR-040, FR-042–FR-044. (BL-028 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-4.10WBS-4.10 Topic conversion workflow: convert to/from ADR/Research Mission; typed link; Converted status. Realizes FR-030 (PH-2). (BL-136 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-4.11WBS-4.11 Topic Reopen: Secretary reopens Closed/Rejected topic with recorded reason; re-enters triage. Realizes FR-045 (PH-2). (BL-137 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-4.12WBS-4.12 Bulk topic operations: Secretary defers/reassigns multiple topics at once. Realizes D-10 (bulk topic operations — deferred item, no dedicated FR; PH-3). (BL-208 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Deferred
WBS-4.2WBS-4.2 Topic submission API + UI: required-field validation (EN+AR); attachment upload via IFileStore. Realizes FR-025–FR-029, FR-038–FR-040, FR-042–FR-044. (BL-029 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-4.3WBS-4.3 Topic status state machine: transitions enforced; immutable rejection/deferral event recording. Realizes FR-025–FR-029, FR-038–FR-040, FR-042–FR-044. (BL-030 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-4.4WBS-4.4 Topic triage workflow: Secretary accept/reject (mandatory rationale)/defer (mandatory reason + date). Realizes FR-025–FR-029, FR-038–FR-040, FR-042–FR-044. (BL-031 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-4.5WBS-4.5 Topic edit lock: Owner/Secretary in Draft/Submitted/Triage; post-Accepted metadata-only for Secretary. Realizes FR-025–FR-029, FR-038–FR-040, FR-042–FR-044. (BL-032 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-4.6WBS-4.6 Topic comment thread: timestamped, attributed, immutable after post. Realizes FR-025–FR-029, FR-038–FR-040, FR-042–FR-044. (BL-033 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-4.7WBS-4.7 Topic detail page: all fields, status history with actors/timestamps, linked artifacts, traceability excerpt. Realizes FR-025–FR-029, FR-038–FR-040, FR-042–FR-044. (BL-034 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-4.8WBS-4.8 Aging indicator: background Hangfire SLA evaluation (thresholds — Critical: 3d, Urgent: 7d, Normal: 21d [unverified — OQ-SLA-URGENCY]); visual badge + Secretary notification. Realizes FR-025–FWBS-4Approved
WBS-4.9WBS-4.9 Prepared + Scheduled status transitions: Secretary marks Prepared; schedules to a meeting. Realizes FR-025–FR-029, FR-038–FR-040, FR-042–FR-044. (BL-036 / EPIC-04).WBS-4Approved
WBS-5Backlog ManagementPH-1LApproved
WBS-5.1WBS-5.1 Backlog list view: badges, status, owner, streams, dates; server-side sort/filter. Realizes FR-031–FR-035, FR-037–FR-038. (BL-037 / EPIC-05).WBS-5Approved
WBS-5.2WBS-5.2 Backlog table/dense view: all fields as columns; user-configurable column visibility/order. Realizes FR-031–FR-035, FR-037–FR-038. (BL-038 / EPIC-05).WBS-5Approved
WBS-5.3WBS-5.3 Backlog kanban view: status columns; @dnd-kit DnD within/between columns with permission check. Realizes FR-031–FR-035, FR-037–FR-038. (BL-039 / EPIC-05).WBS-5Approved
WBS-5.4WBS-5.4 Backlog calendar view: topics by scheduled meeting date; monthly + weekly layout. Realizes FR-031–FR-035, FR-037–FR-038. (BL-040 / EPIC-05).WBS-5Approved
WBS-5.5WBS-5.5 DnD reprioritization: explicit ordinal stored; keyboard-accessible move-up/down alternative (WCAG 2.2 AA). Realizes FR-031–FR-035, FR-037–FR-038. (BL-041 / EPIC-05).WBS-5Approved
WBS-5.6WBS-5.6 Aging indicators on all backlog views: badge, color, tooltip with SLA deadline. Realizes FR-031–FR-035, FR-037–FR-038. (BL-042 / EPIC-05).WBS-5Approved
WBS-5.7WBS-5.7 Timeline/Gantt-lite view: topic date bars; pan/zoom. Realizes FR-036 (PH-2). (BL-128 / EPIC-05).WBS-5Deferred
WBS-6Agenda & Meeting ManagementPH-1LApproved
WBS-6.1WBS-6.1 Meeting record entity + migration: MTG-YYYY-###; date, time, type, mode, agenda reference. Realizes FR-046–FR-052, FR-056, FR-061. (BL-043 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Approved
WBS-6.10WBS-6.10 Webex recording metadata retrieval: Hangfire post-meeting; store title/duration/downloadUrl. Realizes FR-057 (PH-2). (BL-113 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Deferred
WBS-6.11WBS-6.11 Webex transcript retrieval: Transcripts API + snippets; stored linked to meeting; access-restricted. Realizes FR-058 (PH-2). (BL-114 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Deferred
WBS-6.2WBS-6.2 Agenda entity + migration: AGN-YYYY-###; ordered items with time-box, presenter assignment. Realizes FR-046–FR-052, FR-056, FR-061. (BL-044 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Approved
WBS-6.3WBS-6.3 Agenda creation UI: select Scheduled topics; DnD + keyboard alternative; time-box; presenter assign. Realizes FR-046–FR-052, FR-056, FR-061. (BL-045 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Approved
WBS-6.4WBS-6.4 Carry-over auto-suggest: unresolved items from previous meeting surfaced on new agenda. Realizes FR-046–FR-052, FR-056, FR-061. (BL-046 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Approved
WBS-6.5WBS-6.5 Agenda publish: API + Hangfire notification dispatch; in-app notification to all members with deep link. Realizes FR-046–FR-052, FR-056, FR-061. (BL-047 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Approved
WBS-6.6WBS-6.6 Attendance tracking: Secretary marks Present/Absent/Remote per member; quorum context. Realizes FR-046–FR-052, FR-056, FR-061. (BL-048 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Approved
WBS-6.7WBS-6.7 Live meeting notes: free-text Markdown per agenda item; auto-save debounce ≤2s. Realizes FR-046–FR-052, FR-056, FR-061. (BL-049 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Approved
WBS-6.8WBS-6.8 Manual recording attachment: Secretary pastes link or uploads file to meeting record. Realizes FR-046–FR-052, FR-056, FR-061. (BL-050 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Approved
WBS-6.9WBS-6.9 Meeting calendar view: past + upcoming meetings with status badges. Realizes FR-046–FR-052, FR-056, FR-061. (BL-051 / EPIC-06).WBS-6Approved
WBS-7Minutes of Meeting (MoM)PH-1MApproved
WBS-7.1WBS-7.1 MoM entity + migration: MIN-YYYY-###; versioned content; approval state. Realizes FR-053–FR-055. (BL-053 / EPIC-07).WBS-7Approved
WBS-7.2WBS-7.2 MoM generation: compile attendance + agenda items + notes + decisions + actions into structured Markdown. Realizes FR-053–FR-055. (BL-054 / EPIC-07).WBS-7Approved
WBS-7.3WBS-7.3 MoM versioning + approval workflow: Secretary drafts → Reviewer/Chairman annotate → Chairman approves → locked. Realizes FR-053–FR-055. (BL-055 / EPIC-07).WBS-7Approved
WBS-7.4WBS-7.4 MoM distribution: in-app notification to all committee members on approval with deep link. Realizes FR-053–FR-055. (BL-056 / EPIC-07).WBS-7Approved
WBS-8Decision ManagementPH-1LApproved
WBS-8.1WBS-8.1 Decision entity + migration: DECN-YYYY-###; outcome enum, rationale, alternatives, conditions, authority, effective date. Realizes FR-062–FR-067, FR-069. (BL-057 / EPIC-08).WBS-8Approved
WBS-8.2WBS-8.2 Decision creation API + UI: canonical outcome dropdown; alternatives; conditions; downstream link required before Issued. Realizes FR-062–FR-067, FR-069. (BL-058 / EPIC-08).WBS-8Approved
WBS-8.3WBS-8.3 Decision immutability enforcement: content locked once Issued; corrections via superseding decision; supersession chain. Realizes FR-062–FR-067, FR-069. (BL-059 / EPIC-08).WBS-8Approved
WBS-8.4WBS-8.4 Decision history list: filterable by outcome, topic, stream, date range, chairman. Realizes FR-062–FR-067, FR-069. (BL-068 / EPIC-08).WBS-8Approved
WBS-9Voting EnginePH-1MApproved
WBS-9.1WBS-9.1 Vote entity + migration: VOTE-…; eligible voters, options (Approve/Reject; or Approve/ConditionallyApprove/Reject; or custom), quorum threshold, abstention flag; lifecycle status. Realizes FR-WBS-9Approved
WBS-9.2WBS-9.2 Vote configuration + open UI: Secretary configures; vote opens; in-app notification to eligible voters. Realizes FR-070–FR-075, FR-077–FR-078. (BL-061 / EPIC-09).WBS-9Approved
WBS-9.3WBS-9.3 Vote casting: one-vote-per-voter enforcement at API layer; abstention option; live aggregate display. Realizes FR-070–FR-075, FR-077–FR-078. (BL-062 / EPIC-09).WBS-9Approved
WBS-9.4WBS-9.4 Quorum enforcement: vote cannot close if present-voters < quorum; check from attendance record. Realizes FR-070–FR-075, FR-077–FR-078. (BL-063 / EPIC-09).WBS-9Approved
WBS-9.5WBS-9.5 Chairman approval: Confirm / Override (+ mandatory reason) / Abstain-from-override; immutable record. Realizes FR-070–FR-075, FR-077–FR-078. (BL-064 / EPIC-09).WBS-9Approved
WBS-9.6WBS-9.6 Vote audit: all choices + timestamps + chairman action in append-only log; immutable after Ratified. Realizes FR-070–FR-075, FR-077–FR-078. (BL-065 / EPIC-09).WBS-9Approved
WBS-9.7WBS-9.7 Conflict-of-interest flag per voter per vote: declaration recorded; no auto-exclusion. Realizes FR-076 (PH-2). (BL-127 / EPIC-09).WBS-9Approved
Acceptance criteria (74 rows) CSV
idtitlestatementrequirement_idslice_idlifecycle_statusdispositiondisposition_reason_refsuperseded_byintroduced_inretired_insource_kindsource_spanlast_referenced
AC-001**Given** a user with a valid Keycloak account <br> **When** they open ACMP and click "Sign In" <br> **Then** they are r**Given** a user with a valid Keycloak account <br> **When** they open ACMP and click "Sign In" <br> **Then** they are redirected to the Keycloak authorization endpoint, authenticate there, and return to ACMP with an authenticated session; no ACMP-managed password is used or stored.FR-001SL-004Approved1
AC-002**Given** a user whose Keycloak token holds a group/realm-role claim mapping to the ACMP `Secretary` role <br> **When** **Given** a user whose Keycloak token holds a group/realm-role claim mapping to the ACMP `Secretary` role <br> **When** they complete SSO login <br> **Then** the session assigns the `Secretary` role and all Secretary actions are available; no manual role assignment in ACMP is required.FR-002SL-004Approved1
AC-003**Given** a user whose Keycloak token has no ACMP role claim <br> **When** they complete SSO login <br> **Then** the sys**Given** a user whose Keycloak token has no ACMP role claim <br> **When** they complete SSO login <br> **Then** the system denies access or assigns the minimum default role (`Submitter`) per configuration, and an `AuthEvent` is emitted to the audit log.FR-018SL-004Approved1
AC-004**Given** an authenticated user idle beyond the configured session timeout <br> **When** they attempt any protected acti**Given** an authenticated user idle beyond the configured session timeout <br> **When** they attempt any protected action <br> **Then** they are redirected to Keycloak for re-authentication and their session is terminated; no data is lost from any auto-saved in-progress form.FR-001SL-004Approved1
AC-005**Given** a user with `Submitter` role <br> **When** they view the application <br> **Then** navigation for triage, agen**Given** a user with `Submitter` role <br> **When** they view the application <br> **Then** navigation for triage, agenda publishing, meeting scheduling, vote management, decision recording, and chairman approval is not rendered; any direct API call to those endpoints returns HTTP 403.FR-024SL-004Approved1
AC-006**Given** a user with `Auditor` role <br> **When** they attempt to submit a form that creates or mutates a topic, decisi**Given** a user with `Auditor` role <br> **When** they attempt to submit a form that creates or mutates a topic, decision, vote, or action <br> **Then** the submit button is not rendered, and any direct POST/PUT/DELETE returns HTTP 403 with an audit event emitted.FR-024SL-004Approved1
AC-007**Given** an `Administrator` user <br> **When** they attempt to vote on a topic, approve a decision, or close a vote via**Given** an `Administrator` user <br> **When** they attempt to vote on a topic, approve a decision, or close a vote via the API <br> **Then** the system returns HTTP 403; platform-admin authority never extends to committee-content actions (SoD-5).FR-018SL-004Approved1
AC-008**Given** any request to a protected endpoint <br> **When** no valid session token is present <br> **Then** the response**Given** any request to a protected endpoint <br> **When** no valid session token is present <br> **Then** the response is HTTP 401 and no protected data is returned.FR-024SL-004Approved1
AC-009**Given** a `Member` assigned as `Owner` on topic TOP-2026-005 <br> **When** they submit an edit to that topic's descrip**Given** a `Member` assigned as `Owner` on topic TOP-2026-005 <br> **When** they submit an edit to that topic's description <br> **Then** the edit is accepted; when the same Member edits TOP-2026-006 (where they are not Owner), the API returns HTTP 403.FR-022SL-004Approved1
AC-010**Given** a `Member` whose stream assignment is "Stream-A" <br> **When** they attempt to create an action on a topic who**Given** a `Member` whose stream assignment is "Stream-A" <br> **When** they attempt to create an action on a topic whose `AffectedStreams` is exclusively "Stream-B" <br> **Then** the API returns HTTP 403 for stream-scope violation and the attempt is emitted to the audit log.FR-019SL-004Approved1
AC-011**Given** a `Guest/Presenter` assigned as Presenter on topic TOP-2026-007 for meeting MTG-2026-003 <br> **When** they at**Given** a `Guest/Presenter` assigned as Presenter on topic TOP-2026-007 for meeting MTG-2026-003 <br> **When** they attempt to access topic TOP-2026-008 or any action outside that meeting scope <br> **Then** the system returns HTTP 403; the presenter relationship is scoped only to the assigned topic and meeting window.FR-022SL-004Approved1
AC-012**Given** Alice owns action ACT-2026-010 and has marked it Completed <br> **When** Alice attempts to mark ACT-2026-010 a**Given** Alice owns action ACT-2026-010 and has marked it Completed <br> **When** Alice attempts to mark ACT-2026-010 as Verified <br> **Then** the system returns an error ("Verifier cannot be the action owner"), the action stays Completed, and the denied attempt is audited (SoD-1).FR-085SL-008Approved1
AC-013**Given** Bob (Secretary), not the owner of action ACT-2026-010 <br> **When** Bob marks ACT-2026-010 as Verified <br> ****Given** Bob (Secretary), not the owner of action ACT-2026-010 <br> **When** Bob marks ACT-2026-010 as Verified <br> **Then** the action transitions to Verified, the verification actor and timestamp are recorded, and an `ActionVerified` audit event is emitted (SoD-1 positive).FR-085SL-008Approved1
AC-014**Given** Secretary Carol is both sole author and approver of MoM MIN-2026-002 <br> **When** Carol approves it <br> **Th**Given** Secretary Carol is both sole author and approver of MoM MIN-2026-002 <br> **When** Carol approves it <br> **Then** the system allows approval but emits a flagged audit event (`MinutesApprovedBySoleAuthor`) and shows a warning indicator; the MoM transitions to Approved (soft SoD-2).FR-054SL-007Approved1
AC-015**Given** Chairman Dave attempts to close vote VOTE-2026-004 and record his own override on the same decision without a **Given** Chairman Dave attempts to close vote VOTE-2026-004 and record his own override on the same decision without a Secretary or second Member co-attesting the tally <br> **When** the close action is submitted <br> **Then** the system blocks the close and returns an error requiring co-attestation; the vote stays Open (SoD-3).FR-074SL-009Approved1
AC-016**Given** Secretary Eva co-attests the tally for vote VOTE-2026-004 and Chairman Dave records an Override decision <br> **Given** Secretary Eva co-attests the tally for vote VOTE-2026-004 and Chairman Dave records an Override decision <br> **When** the override is submitted <br> **Then** the decision records Dave's override, the mandatory justification, Eva's co-attestation, and the timestamp; the event is `DecisionIssued` with `overrideFlag=true` and the audit entry is immutable.FR-074SL-009Approved1
AC-017**Given** a state transition on any governed entity (topic, decision, vote, action, risk, MoM) <br> **When** the transac**Given** a state transition on any governed entity (topic, decision, vote, action, risk, MoM) <br> **When** the transaction commits <br> **Then** an audit entry is created with entity type, entity ID, action type, actor ID, UTC timestamp, before-state JSON, after-state JSON, and correlation ID, visible to Auditor, Chairman, and Secretary (**not** Administrator — SoD-5, ADR-0027 supersedes the FR-153 role clause).FR-150Approved1
AC-018**Given** an audit log entry exists in the database <br> **When** any party (a DBA acting outside the app, or any API ca**Given** an audit log entry exists in the database <br> **When** any party (a DBA acting outside the app, or any API call including Administrator) attempts to UPDATE or DELETE that row <br> **Then** the attempt fails via database constraint or application guard; no audit row is ever modified in normal operation.FR-152Approved1
AC-019**Given** the audit log implements a hash-chain where each entry's hash covers its data plus the previous entry's hash <**Given** the audit log implements a hash-chain where each entry's hash covers its data plus the previous entry's hash <br> **When** the Auditor (or Chairman/Secretary; **not** Administrator — SoD-5, ADR-0027) runs the integrity check <br> **Then** the check reports all entries valid, or reports the first broken link and its entry ID if any entry was tampered with.FR-152Approved1
AC-020**Given** an Auditor searches the audit log with filters entity_type="Topic", actor="user-42", date range "2026-06-01..2**Given** an Auditor searches the audit log with filters entity_type="Topic", actor="user-42", date range "2026-06-01..2026-06-30" <br> **When** the search runs <br> **Then** paginated results show only matching entries with entity type, ID, action type, actor, and timestamp; a Member or Submitter running the same search receives HTTP 403.FR-153Approved1
AC-021**Given** a Secretary configures vote VOTE-2026-005 with eligible voters {Alice, Bob, Carol}, options {Approve, Reject},**Given** a Secretary configures vote VOTE-2026-005 with eligible voters {Alice, Bob, Carol}, options {Approve, Reject}, quorum 2, abstention allowed <br> **When** the vote is opened <br> **Then** the configuration locks, only the three eligible voters can cast a ballot, and the vote appears in each voter's notification center with a deep link.FR-070SL-009Approved1
AC-022**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Open and Alice has already cast her vote <br> **When** Alice attempts a second ballot <b**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Open and Alice has already cast her vote <br> **When** Alice attempts a second ballot <br> **Then** the system rejects it with an error ("You have already voted"); the first ballot is unchanged and the attempt is audited.FR-072SL-009Approved1
AC-023**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Closed <br> **When** the Auditor views the vote record <br> **Then** each eligible voter**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Closed <br> **When** the Auditor views the vote record <br> **Then** each eligible voter's individual choice is visible and attributed (not anonymous), aggregate totals are shown, no name is masked, and the tally matches what the Chairman sees.FR-073SL-009Approved1
AC-024**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 has quorum 2 and only 1 eligible voter has cast a ballot <br> **When** the Secretary attemp**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 has quorum 2 and only 1 eligible voter has cast a ballot <br> **When** the Secretary attempts to close the vote <br> **Then** the system rejects the close with an error ("Quorum not met: 1 of 2 required votes cast") and the vote stays Open.FR-071SL-009Approved1
AC-025**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Closed and Ratified <br> **When** any user (including Administrator or Chairman) attempt**Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Closed and Ratified <br> **When** any user (including Administrator or Chairman) attempts to modify a ballot, the tally, or the chairman's recorded action via API <br> **Then** the system returns an error ("Vote record is immutable after close"), no field is altered, and the attempt is audited.FR-075SL-009Approved1
AC-026**Given** a vote is in Ratified state <br> **When** any party attempts to transition it back to Closed, Open, or Configu**Given** a vote is in Ratified state <br> **When** any party attempts to transition it back to Closed, Open, or Configured <br> **Then** the system rejects the transition; vote lifecycle is strictly forward-only.FR-077SL-009Approved1
AC-027**Given** decision DECN-2026-003 is in Issued status <br> **When** any user (including Secretary or Chairman) attempts t**Given** decision DECN-2026-003 is in Issued status <br> **When** any user (including Secretary or Chairman) attempts to edit its outcome, rationale, alternatives, or conditions via API or UI <br> **Then** the system returns an error ("Issued decisions are immutable; create a superseding decision"), no field is altered, and the attempt is audited.FR-066SL-007Approved1
AC-028**Given** a Secretary creates decision DECN-2026-004 that supersedes DECN-2026-003 <br> **When** DECN-2026-004 is Issued**Given** a Secretary creates decision DECN-2026-004 that supersedes DECN-2026-003 <br> **When** DECN-2026-004 is Issued <br> **Then** DECN-2026-003 is marked Superseded with a `SupersededByDecisionId` back-link to DECN-2026-004; both records remain readable and the prior content is unchanged.FR-065SL-007Approved1
AC-029**Given** a Secretary attempts to mark decision DECN-2026-005 as Issued with no downstream artifact links <br> **When** **Given** a Secretary attempts to mark decision DECN-2026-005 as Issued with no downstream artifact links <br> **When** the submit runs <br> **Then** the system rejects it with an error requiring at least one downstream link (Action, Risk, or other artifact) before Issued, and the decision stays in Draft.FR-067SL-007Approved1
AC-030**Given** a user submits a new topic missing a required field (title, description, type, or affected stream) <br> **When**Given** a user submits a new topic missing a required field (title, description, type, or affected stream) <br> **When** the form is submitted <br> **Then** the API returns HTTP 400 with field-level validation errors in the active locale (EN or AR), no topic is created, and the form highlights the missing fields.FR-025SL-005Approved1
AC-031**Given** a Secretary triages a submitted topic and selects Reject <br> **When** the Reject is submitted with no rejecti**Given** a Secretary triages a submitted topic and selects Reject <br> **When** the Reject is submitted with no rejection rationale <br> **Then** the system rejects it with an error ("Rejection reason is required") and the topic stays in Submitted or Triage status.FR-029SL-005Approved1
AC-032**Given** a Secretary provides a valid rejection rationale and submits <br> **When** the rejection is processed <br> **T**Given** a Secretary provides a valid rejection rationale and submits <br> **When** the rejection is processed <br> **Then** the topic transitions to Rejected; the reason, actor, and timestamp are recorded as an immutable history event and the submitter receives an in-app notification.FR-029SL-005Approved1
AC-033**Given** topic TOP-2026-010 has been Rejected with a recorded reason <br> **When** any user (including Administrator) a**Given** topic TOP-2026-010 has been Rejected with a recorded reason <br> **When** any user (including Administrator) attempts to delete or modify the rejection event <br> **Then** the system rejects the mutation; the rejection event is immutable.FR-044SL-005Approved1
AC-034**Given** topic TOP-2026-012 is Accepted and a Member who is not the Owner attempts to edit its description <br> **When***Given** topic TOP-2026-012 is Accepted and a Member who is not the Owner attempts to edit its description <br> **When** the edit is submitted <br> **Then** the system returns HTTP 403; only the Secretary may edit metadata after Acceptance and content fields are locked against retroactive modification.FR-040SL-005Approved1
AC-035**Given** a topic is Accepted and the Owner has completed all required preparation materials <br> **When** the Secretary**Given** a topic is Accepted and the Owner has completed all required preparation materials <br> **When** the Secretary marks the topic as Prepared <br> **Then** the topic transitions to Prepared, a `TopicPrepared` audit event is emitted, and the topic becomes eligible for scheduling.FR-042SL-005Approved1
AC-036**Given** MoM MIN-2026-003 is Published (approved and locked) <br> **When** the Secretary finds an error and submits a c**Given** MoM MIN-2026-003 is Published (approved and locked) <br> **When** the Secretary finds an error and submits a correction <br> **Then** the system creates a new version (MIN-2026-003 v2) as a superseding document; the prior version stays readable, immutable, and linked, with no in-place edit.FR-054SL-007Approved1
AC-037**Given** MoM MIN-2026-004 is InReview and the Chairman requests changes <br> **When** the Chairman submits the change r**Given** MoM MIN-2026-004 is InReview and the Chairman requests changes <br> **When** the Chairman submits the change request <br> **Then** the MoM transitions back to Draft, the Secretary is notified, and the review cycle restarts.FR-054SL-007Approved1
AC-038**Given** the Chairman approves MoM MIN-2026-005 <br> **When** the approval is committed <br> **Then** the MoM transitio**Given** the Chairman approves MoM MIN-2026-005 <br> **When** the approval is committed <br> **Then** the MoM transitions to Published (immutable), an in-app notification with a deep link is sent to all committee members, and the `MoMApproved` audit event records the actor and timestamp.FR-055SL-007Approved1
AC-039**Given** a user has an unsaved form with data and the interface is in English <br> **When** they switch the locale to A**Given** a user has an unsaved form with data and the interface is in English <br> **When** they switch the locale to Arabic <br> **Then** all labels, buttons, validation messages, and navigation render in Arabic and the form data is preserved exactly; no unsaved data is lost during the switch.FR-003Approved1
AC-040**Given** a user selects Arabic as their locale <br> **When** any page renders <br> **Then** `dir="rtl"` is applied to t**Given** a user selects Arabic as their locale <br> **When** any page renders <br> **Then** `dir="rtl"` is applied to the root, text flows right-to-left, navigation is mirrored, inputs are right-aligned, table columns order right-to-left, and no element uses a hardcoded LTR layout.FR-004Approved1
AC-041**Given** the Arabic locale is active <br> **When** a visual regression test captures every page <br> **Then** no LTR fl**Given** the Arabic locale is active <br> **When** a visual regression test captures every page <br> **Then** no LTR flow artifacts are detected (no left-anchored text blocks, misaligned icons, or cropped RTL text) on Chrome and Edge.FR-004Approved1
AC-042**Given** a user sets their theme preference to "dark" <br> **When** they end the session and log in again <br> **Then****Given** a user sets their theme preference to "dark" <br> **When** they end the session and log in again <br> **Then** the dark theme is active without being set again.FR-005Approved1
AC-043**Given** a user on the backlog view who cannot or chooses not to use drag-and-drop <br> **When** they select a topic an**Given** a user on the backlog view who cannot or chooses not to use drag-and-drop <br> **When** they select a topic and use the keyboard move-up/move-down controls <br> **Then** the topic's priority ordinal changes correctly, the UI reflects the new position, and the change is persisted with no mouse interaction.FR-034SL-005Approved1
AC-044**Given** a user building an agenda on the agenda-item reorder UI <br> **When** they use the keyboard alternative (move-**Given** a user building an agenda on the agenda-item reorder UI <br> **When** they use the keyboard alternative (move-up/move-down or ARIA-described interaction) <br> **Then** items reorder correctly without drag-and-drop and the focus ring stays visible and moves to the moved item.FR-047SL-006Approved1
AC-045**Given** any interactive element (buttons, links, form controls, DnD alternatives, modals) <br> **When** a keyboard-onl**Given** any interactive element (buttons, links, form controls, DnD alternatives, modals) <br> **When** a keyboard-only user tabs through the page <br> **Then** every interactive element receives a visible focus indicator (contrast ratio at least 3:1, WCAG 2.2 AA SC 2.4.11) and nothing mouse-reachable is keyboard-unreachable.FR-034SL-005Approved1
AC-046**Given** any form field, label, button, or icon <br> **When** inspected for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance <br> **Then** every **Given** any form field, label, button, or icon <br> **When** inspected for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance <br> **Then** every control has a programmatically associated label, every icon-only button has an `aria-label`, DOM reading order matches visual order in LTR and RTL, and contrast is at least 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text.FR-004Approved1
AC-047**Given** a user has unsaved changes to a form (topic edit, action update, MoM draft) <br> **When** they navigate away v**Given** a user has unsaved changes to a form (topic edit, action update, MoM draft) <br> **When** they navigate away via browser back, nav menu, or route change <br> **Then** a confirmation prompt warns of unsaved changes and offers to leave without saving; confirming proceeds, cancelling keeps them on the form with data intact.FR-015Approved1
AC-048**Given** a user is editing a form with unsaved changes <br> **When** they attempt to close the browser tab <br> **Then***Given** a user is editing a form with unsaved changes <br> **When** they attempt to close the browser tab <br> **Then** the browser's built-in beforeunload dialog is triggered (standard behavior; no data is silently lost).FR-015Approved1
AC-049**Given** a user attempts to attach a file to a topic <br> **When** the file exceeds the maximum allowed size (configura**Given** a user attempts to attach a file to a topic <br> **When** the file exceeds the maximum allowed size (configurable, default 50 MB) or has a disallowed MIME type <br> **Then** the system rejects the upload with a clear locale-aware error naming the violated constraint; no partial file is stored and the topic is unchanged.FR-006Approved1
AC-050**Given** a valid file is uploaded to a topic <br> **When** the upload completes <br> **Then** the metadata (filename, s**Given** a valid file is uploaded to a topic <br> **When** the upload completes <br> **Then** the metadata (filename, size, MIME type, uploader ID, UTC timestamp, topic ID) is stored in SQL Server, the content is stored in MinIO via `IFileStore`, and a `DocumentAttached` audit event is emitted.FR-006Approved1
AC-051**Given** a Secretary publishes an agenda for meeting MTG-2026-010 <br> **When** the publish is committed <br> **Then** **Given** a Secretary publishes an agenda for meeting MTG-2026-010 <br> **When** the publish is committed <br> **Then** an in-app notification is created for every committee member within 5 seconds, each carrying the meeting date, agenda title, and a deep link, appearing in the recipient's notification center.FR-130SL-006Approved1
AC-052**Given** an eligible voter receives an in-app notification that vote VOTE-2026-008 is Open <br> **When** they click the**Given** an eligible voter receives an in-app notification that vote VOTE-2026-008 is Open <br> **When** they click the notification <br> **Then** they navigate directly to the voting UI for that vote/topic with no additional steps.FR-131SL-006Approved1
AC-053**Given** the Webex adapter is not yet active (v1 in-app only) <br> **When** any notification is dispatched <br> **Then***Given** the Webex adapter is not yet active (v1 in-app only) <br> **When** any notification is dispatched <br> **Then** it is delivered only via the in-app notification center; no email or Webex is attempted and the absence of other channels raises no error.FR-129SL-006Approved1
AC-054**Given** action ACT-2026-020 has a due date of T+3 days and the reminder threshold is 3 days <br> **When** the daily Ha**Given** action ACT-2026-020 has a due date of T+3 days and the reminder threshold is 3 days <br> **When** the daily Hangfire reminder job runs <br> **Then** the action owner receives an in-app notification of the upcoming due date containing the action title and due date.FR-083SL-008Approved1
AC-055**Given** action ACT-2026-021 is Overdue by more than 2 days (configurable) <br> **When** the Hangfire escalation job ru**Given** action ACT-2026-021 is Overdue by more than 2 days (configurable) <br> **When** the Hangfire escalation job runs <br> **Then** the Secretary and Chairman receive an in-app escalation with the action title, owner, and days overdue, and the escalation is recorded in the audit log.FR-084SL-008Approved1
AC-056**Given** ACMP is running in Docker Compose <br> **When** an Administrator opens the Hangfire dashboard <br> **Then** th**Given** ACMP is running in Docker Compose <br> **When** an Administrator opens the Hangfire dashboard <br> **Then** they see the job queue, history, and failed jobs; failed jobs retry per the configured policy and reminder and escalation executions are listed with timestamps.FR-011Approved1
AC-057**Given** topic TOP-2026-030 has Urgency=Critical and has been in Triage for 4 days, exceeding the 3-day Critical SLA <b**Given** topic TOP-2026-030 has Urgency=Critical and has been in Triage for 4 days, exceeding the 3-day Critical SLA <br> **When** any user views the backlog <br> **Then** the topic shows an aging badge (for example "Overdue 1d"), the Secretary has received an in-app SLA-breach notification, and the badge updates daily.FR-038SL-005Approved1
AC-058**Given** an Administrator deactivates the account for "Bob" <br> **When** Bob attempts to log in <br> **Then** his Keyc**Given** an Administrator deactivates the account for "Bob" <br> **When** Bob attempts to log in <br> **Then** his Keycloak session is valid but ACMP rejects access; all his historical votes, authorship credits, and action assignments stay attributed to "Bob" and no historical data is anonymized.FR-020SL-004Approved1
AC-059**Given** any authenticated user of any role opens the Member Directory <br> **When** the page loads <br> **Then** a lis**Given** any authenticated user of any role opens the Member Directory <br> **When** the page loads <br> **Then** a list of all active committee members is shown with name, role, stream membership, and email; deactivated users do not appear and no authentication error is raised for any viewer role.FR-021SL-004Approved1
AC-060**Given** a user enters a query in the global search bar <br> **When** the search executes <br> **Then** results return **Given** a user enters a query in the global search bar <br> **When** the search executes <br> **Then** results return within 3 seconds grouped by artifact type (Topics, Decisions, MoMs), each showing ID, title, matched excerpt, status, and a deep link that navigates to the artifact.FR-143SL-015Approved1
AC-061**Given** a user types an Arabic query in the global search bar <br> **When** the search executes <br> **Then** results **Given** a user types an Arabic query in the global search bar <br> **When** the search executes <br> **Then** results include Arabic-language matches via the SQL Server Arabic word-breaker, relevant English results are also returned, and no character encoding errors occur.FR-145SL-015Approved1
AC-062**Given** a user views the detail page of any artifact (topic, decision, action, risk, dependency, MoM) <br> **When** th**Given** a user views the detail page of any artifact (topic, decision, action, risk, dependency, MoM) <br> **When** the traceability panel loads <br> **Then** all upstream and downstream typed relationships are displayed with relationship type, target artifact ID, title, and a navigable link.FR-146SL-015Approved1
AC-063**Given** a Secretary creates a typed traceability edge from topic TOP-2026-040 (DerivedFrom) to decision DECN-2026-015 **Given** a Secretary creates a typed traceability edge from topic TOP-2026-040 (DerivedFrom) to decision DECN-2026-015 <br> **When** the edge is saved <br> **Then** the topic's panel shows the decision as an upstream DerivedFrom link, the decision's panel shows the topic as a downstream DerivedFrom link, and the edge creation is audited.FR-147SL-015Approved1
AC-064**Given** any authenticated committee member logs in <br> **When** the committee dashboard loads <br> **Then** it displa**Given** any authenticated committee member logs in <br> **When** the committee dashboard loads <br> **Then** it displays backlog count by status and urgency, the next scheduled meeting with agenda link, open action counts by status (Open/InProgress/Blocked), overdue action count, and the titles/IDs of the last 5 issued decisions, all from live data.FR-135SL-012Approved1
AC-065**Given** a Secretary logs in <br> **When** the secretary dashboard loads <br> **Then** it shows count of topics in Tria**Given** a Secretary logs in <br> **When** the secretary dashboard loads <br> **Then** it shows count of topics in Triage awaiting review, count of MoMs awaiting approval, count of overdue actions beyond the escalation threshold, and the list of topics exceeding their urgency SLA with aging day counts.FR-136SL-012Approved1
AC-066**Given** the Chairman logs in <br> **When** the chairman dashboard loads <br> **Then** it shows votes awaiting chairman**Given** the Chairman logs in <br> **When** the chairman dashboard loads <br> **Then** it shows votes awaiting chairman approval (Closed, not yet Ratified), escalated risks, escalated actions, and topics Deferred at least twice.FR-137SL-012Approved1
AC-067**Given** Webex is enabled and a committee-wide event occurs (e.g. agenda published) <br> **When** the notification disp**Given** Webex is enabled and a committee-wide event occurs (e.g. agenda published) <br> **When** the notification dispatches <br> **Then** exactly one Adaptive Card v1.3 (≤80 KB, no sensitive content) is posted to the committee Webex space and the in-app notification is still delivered per recipient.FR-129SL-006Approved1
AC-068**Given** the Webex API answers HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header <br> **When** the card-send job runs <br> **Then** it**Given** the Webex API answers HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header <br> **When** the card-send job runs <br> **Then** it reschedules for exactly that delay (no tight loop) and dead-letters after the retry cap, visible in the admin job monitor.FR-132SL-006Approved1
AC-069**Given** an inbound Webex webhook <br> **When** its `x-spark-signature` HMAC is invalid <br> **Then** the request is re**Given** an inbound Webex webhook <br> **When** its `x-spark-signature` HMAC is invalid <br> **Then** the request is rejected 401 with no processing; a valid signature returns 200 and is processed idempotently (replayed events older than 5 minutes are dropped).FR-057SL-013Approved1
AC-070**Given** a recording-ready webhook for a correlated Webex meeting <br> **When** it is processed <br> **Then** the recor**Given** a recording-ready webhook for a correlated Webex meeting <br> **When** it is processed <br> **Then** the recording reference (playback/download URL + duration) is stored on the ACMP meeting and a `Meetings.RecordingAttached` audit event is emitted.FR-056SL-013Approved1
AC-071**Given** `Webex:Enabled` is false (air-gapped / v1 posture) <br> **When** any notification dispatches or the webhook en**Given** `Webex:Enabled` is false (air-gapped / v1 posture) <br> **When** any notification dispatches or the webhook endpoint is called <br> **Then** no outbound Webex call is made, in-app remains the sole channel, and no error is raised.FR-129SL-006Approved1
AC-072**Given** an online meeting is scheduled with Webex enabled and a valid OAuth token <br> **When** the create job runs <b**Given** an online meeting is scheduled with Webex enabled and a valid OAuth token <br> **When** the create job runs <br> **Then** a Webex meeting is created and its id + join URL are stored on the ACMP meeting (enabling recording correlation); with no token the meeting persists without Webex and no error surfaces.FR-056SL-013Approved1
AC-073**Given** a Secretary or Chairman uploads a recording file (allowed video type, ≤ 2 GB) to a meeting <br> **When** the u**Given** a Secretary or Chairman uploads a recording file (allowed video type, ≤ 2 GB) to a meeting <br> **When** the upload completes <br> **Then** the file is stored in MinIO (`acmp-recordings`) via `IFileStore` under a server-derived key, its metadata (filename, size, content type) is stored on the meeting, a `Meetings.RecordingUploaded` audit event is emitted, and any committee member can play it back via a short-lived pre-signed URL.FR-056SL-013Approved1
AC-074**Given** a meeting has a recording (uploaded file or Webex reference) <br> **When** a Secretary or Chairman deletes it **Given** a meeting has a recording (uploaded file or Webex reference) <br> **When** a Secretary or Chairman deletes it <br> **Then** the recording reference is cleared from the meeting, the stored MinIO object is removed for an uploaded file, and a `Meetings.RecordingRemoved` audit event is emitted; a Member is forbidden (403).FR-056SL-013Approved1

AV-012AC-012MetSegregationOfDutiesTests + ActionHandlerTests (owner-verify denied + `ActionVerifyDenied` audit, stays Completed) + ActionsApiTests (owner verify → 403, HTTP) + ActionActions.test (Verify hidden from owner/completer; server 403 surfaced without closing the dialog) + p17b-immutability.spec.ts (live real-stack: the owner/completer opens their own Completed action and is offered NO Verify button)1
AV-013AC-013MetSegregationOfDutiesTests + ActionHandlerTests (independent verifier → Verified + `ActionVerified` audit) + ActionsApiTests (third-party verify → 204, Verified) + ActionActions.test (a non-owner verifier sees + triggers Verify → `useVerifyAction`) + p17b-actions.spec.ts (live real-stack: a chairman owns + completes an action, then the secretary — a different sub — opens it and drives Verify in the UI → 204 → the action reaches Verified)1
AV-014AC-014MetMinutesHandlerTests (sole-author approval allowed + flagged; different approver clears the flag) + MinutesApiTests (sole-author publish → ApprovedBySoleAuthor) + MeetingMinutes.test (approved sole-author MoM renders the warning badge; a non-sole-author approval renders none) + p17b-sole-author.spec.ts (live real-stack: a secretary authors → submits → approves a MoM — author === approver — and the minutes banner renders the "Approved by sole author" warning)1
AV-015AC-015MetSegregationOfDutiesTests + VoteHandlerTests (chair counted the vote → issue 403 + audited denial; vote stays Closed, not ratified) + RecordDecisionDialog.test (the issue 403 is surfaced inline; no navigation) + p17b-decision-issue.spec.ts (live real-stack: the chairman who CLOSED the coupled vote records a decision and attempts issue through the RecordDecisionDialog → server 403 surfaced inline; the vote stays Closed, not Ratified)1
AV-016AC-016MetSegregationOfDutiesTests + DecisionHandlerTests (override + justification + flag) + VoteHandlerTests (secretary counted → chair issue allowed + vote Ratified) + RecordDecisionDialog.test (record→issue two-step, override+justification body, record-once retry guard) + p17b-decision-issue.spec.ts (live real-stack, F-03 leg: a secretary closes the coupled vote, then the chairman records + issues the decision with an override + justification through the RecordDecisionDialog → decision Issued + vote Ratified)1
AV-017AC-017MetAuditAtomicityTests (enriched before/after populated end-to-end on real SQL) + AuditApiTests (register surfaces normalized v1+v2 rows, entityType/actor filters, paginated) + the PR2 per-module emit-site migration to `EmitEnrichedAsync`1
AV-018AC-018MetAuditEventEnrichmentTests (valid v1→v2 chain verifies; enriched-field tamper flagged with the correct `Reason`) + AuditEvent design (no public setters, no Update/Delete path; SaveChanges only Adds) + UNIQUE `PreviousHash`/`Hash` non-forking indexes1
AV-019AC-019MetAuditEventEnrichmentTests (tamper → `Verify` reports non-null `BrokenAtSequence` with `Reason`) + AuditApiTests (`GET /api/audit/verify` → {IsValid, BrokenAtSequence, Reason} over an intact chain, RBAC-gated)1
AV-020AC-020MetAuditApiTests (Auditor/Chairman/Secretary → 200; Member/Reviewer/**Administrator** → 403; no token → 401 — on both `/api/audit` and `/api/audit/verify`)1
AV-021AC-021MetVoteTests + VoteHandlerTests (Open locks config + present-quorum via the seam + VoteOpened fan-out) + VotesApiTests + p17b-meeting-vote.spec.ts (live real-stack: the secretary calls a vote from the in-session MeetingWorkspace's CallVoteDialog, configures the eligible voters + quorum, then opens it on the vote page — the config locks and the roster is exactly the two voting-eligible members)1
AV-022AC-022MetVoteTests (second cast throws) + VoteHandlerTests (double-vote audited denial `Decisions.BallotDenied`) + VotesApiTests (2nd cast → 409) + p17b-immutability.spec.ts (live real-stack: a voter who has cast is shown their recorded ballot + a "Change vote" action — Fork 1 — never an "already voted" rejection)1
AV-023AC-023MetVoteHandlerTests + VotesApiTests (GetVoteByKey returns attributed ballots, no masking) + p17b-voting.spec.ts (live real-stack: a chairman genuinely casts a ballot, the secretary closes the vote, then the closed roster renders that ballot attributed to the chairman by name + choice — never anonymized, ADR-0010)1

PE-128Status reconciliation after re-population, value-diffed against the prior package rather than assumed: deferred-work Done = historic D-13/15/16/18/19/21/23 (identity-keyed), Activated = D-02; 15 work items re-marked Deferred; PH-0/PH-1 re-marked Implemented; risk states reproduced by the 2.4.0 parser without repair.2026-07-23
PE-129Residuals after re-population, deliberately left open: the derived backlog still lists unevidenced work items as open (recorded as an open defect row); leaf work items carry no phase inheritance; the package-header name retains the interim -v2 suffix (cosmetic; no tool writes the packages row).2026-07-23
Defects (13 rows) CSV
idtitleseveritystatusfound_infixed_bylast_referenced
DEF-0012.3.0 migration: title-fallback path was lossy - _clean_line capped at 120, stripped every ASCII hyphen, then wrote statement: title, destroying the full text of every fallback row (78 requirements, 50 tests, 4 phases). Fixed by the 2.4.0 parser: long-form columns take the raw cell; title_fallbacks reclassified as a data-loss warning.highFixed
DEF-0022.3.0 migration: silent truncation of ~415 fields at exactly 120 or 200 characters with no warning at any stage. Fixed by the 2.4.0 parser (statements full-text; truncations ledger reports title caps) and verified in the re-population fidelity report: no statement field at any cap.highFixed
DEF-0032.3.0 migration: hyphen deletion corrupted governed identifiers (FR-001 became FR 001; BL- cross-references severed). Fixed by the 2.4.0 parser: _clean_line strips markdown positionally and ASCII hyphens survive; verified on the re-populated work-item titles.highFixed
DEF-0042.3.0 migration: typed-column starvation - values parked in the provenance bag while tests.kind, deferred_work.activation_trigger, kpis.measure and stakeholder role columns stayed NULL. Largely fixed by 2.4.0 column aliases; residual: wbs_items.phase_id on leaves (no parent inheritance) and stakeholders.role (v1 value is byte-identical to name).highFixed
DEF-0052.3.0 migration: positional identifier renumbering - D-nn mapped to DW-NNN by row order over an unsorted source, silently shifting five ids. Fixed by the 2.4.0 parser: the crosswalk keys on the parsed number (verified identity D-01..D-23 to DW-001..DW-023 in the re-population preview).highFixed
DEF-0062.3.0 migration: degenerate titles on the 20 WBS epic rows (the phase-number cell became the title). Partially fixed by 2.4.0's degenerate-title guard, which however picks the EPIC id cell (EPIC-18) rather than the Title cell because the title-column alias list matches epic before any title alias and the 3-character guard does not fire on 7 characters. Real epic names re-applied in the carry-over; upstream alias fix suggested.mediumFixed
DEF-0072.3.0 migration: a source document consumed into rows and its narrative discarded (planning/roadmap.md produced phase and milestone rows and no document, losing the P1-P19 ladder and the legacy token map). Fixed by the 2.4.0 parser: row-bearing files also emit a narrative document with sections.highFixed
DEF-0082.3.0 migration: section splitting on level-two headings only collapsed 23 dated progress-log entries into one 82622-character preamble. Fixed by the 2.4.0 parser: sections split on the shallowest heading level below the document title.mediumFixed
DEF-009Emitted handoff prompts carried v1-protocol instructions and dead relative links, and the kickoff prompt was seven slices stale. Fixed in the 2.3.0-era repair (prompts rewritten to the v2 protocol) and preserved through the re-population: the managed emitter refuses to overwrite the diverged on-disk v2 files, and the PRM rows are re-authored to match.highFixed
DEF-010Status defaulting applied where the status is present: all phases defaulted to Approved although the roadmap prose carries per-phase status sentences (complete / substantially delivered / not started). Still true of the 2.4.0 parser (the prose sentence is not parsed); PH-0/PH-1 re-marked Implemented in the carry-over, keyed to the roadmap prose.mediumFixed
DEF-0112.3.0 migration: risk status written to the wrong column (lifecycle only; risk_state kept its default so closed and accepted risks read open). Fixed by the 2.4.0 parser's risk-state mapping - verified in the re-population value diff: retired and accepted reproduced with no repair.mediumFixed
DEF-012The derived backlog view over-reports open work: imported packages carry no execution state, so unevidenced work items land Approved and v_backlog lists them as open for a project whose ladder is complete. The 2.4.0 execution_state_note now discloses this at populate. Evidence-based triage re-applied on 15 deferred rows + the PH-3 tree; the residue (items with no per-item shipping evidence) is deliberately left visible rather than bulk-closed.mediumOpen
DEF-0132.3.0 migration: acceptance criteria were set Approved on import before slices existed, so the immutability trigger permanently blocked slice binding and v_phase_exit read zero forever. Fixed by the 2.4.0 parser (ACs land Proposed) plus this pass's carry-over: all mappable ACs bound to their slice while Proposed, then approved - order is load-bearing, approve-then-bind is impossible.highFixed

DW-014Webex per-user DM cards, meeting auto-invitations, and email→member attendance auto-populatemedium`ICommitteeDirectory` exposes member email (a Membership seam extension) — email is persisted but not projected todayOpenbriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md
DW-015**⭐ HIGH PRIORITY — Topic *Prepare* (Accepted→Prepared) has no UI affordance.** `POST /api/topics/{id}/prepare` (BL-036 / AC-035) shipped **backend-only**: the SPA never calls it (only `accept`/`return` are wired, in the kanban). So no topic reaches `Prepared`, the agenda-builder pool (`GET /topics?status=Prepared`) is **permanently empty in-product**, and "Search topics" shows nothing — the intake→agenda **core loop is broken for real users**. Discovered 2026-07-07 from an operator bug report; root-caused against `docs/domain/entity-lifecycles.md §1` (W4) + the design files (which omit Prepare entirely — the kanban has 5 buckets, no `Prepared`; the timeline jumps Accepted→Scheduled).medium**Activate immediately.** Fix (frontend-only, no ADR): `usePrepareTopic` + a **"Mark prepared"** button on an `Accepted` topic detail (`TopicDetail.tsx` `.dt-actions`); **gating = show-and-enforce** (render for any Accepted topic, backend 403s — the owner is often a plain Member, so a role gate would hide it from the right person); **invalidate three query keys** (backlog + `['topics','prepared']` + `['topics','detail',key]`); a **"Prepared" badge** on kanban cards (Accepted & Prepared share the `accepted` bucket, `topicMeta.ts`, so Prepared is otherwise invisible); reword the agenda-pool empty-state to point to the action. **Optional W4-completeness:** notify the Secretary on prepare (`PrepareTopicHandler` only audits today; the `INotificationChannel` seam exists). **Open sub-questions (raise an OQ if pursued):** the domain has **no "un-prepare" / defer-from-Prepared** transition (a mis-prepared topic is stuck until scheduled); and **design-update-owed** (the design omits Prepare). **✅ RESOLVED 2026-07-09 (Tier 3):** "Mark prepared" button on the Accepted-topic detail (`usePrepareTopic`, show-and-enforce, inline 403) + Prepared kanban badge + reworded agenda empty-state + `PrepareTopicHandler` Secretary notification (skip-self); `core-loop.spec` now **clicks the button** instead of a direct-HTTP prepare (the swapped spec was run locally against the live stack — green; CI re-validates on the PR), and `TopicApiTests` proves the new DI seams. Un-prepare gap → **OQ-049** (default = defer). Design-update-owed still stands.Donebriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md
DW-016Audit immutability + verify hardening: (1) a **DB-permission grant** that makes the `audit` schema INSERT/SELECT-only (block UPDATE/DELETE for out-of-app / DBA writes), and (2) a **nightly Hangfire chain-verify job** (C-INS-02) that runs `GET /api/audit/verify` on a schedule and alerts on a broken link.mediumP16 security-hardening sliceDonebriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md
DW-017Enriched before/after for **owned-child** audit rows. Child ops (e.g. Research add/verify Finding, add/set-status Recommendation) emit an `AuditEvent` (INV-005 holds) but with **empty before/after**: `AuditCaptureInterceptor` captures only mutated `AuditableEntity` rows, and owned children (`Finding`/`Recommendation`) are `BaseEntity`, while the parent aggregate's scalars are unchanged on a child add. Enhancement = make owned children `AuditableEntity` (audit columns → migration) + emit the child-op audit with the **child** as subject (`FND-`/`REC-`) so the capture correlates.mediumAudit-fidelity review / P16Openbriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md
DW-018Audit-append **concurrency contention.** `SqlAuditSink` takes no application-level lock: two genuinely concurrent state-changing requests both read the same chain tip (`TipHashAsync`) and race the `audit.AuditEvents` `PreviousHash` UNIQUE index — the loser gets SQL error 2601 (duplicate key) and the whole operation **500s** (fail-closed, but a hard error rather than a retry). Accepted at design time (ADR-0009/0026 + the `SqlAuditSink.cs` `ponytail:` note — "negligible at ≤20-user on-prem scale; if it ever bites, serialize via `sp_getapplock` or retry"). **Reproduced 2026-07-13** by the P15b live VR: the SPA's login-time `Membership.ProfileSynced` append raced an immediate seed burst, so one `Research.MissionProposed` lost the race and 500'd (audit seq 12/13 both chained off tip `1f738021`). Fix (the pre-named upgrade path): a **bounded retry** on 2601/2627 inside `AppendAsync` — re-read tip, recompute hash, re-insert — which preserves chain linearity + the fail-closed guarantee; or `sp_getapplock` serialization. **2026-07-13 — operator chose to fix now**, then (after adversarial review) to fix it **robustly** rather than cheaply. Arc: a first pass added a bounded optimistic-retry in `SqlAuditSink`; CI then showed the 8-way stress case **deadlocks (SQL 1205)**, whose victim's transaction is rolled back → in-place retry cannot recover. A devil's-advocate review further found a **sink-level app lock is lock-order-unsafe** (handlers like `IssueDecision`/`VerifyAction` emit-then-write-more, inverting `{lock, module-rows}` order). **✅ RESOLVED 2026-07-13 via ADR-0028 (serialize at tx-open):** `AmbientTransaction.EnsureStartedAsync` takes a **transaction-scoped exclusive `sp_getapplock`** ('acmp-audit-chain') immediately after `BeginTransaction` — before any module write — so every audited write-command acquires the chain lock first (one consistent order → no cross-handler deadlock) and appends serialize (no fork, no 1205). Held to commit ⇒ next command reads a committed tip; `THROW` on lock-timeout ⇒ fail-closed; SQL-Server-only (degrades to no-op elsewhere). The **denial/autocommit path** (no ambient tx) keeps the bounded retry as its backstop (broadened to catch the `PreviousHash` **or** `Hash` index race; deadlock-free — no crosswise module locks). Tests: `Concurrent_commands_all_commit_without_forking_the_audit_chain` (8 write-commands serialize) + `Concurrent_denials_all_record_their_audit_via_the_retry_without_forking_the_chain` (8 denials converge via retry) + the same-tx atomicity suite, all green (CI-proven for the write-command deadlock case). No schema/migration.mediumDonebriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md
DW-019**Flaky frontend test — `src/Acmp.Web/src/features/decisions/DecisionPage.test.tsx:174`.** Intermittently reads `/decisions/DECN-2026-008` where `/decisions/DECN-2026-015` is expected; unrelated red on the D-18 PR #111 CI run 2026-07-13, green on the immediate re-run. **⚠ The original diagnosis recorded here was wrong** and is corrected in place (P17a, 2026-07-17): it blamed "non-deterministic decision selection (order-/hash-dependent pick over the mocked decisions list)" — **there is no decisions list and no selection in that test.** `useDecision` is mocked to one fixed object (`:54-56`) and the supersede mutation is a fixed `mockResolvedValue({ key: 'DECN-2026-015' })` (`:78`); both are deterministic. **Real mechanism:** `DECN-2026-008` is simply `setup()`'s initial route (`:62`), and `DECN-2026-015` is reachable only *after* `SupersedeDialog.onConfirm` awaits `mutateAsync` and then calls `navigate()` (`SupersedeDialog.tsx:70-81`). The assertion read `expect(await screen.findByTestId('loc')).toHaveTextContent(...)` — but `loc` (`LocationDisplay`) is **always mounted**, so `findByTestId` resolved on its first poll without waiting for anything, and `toHaveTextContent` then evaluated **exactly once** against a pathname the post-await `navigate()` may not yet have flushed. Hence the initial route, and hence why a re-run passes. **Scope note:** the sibling sync assertions at `:168`/`:188`/`:209`/`:217-220` are **not** affected — `mutateAsync` is *invoked* synchronously inside `onConfirm`, so an awaited `user.click` guarantees the call happened; only code after the handler's own `await` races. They were deliberately left untouched.medium**✅ Done 2026-07-17 (P17a)** — retry the *assertion* rather than the element: `await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByTestId('loc')).toHaveTextContent('/decisions/DECN-2026-015'))`. `:235` left as-is (sync `getByTestId` asserting the path *stays* on the error path — correct there, and preceded by a real `findByText` await).Donebriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md
DW-020**Confidentiality ABAC (C-AUTHZ-04) — `Restricted` topics.** `security-controls.md` C-AUTHZ-04 specifies a `ConfidentialityRequirement` ABAC handler restricting `Restricted` topics (security findings, sensitive partner matters) to Chair/Coord + explicit grantees + Owner/Assignees + Auditor, excluded from default stream visibility **and search results**. Verified 2026-07-16 (P16a): there is **no `Confidentiality`/`Restricted`/`Sensitivity` field anywhere in `src/**/*.cs`** — so this is a **feature**, not a hardening pass (domain field + migration + create/edit UI + search-exclusion + ABAC handler + tests), out of P16 scope.mediumDedicated Confidentiality-ABAC feature slice (raise an FR/OQ)Openbriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md
DW-021**Container hardening — 3 HIGH Dockerfile misconfigs (Trivy).** P16b's Trivy fs scan (gating on CRITICAL) surfaced 3 HIGH findings, non-blocking under the CRITICAL gate and no longer shown in CI once `--severity CRITICAL` filters the report: **DS-0002** (no non-root `USER`) on `deploy/Dockerfile.backend` **and** `deploy/Dockerfile.web`, and **DS-0029** (`apt-get install` missing `--no-install-recommends`) on `deploy/Dockerfile.sqlserver:8-14`. 0 CRITICAL, 0 vuln, 0 secret.mediumB4 kickoff — add non-root `USER` + `--no-install-recommends`, then tighten the Trivy gate to `--severity CRITICAL,HIGH`Donebriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md
DW-022**Inline `style={{}}` usage in the SPA — style hygiene, NOT a security control.** ~110 inline-style sites across ~25 components (18 static, 7 dynamic + `SortableList.tsx`'s @dnd-kit drag transform). P16-B3 planned to refactor them all (static → CSS classes, dynamic → a `useCssVars` CSSOM hook, plus a @dnd-kit CSSOM shim) in order to drop `style-src 'unsafe-inline'`. **Verified 2026-07-16 that the refactor is unnecessary for that goal:** CSP governs `<style>` elements and `style=""` attributes, not the CSSOM, and react-dom applies the `style` prop via CSSOM writes (`style.setProperty(...)` / `style[name] = v` — confirmed in the react-dom source), never serialising a style attribute. With no SSR (client-rendered Vite SPA), no inline style in `index.html`, DOMPurify's allowlist excluding `style` attrs + `<style>` tags, and no CSS-in-JS dependency, `style-src 'self'` ships **with the code as-is** (browser-verified: CSSOM writes apply, `setAttribute('style')` and injected `<style>` are blocked). C-WEB-02 is therefore **Met without this work**.mediumA frontend consistency/design-system pass elects to take it on. **Do not activate for CSP reasons** — that rationale is disproven.Openbriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md
DW-023**P19 closeable-gap follow-up (four buildable items).** P19 was scoped reports-only, so the buildable gaps its audit surfaced are collected here rather than built in the audit slice. (a) **AC-043/FR-034** keyboard priority reorder in the backlog — full-stack, mirror the AgendaBuilder AC-044 ±1-delta pattern (new `POST /api/topics/{id}/priority/move {delta}` neighbor-swap + kanban move-up/down buttons); closes A-03's backlog leg. (b) **AC-032** reject→submitter notify (inject `INotificationChannel` into `RejectTopicHandler`) + **AC-057** Secretary SLA-breach Hangfire sweep mirroring `SweepActionRemindersHandler` (+ a persisted `SlaNotifiedAt` marker on `Topic` + migration). (c) **BL-016** localized validation via an error-code contract translated by the client i18n (AC-049 the real gap; AC-030 already client-localized). (d) **wcag22aa** e2e axe-tag bump on `rtl-a11y.spec.ts` + extend the sweep to the kanban view. Also fold in the **F-04 de-flake** of the `SqlAuditSink` tip-race coverage. Each flips its AC only after its live-leg lands.mediumBuilt on `feat/D23-closeable-gaps` (#149) + `feat/D23-tail-upload-l10n` 2026-07-20/21Donebriefv1:execution/deferred-work-register.md

Document sections (633 rows) CSV

SEC-001DOC-001Preamble--- status: Approved version: 1.0.0 updated: 2026-07-06 owner: lead-secretary --- # Project Charter — ACMP The Architecture Committee Management Platform (ACMP) charter: the problem, objectives, goals/non-goals, scope, success metrics, and stakeholders. Scope was locked with the Secretary on 2026-06-24; changes after lock require a recorded decision (`DEC-`/ADR). This charter defers to the registers under [requirements/](requirements/) and [decisions/](decisions/) for detail.1
SEC-002DOC-001Problem statementThe organization's Architecture Committee governs architecture decisions through a **text-file backlog and ad-hoc documents**. There is no single, auditable system of record: topics, agendas, meetings, minutes, votes, decisions, ADRs, actions, risks, and dependencies live in disconnected artifacts, with no traceability from a need to its decision to its follow-up, no immutability guarantees on votes/decisions, and no bilingual (EN/AR) support. This makes governance slow, hard to audit, and easy to drift. Full context: [domain/current-state](domain/) and [domain/pain-points](domain/).2
SEC-003DOC-001Objectives- Be the **single system of record** for one Architecture Committee: topic intake → backlog → agenda → meeting → minutes → voting → decision → ADR → action → risk → dependency, with end-to-end traceability. - Make the sensitive records **auditable and immutable** (votes, issued decisions, approved ADRs, published minutes; hash-chained). - Be **bilingual and RTL-first** (EN + AR), accessible (AA), and usable by ≤20 committee members on-prem. - Replace the text-file process without becoming generic project management — **architecture governance, not PM**.3
SEC-004DOC-001Goals and non-goals### Goals - A complete, live governance loop with attributed voting and chairman approval recorded by name. - Policy-based authorization (role + per-topic ABAC) with segregation of duties. - Self-contained on-prem deployment (all runtime deps bundled; zero external runtime services in v1). - First-class traceability: every requirement → decision → work → test → risk → acceptance. ### Non-goals (anti-goals) - Not generic project/sprint management (no boards, velocity, capacity planning). - Not a diagram engine (Tarseem renders; the JSON spec is the source of truth). - Not a research-methodology engine (Keystone is the optional companion). - Not a meeting/video platform, mobile-native app, BPM engine, or public portal.4
SEC-005DOC-001Scope### In scope The PH-1 governance loop across Platform, Membership, Topics, Meetings, Decisions, Voting, Actions, Risks, Dependencies, Notifications (in-app), Reporting (role dashboards), Search & Traceability, and Audit & Records — the Must-priority functional requirements in [requirements/functional.md](requirements/functional.md). PH-2 adds ADRs, Invariants, Research/Knowledge, Diagrams, the Webex adapter, and expanded reporting. Phasing: [planning/roadmap.md](planning/roadmap.md). ### Out of scope Generic PM (OOS-01), in-app diagram engine (OOS-02), research-methodology engine (OOS-03), video conferencing (OOS-04), mobile-native (OOS-05), public self-registration (OOS-06), real-time collaborative editing (OOS-07), BPM engine (OOS-08), IdP management (OOS-09), data-warehouse/BI (OOS-10), own email/SMS infra in v1 (OOS-11), external partner portals (OOS-12), Kubernetes/broker (OOS-13), custom search cluster (OOS-14), WebSocket infra (OOS-15). These are rejected for v1/v2 unless a future decision reverses them; deferred (not rejected) items are in [execution/deferred-work-register.md](execution/deferred-work-register.md).5
SEC-006DOC-001Success metrics (KPI-)Governing metric: **traceability completeness** — every MVP requirement reaches a decision, a work item, a test, and an acceptance criterion (Keystone gate G-TRACE). The full catalogue is [domain/metrics-kpi-catalog](domain/) (`KPI-001…KPI-021`); representative targets: | ID | Metric | Baseline | Target | Measurement | Status | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | KPI-001 | Topic-to-decision cycle time | text-file era: unmeasured | trend down; visible per stream | dashboard aggregate | Instrumented (P12) | | KPI-002 | Action SLA adherence (% actions closed by due date) | unmeasured | ≥ committee-agreed threshold | actions dashboard | Instrumented | | KPI-003 | Backlog age (topics aging past urgency SLA) | unmeasured | surfaced + declining | aging indicator | Instrumented | | KPI-004 | Vote-to-ratification integrity (hash-chain verifiable) | n/a | 100% verifiable | Auditor chain check | Met (ADR-0009) | | KPI-005 | Bilingual coverage (strings with EN+AR + RTL clean) | 0% | 100% before ship | i18n parity + RTL VR | Met |6
SEC-007DOC-001Stakeholders (STK-)| ID | Stakeholder / role | Interest | Influence (H/M/L) | |---|---|---|---| | STK-001 | Committee Chairman | Final approval/override on decisions and votes; governance authority | H | | STK-002 | Secretary of the Committee (أمين سر اللجنة) | Primary user / product owner; runs the committee lifecycle | H | | STK-003 | Committee Members | Read all streams; vote; own/contribute to topics | M | | STK-004 | Reviewer / Auditor | Independent verification; audit-chain integrity | M | | STK-005 | Administrator | User provisioning (via Keycloak), Hangfire/ops config | M | | STK-006 | Submitter / Guest-Presenter | Stream requests; time-boxed presentation access (PH-2) | L | Full analysis: [domain/stakeholders](domain/).7
SEC-008DOC-001Constraints and assumptions (summary)Hard constraints (`CON-001…015`) and assumptions (`ASM-001…016`) are in [requirements/constraint-register.md](requirements/constraint-register.md) and [decisions/assumption-register.md](decisions/assumption-register.md). Load-bearing: self-contained deployment (CON-001), mandated stack (CON-002), single committee (CON-010), no email in v1 (CON-006), attributed voting (CON-011), Gregorian dates (CON-012). The non-negotiable invariants are [requirements/invariant-register.md](requirements/invariant-register.md).8
SEC-009DOC-001ApprovalScope confirmed by the Secretary on 2026-06-24 and treated as settled canon (mirrored in the settled-decisions of [decisions/open-decision-register.md](decisions/open-decision-register.md)). This charter is `Approved`; material change requires a new `DEC-`/ADR.9
SEC-010DOC-002Preamble--- status: Approved version: 1.0.0 updated: 2026-07-06 owner: lead-secretary --- # Executive Summary — ACMP1
SEC-011DOC-002In one paragraphACMP is a focused, auditable, bilingual (EN/AR) web platform that is the **single system of record for one Architecture Committee** — topic intake → backlog → agenda → meeting → minutes → voting → decision → ADR → action → risk → dependency, with end-to-end traceability. It replaces a text-file backlog and is deliberately **architecture governance, not generic project management**. It runs **on-prem, self-contained, for ≤20 users**. The MVP (PH-1) governance loop is **built and live through P12**; PH-2/PH-3 add diagrams, Webex, research/knowledge, and AI-assisted extraction.2
SEC-012DOC-002Problem and objectiveThe committee governs architecture through disconnected text files and ad-hoc documents: no single record, no traceability from need → decision → follow-up, no immutability on votes/decisions, no bilingual support. The objective is one auditable, immutable-where-it-matters, RTL-first system of record that makes governance fast, traceable, and tamper-evident.3
SEC-013DOC-002RecommendationBuild a **modular monolith** on .NET 8 / ASP.NET Core / EF Core with **SQL Server as the single datastore**, a **React + TypeScript + Vite** RTL-first frontend, **self-hosted Keycloak (OIDC)** for identity, and **self-hosted Hangfire / Seq / MinIO** — all **bundled in one Docker Compose stack** with zero external runtime services in v1 (Webex is the only external dependency, deferred to Phase 2). Diagrams via the **Tarseem** sidecar (Phase 2); research via the **optional Keystone** companion. Rationale: [architecture/architecture.md](architecture/architecture.md); options weighed in [architecture/technology-comparison.md](architecture/technology-comparison.md); decisions in [adrs/](adrs/).4
SEC-014DOC-002Why this over the alternatives- **Modular monolith, not microservices** — right-sized for ≤20 users on-prem; distribution is pure cost here (ADR-0001). - **SQL Server only, not polyglot** — transactional + columnstore reporting + full-text search from one engine; no second DB to run or back up (ADR-0003). - **Self-hosted bundled Keycloak, not federate-to-org-IdP** — the org has no IdP; ACMP owns its realm and ships it (ADR-0015). - **SQL FTS first, not a search cluster** — sufficient at scale; OpenSearch/Meilisearch only if it outgrows FTS (ADR-0011).5
SEC-015DOC-002Scope at a glance| | Summary | |---|---| | MVP delivers (PH-1) | The full governance loop: intake→triage→agenda→meeting→vote→decision→action, RBAC+ABAC, in-app notifications, EN/AR+RTL, hash-chained audit, role dashboards, FTS, typed traceability, risks + basic dependencies | | Full target adds (PH-2/3) | ADRs + Invariants, Research/Keystone import, Knowledge wiki, Tarseem diagrams + dependency graph, Webex adapter, impact analysis, expanded reporting, AI-assisted extraction (human-gated), email channel | | Explicitly out of scope | Generic PM, in-app diagram engine, BPM engine, mobile-native, public self-registration, real-time collab editing, Kubernetes/broker, custom search cluster, external partner portal (see [charter](00-charter.md) §Out of scope) |6
SEC-016DOC-002Plan and effortFour phases: **PH-0** discovery/validation (done), **PH-1** MVP governance (done, delivered incrementally through P12), **PH-2** governance expansion (substantially delivered — ADRs/Invariants/traceability/reporting shipped; Webex/Tarseem/Knowledge remain), **PH-3** research & knowledge (not started). Details + gates: [planning/roadmap.md](planning/roadmap.md).7
SEC-017DOC-002Top risksRTL effort underestimated (RISK-002), committee adoption (RISK-007), scope creep to generic PM (RISK-006), Tarseem early maturity (RISK-005), Webex Assistant enablement (RISK-008), AI-extraction privacy (RISK-012). Full register: [risks/risk-register.md](risks/risk-register.md).8
SEC-018DOC-002What we are asking forContinued execution of the remaining PH-2 backlog and hardening **under this package's Keystone governance** — every change traced (requirement → decision → work → test → acceptance), every settled decision respected, every invariant enforced. Start point: [handoff/initial-prompt.md](handoff/initial-prompt.md).9
SEC-019DOC-003Preamble# ACMP Architecture Decision Records This folder contains the Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) for the Architecture Committee Management Platform (ACMP). All records use the **MADR** (Markdown Architectural Decision Records) format. See: https://adr.github.io/madr/ ---1
SEC-020DOC-003ADR Index| ID | Title | Status | Date | |----|-------|--------|------| | [ADR-0001](ADR-0001-modular-monolith.md) | Modular Monolith as Macro-Architecture | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0002](ADR-0002-dotnet-clean-architecture.md) | .NET 8 + ASP.NET Core + Clean Architecture + Vertical Slice + EF Core + REST | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0003](ADR-0003-sql-server-single-datastore.md) | Microsoft SQL Server as the Single Datastore | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0004](ADR-0004-keycloak-oidc-identity.md) | Keycloak (OIDC) as Identity Provider | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0005](ADR-0005-notification-channel-abstraction.md) | Notification Channel Abstraction (INotificationChannel) | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0006](ADR-0006-tarseem-render-sidecar.md) | Tarseem as Containerized Render Sidecar (JSON Spec = Source of Truth) | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0007](ADR-0007-keystone-optional-companion.md) | Keystone as Optional Companion (Not Embedded, Not a Hard Dependency) | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0008](ADR-0008-traceability-relationship-model.md) | Typed Directed Relationship Edges as the Traceability Model | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0009](ADR-0009-audit-immutability.md) | Append-Only Audit Log with Immutability and Hash-Chain Integrity | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0010](ADR-0010-voting-model.md) | Always-Attributed Voting Model | Accepted | 2026-06-24 | | [ADR-0011](ADR-0011-search-sqlserver-fts.md) | SQL Server Full-Text Search in v1;
