(Historical fallback: static role config for dev/staging.) | Tech Lead | MVP | Closed | | RISK-002 | **Bilingual/RTL effort underestimated**: RTL layout with logical CSS requires discipline across all UI components; a single LTR layout smuggled in silently creates a regression. EN↔AR locale switching with unsaved-data protection adds surface area. Total RTL effort is consistently underestimated in similar projects. (Category: Technical; Exposure: High) | M | H | Bilingual and RTL are first-class (P-07) from day 1, not a retrofit. All React components use logical CSS properties. Automated RTL visual regression test (Playwright, both languages) runs in CI on every PR. Dedicated RTL pass in Definition of Done (docs/execution/definition-of-done.md). | Tech Lead / Frontend | MVP | Open | | RISK-003 | **Colluding privileged insiders**: A Chairman + Secretary acting together could attempt to alter or suppress a vote or decision record. Collusion cannot be prevented by software alone. (Category: Security / Governance; Exposure: Medium) | H | L | Immutability enforced at code level (no UPDATE/DELETE on audit rows). SHA-256 hash chain over vote + issued-decision audit records makes undetected tampering computationally infeasible (ADR-0009). Auditor role can independently verify chain integrity. Human/process controls (independent audit, legal) are the remaining layer. Accept residual risk. | Security Reviewer | MVP | Monitoring | | RISK-004 | **At-rest encryption keys stored on-host (no HSM)**: At this scale (on-prem VM, ≤20 users, no budget for HSM), database and MinIO encryption keys will be stored on the same host. This is a weaker key-protection posture than HSM. (Category: Security; Exposure: Low) | M | L | Risk is proportional to scale and classification. Mitigations: full-disk encryption on VM; keys in secrets manager (env vars / Docker secrets, not in image); access control at VM/OS layer; document risk explicitly. Accept residual risk as proportional to org's risk appetite for an internal tool. Re-evaluate if data classification escalates. | Security Reviewer | MVP | Accepted | | RISK-005 | **Tarseem early maturity (v1.0.0, ~9 days old at PH-0)**: Released 2026-06-17. Schema declared frozen at v1.0, but as a new project it may have undiscovered bugs, missing capabilities, or schema corrections in early patches. (Category: Technical / External dependency; Exposure: Medium) | M | M | JSON spec is the version-controlled source of truth (ADR-0006). Render artifacts are re-generatable; a broken render is never blocking. ACMP pins to a specific Tarseem release tag in the sidecar Dockerfile. Monitor Tarseem releases; upgrade only after review. Maintain a fallback for diagram-type gaps (attach raw spec + manual export). | Tech Lead | MVP | Monitoring | | RISK-006 | **Scope creep toward generic PM**: Stakeholders may request sprint boards, team capacity planning, resource allocation, or other generic PM features that are explicitly out of scope (OOS-01). (Category: Product / Governance; Exposure: Medium) | M | M | Reference P-01 ("architecture governance, not PM") in every sprint review. Any feature request that is not traceable to an FR or a new accepted OQ is rejected. Out-of-scope exclusions (OOS-01–OOS-15) are cited by ID. | Product Owner (Secretary) | MVP | Open | | RISK-007 | **Committee adoption risk**: Members revert to the text-file process if the platform adds friction or lacks critical features. Adoption failure would make PH-1 delivery irrelevant. (Category: Product / Change management; Exposure: High) | H | M | Involve Secretary and ≥1 champion member in PH-1 design review. Conduct a pilot run of one real committee meeting on the platform before go-live. Gather feedback; address Sev-1 UX blockers before PH-1 go-live. Maintain parallel text-file as fallback only during transition; communicate end-date. | Product Owner (Secretary) | MVP | Open | | RISK-008 | **Webex licensing / Webex Assistant enablement**: Webex transcript retrieval (FR-058) requires Webex Assistant to be enabled — this cannot be done programmatically and requires host/Control Hub configuration or policy. Licensing for bot/integration accounts may require procurement. (Category: External dependency; Exposure: High) | M | H | Webex features are Phase 2 (ADR-0005); in-app notifications operate in PH-1 without Webex. INotificationChannel abstraction means Webex is purely additive. Initiate Webex licensing conversation in PH-0 so it doesn't block PH-2. Treat transcript unavailability as an accepted PH-2 fallback. | Secretary | Full (PH2) | Open | | RISK-009 | **Air-gapped / private-registry dependency and image mirroring**: On-prem deployment may require all container images (ACMP app, SQL Server, Seq, MinIO, Tarseem, Node/Chromium base images) to be mirrored to a private registry. NuGet and npm packages may need internal mirrors. If mirror setup is not coordinated early, deployment is blocked. (Category: Infrastructure / External; Exposure: High) | H | M | OQ-REGISTRY and OQ-NUGET-MIRROR items in docs/decisions/open-decision-register.md. Default recommendation: use public registries in dev/staging; document all image references with digests for prod pull. Coordinate with org's IT/infrastructure team for private registry setup as a PH-0/PH-1 delivery dependency. | Tech Lead / DevSecOps | MVP | Open | | RISK-010 | **Arabic FTS word-breaker quality in SQL Server**: SQL Server's built-in Arabic word-breaker may produce poor-quality search results for committee-specific terminology, especially technical terms transliterated from English. Users searching in AR may get poor recall/precision. (Category: Technical; Exposure: Medium) | M | M | PH-0 spike: run 20 sample Arabic committee queries through SQL Server FTS and score results. If quality is insufficient, escalate OQ-AR-FTS to consider Meilisearch (self-hosted, app-owned) as a Phase 2 search backend behind the same abstraction. Do not commit to Meilisearch until spike results are known. | Tech Lead | MVP | Open | | RISK-012 | **AI extraction privacy (Phase 3)**: Transcript content sent to an LLM endpoint may contain sensitive committee deliberations. If the LLM endpoint is cloud-based, data may leave the on-prem environment. OWASP LLM01 (prompt injection) applies to AI-extracted candidates. (Category: Security / Privacy; Exposure: Critical) | H | H | AI extraction is Phase 3 (settled decision); manual first. Feature is OFF by default; Admin activation requires explicit data-residency confirmation logged in the system. LLM endpoint must be on-prem or org-approved cloud with data-processing agreement. Human approval gate mandatory — no AI content enters records without Secretary approval. Candidate content marked as provisional. | Security Reviewer | Full (PH3) | Open |1
SEC-599DOC-064Preamble--- status: Approved version: 1.0.0 updated: 2026-07-06 owner: lead-secretary --- # Acceptance Criteria — ACMP This register reshapes ACMP's 66 acceptance criteria into the Keystone v1.0.0 table form so every `AC-###` is a defined, validator-recognized row. Each criterion is written as a Given / When / Then scenario, kept faithful to `docs/validation/acceptance-criteria.md`. The **Verifies** column carries the FR- and US- identifiers each criterion validates (Keystone gate G-TRACE), and **Phase** places each criterion in the delivery roadmap. All criteria here are PH-1 governance gates; they are immutable after approval and are superseded, never edited. | ID | Given / When / Then | Verifies | Phase | |---|---|---|---| | AC-001 | **Given** a user with a valid Keycloak account <br> **When** they open ACMP and click "Sign In" <br> **Then** they are redirected to the Keycloak authorization endpoint, authenticate there, and return to ACMP with an authenticated session; no ACMP-managed password is used or stored. | FR-001, US-001 | PH-1 | | AC-002 | **Given** a user whose Keycloak token holds a group/realm-role claim mapping to the ACMP `Secretary` role <br> **When** they complete SSO login <br> **Then** the session assigns the `Secretary` role and all Secretary actions are available; no manual role assignment in ACMP is required. | FR-002, FR-018, US-002 | PH-1 | | AC-003 | **Given** a user whose Keycloak token has no ACMP role claim <br> **When** they complete SSO login <br> **Then** the system denies access or assigns the minimum default role (`Submitter`) per configuration, and an `AuthEvent` is emitted to the audit log. | FR-018, US-002 | PH-1 | | AC-004 | **Given** an authenticated user idle beyond the configured session timeout <br> **When** they attempt any protected action <br> **Then** they are redirected to Keycloak for re-authentication and their session is terminated; no data is lost from any auto-saved in-progress form. | FR-001, FR-013, US-003 | PH-1 | | AC-005 | **Given** a user with `Submitter` role <br> **When** they view the application <br> **Then** navigation for triage, agenda publishing, meeting scheduling, vote management, decision recording, and chairman approval is not rendered; any direct API call to those endpoints returns HTTP 403. | FR-024, US-004 | PH-1 | | AC-006 | **Given** a user with `Auditor` role <br> **When** they attempt to submit a form that creates or mutates a topic, decision, vote, or action <br> **Then** the submit button is not rendered, and any direct POST/PUT/DELETE returns HTTP 403 with an audit event emitted. | FR-024, US-004 | PH-1 | | AC-007 | **Given** an `Administrator` user <br> **When** they attempt to vote on a topic, approve a decision, or close a vote via the API <br> **Then** the system returns HTTP 403; platform-admin authority never extends to committee-content actions (SoD-5). | FR-018 | PH-1 | | AC-008 | **Given** any request to a protected endpoint <br> **When** no valid session token is present <br> **Then** the response is HTTP 401 and no protected data is returned. | FR-024 | PH-1 | | AC-009 | **Given** a `Member` assigned as `Owner` on topic TOP-2026-005 <br> **When** they submit an edit to that topic's description <br> **Then** the edit is accepted; when the same Member edits TOP-2026-006 (where they are not Owner), the API returns HTTP 403. | FR-022, US-015 | PH-1 | | AC-010 | **Given** a `Member` whose stream assignment is "Stream-A" <br> **When** they attempt to create an action on a topic whose `AffectedStreams` is exclusively "Stream-B" <br> **Then** the API returns HTTP 403 for stream-scope violation and the attempt is emitted to the audit log. | FR-019 | PH-1 | | AC-011 | **Given** a `Guest/Presenter` assigned as Presenter on topic TOP-2026-007 for meeting MTG-2026-003 <br> **When** they attempt to access topic TOP-2026-008 or any action outside that meeting scope <br> **Then** the system returns HTTP 403; the presenter relationship is scoped only to the assigned topic and meeting window. | FR-022 | PH-1 | | AC-012 | **Given** Alice owns action ACT-2026-010 and has marked it Completed <br> **When** Alice attempts to mark ACT-2026-010 as Verified <br> **Then** the system returns an error ("Verifier cannot be the action owner"), the action stays Completed, and the denied attempt is audited (SoD-1). | FR-085, US-062 | PH-1 | | AC-013 | **Given** Bob (Secretary), not the owner of action ACT-2026-010 <br> **When** Bob marks ACT-2026-010 as Verified <br> **Then** the action transitions to Verified, the verification actor and timestamp are recorded, and an `ActionVerified` audit event is emitted (SoD-1 positive). | FR-085, US-062 | PH-1 | | AC-014 | **Given** Secretary Carol is both sole author and approver of MoM MIN-2026-002 <br> **When** Carol approves it <br> **Then** the system allows approval but emits a flagged audit event (`MinutesApprovedBySoleAuthor`) and shows a warning indicator; the MoM transitions to Approved (soft SoD-2). | FR-054, US-042 | PH-1 | | AC-015 | **Given** Chairman Dave attempts to close vote VOTE-2026-004 and record his own override on the same decision without a Secretary or second Member co-attesting the tally <br> **When** the close action is submitted <br> **Then** the system blocks the close and returns an error requiring co-attestation; the vote stays Open (SoD-3). | FR-074, FR-075, US-048 | PH-1 | | AC-016 | **Given** Secretary Eva co-attests the tally for vote VOTE-2026-004 and Chairman Dave records an Override decision <br> **When** the override is submitted <br> **Then** the decision records Dave's override, the mandatory justification, Eva's co-attestation, and the timestamp; the event is `DecisionIssued` with `overrideFlag=true` and the audit entry is immutable. | FR-074, US-048 | PH-1 | | AC-017 | **Given** a state transition on any governed entity (topic, decision, vote, action, risk, MoM) <br> **When** the transaction commits <br> **Then** an audit entry is created with entity type, entity ID, action type, actor ID, UTC timestamp, before-state JSON, after-state JSON, and correlation ID, visible to Auditor, Chairman, and Secretary (**not** Administrator — SoD-5, ADR-0027 supersedes the FR-153 role clause). | FR-150, FR-151, US-075 | PH-1 | | AC-018 | **Given** an audit log entry exists in the database <br> **When** any party (a DBA acting outside the app, or any API call including Administrator) attempts to UPDATE or DELETE that row <br> **Then** the attempt fails via database constraint or application guard; no audit row is ever modified in normal operation. | FR-152, US-076 | PH-1 | | AC-019 | **Given** the audit log implements a hash-chain where each entry's hash covers its data plus the previous entry's hash <br> **When** the Auditor (or Chairman/Secretary; **not** Administrator — SoD-5, ADR-0027) runs the integrity check <br> **Then** the check reports all entries valid, or reports the first broken link and its entry ID if any entry was tampered with. | FR-152, US-076 | PH-1 | | AC-020 | **Given** an Auditor searches the audit log with filters entity_type="Topic", actor="user-42", date range "2026-06-01..2026-06-30" <br> **When** the search runs <br> **Then** paginated results show only matching entries with entity type, ID, action type, actor, and timestamp; a Member or Submitter running the same search receives HTTP 403. | FR-153, US-077 | PH-1 | | AC-021 | **Given** a Secretary configures vote VOTE-2026-005 with eligible voters {Alice, Bob, Carol}, options {Approve, Reject}, quorum 2, abstention allowed <br> **When** the vote is opened <br> **Then** the configuration locks, only the three eligible voters can cast a ballot, and the vote appears in each voter's notification center with a deep link. | FR-070, US-045 | PH-1 | | AC-022 | **Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Open and Alice has already cast her vote <br> **When** Alice attempts a second ballot <br> **Then** the system rejects it with an error ("You have already voted"); the first ballot is unchanged and the attempt is audited. | FR-072, US-046 | PH-1 | | AC-023 | **Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Closed <br> **When** the Auditor views the vote record <br> **Then** each eligible voter's individual choice is visible and attributed (not anonymous), aggregate totals are shown, no name is masked, and the tally matches what the Chairman sees. | FR-073, US-046 | PH-1 | | AC-024 | **Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 has quorum 2 and only 1 eligible voter has cast a ballot <br> **When** the Secretary attempts to close the vote <br> **Then** the system rejects the close with an error ("Quorum not met: 1 of 2 required votes cast") and the vote stays Open. | FR-071, US-047 | PH-1 | | AC-025 | **Given** vote VOTE-2026-005 is Closed and Ratified <br> **When** any user (including Administrator or Chairman) attempts to modify a ballot, the tally, or the chairman's recorded action via API <br> **Then** the system returns an error ("Vote record is immutable after close"), no field is altered, and the attempt is audited. | FR-075, US-049 | PH-1 | | AC-026 | **Given** a vote is in Ratified state <br> **When** any party attempts to transition it back to Closed, Open, or Configured <br> **Then** the system rejects the transition; vote lifecycle is strictly forward-only. | FR-077, US-051 | PH-1 | | AC-027 | **Given** decision DECN-2026-003 is in Issued status <br> **When** any user (including Secretary or Chairman) attempts to edit its outcome, rationale, alternatives, or conditions via API or UI <br> **Then** the system returns an error ("Issued decisions are immutable; create a superseding decision"), no field is altered, and the attempt is audited. | FR-066, US-055 | PH-1 | | AC-028 | **Given** a Secretary creates decision DECN-2026-004 that supersedes DECN-2026-003 <br> **When** DECN-2026-004 is Issued <br> **Then** DECN-2026-003 is marked Superseded with a `SupersededByDecisionId` back-link to DECN-2026-004; both records remain readable and the prior content is unchanged. | FR-065, US-055 | PH-1 | | AC-029 | **Given** a Secretary attempts to mark decision DECN-2026-005 as Issued with no downstream artifact links <br> **When** the submit runs <br> **Then** the system rejects it with an error requiring at least one downstream link (Action, Risk, or other artifact) before Issued, and the decision stays in Draft. | FR-067, US-054 | PH-1 | | AC-030 | **Given** a user submits a new topic missing a required field (title, description, type, or affected stream) <br> **When** the form is submitted <br> **Then** the API returns HTTP 400 with field-level validation errors in the active locale (EN or AR), no topic is created, and the form highlights the missing fields. | FR-025, US-016 | PH-1 | | AC-031 | **Given** a Secretary triages a submitted topic and selects Reject <br> **When** the Reject is submitted with no rejection rationale <br> **Then** the system rejects it with an error ("Rejection reason is required") and the topic stays in Submitted or Triage status. | FR-029, US-020 | PH-1 | | AC-032 | **Given** a Secretary provides a valid rejection rationale and submits <br> **When** the rejection is processed <br> **Then** the topic transitions to Rejected; the reason, actor, and timestamp are recorded as an immutable history event and the submitter receives an in-app notification. | FR-029, US-020 | PH-1 | | AC-033 | **Given** topic TOP-2026-010 has been Rejected with a recorded reason <br> **When** any user (including Administrator) attempts to delete or modify the rejection event <br> **Then** the system rejects the mutation; the rejection event is immutable. | FR-044, US-021 | PH-1 | | AC-034 | **Given** topic TOP-2026-012 is Accepted and a Member who is not the Owner attempts to edit its description <br> **When** the edit is submitted <br> **Then** the system returns HTTP 403; only the Secretary may edit metadata after Acceptance and content fields are locked against retroactive modification. | FR-040, US-032 | PH-1 | | AC-035 | **Given** a topic is Accepted and the Owner has completed all required preparation materials <br> **When** the Secretary marks the topic as Prepared <br> **Then** the topic transitions to Prepared, a `TopicPrepared` audit event is emitted, and the topic becomes eligible for scheduling. | FR-042, US-022 | PH-1 | | AC-036 | **Given** MoM MIN-2026-003 is Published (approved and locked) <br> **When** the Secretary finds an error and submits a correction <br> **Then** the system creates a new version (MIN-2026-003 v2) as a superseding document; the prior version stays readable, immutable, and linked, with no in-place edit. | FR-054, US-042 | PH-1 | | AC-037 | **Given** MoM MIN-2026-004 is InReview and the Chairman requests changes <br> **When** the Chairman submits the change request <br> **Then** the MoM transitions back to Draft, the Secretary is notified, and the review cycle restarts. | FR-054, US-042 | PH-1 | | AC-038 | **Given** the Chairman approves MoM MIN-2026-005 <br> **When** the approval is committed <br> **Then** the MoM transitions to Published (immutable), an in-app notification with a deep link is sent to all committee members, and the `MoMApproved` audit event records the actor and timestamp. | FR-055, US-043 | PH-1 | | AC-039 | **Given** a user has an unsaved form with data and the interface is in English <br> **When** they switch the locale to Arabic <br> **Then** all labels, buttons, validation messages, and navigation render in Arabic and the form data is preserved exactly; no unsaved data is lost during the switch. | FR-003, US-005 | PH-1 | | AC-040 | **Given** a user selects Arabic as their locale <br> **When** any page renders <br> **Then** `dir="rtl"` is applied to the root, text flows right-to-left, navigation is mirrored, inputs are right-aligned, table columns order right-to-left, and no element uses a hardcoded LTR layout. | FR-004, US-006 | PH-1 | | AC-041 | **Given** the Arabic locale is active <br> **When** a visual regression test captures every page <br> **Then** no LTR flow artifacts are detected (no left-anchored text blocks, misaligned icons, or cropped RTL text) on Chrome and Edge. | FR-004, US-006 | PH-1 | | AC-042 | **Given** a user sets their theme preference to "dark" <br> **When** they end the session and log in again <br> **Then** the dark theme is active without being set again. | FR-005, US-007 | PH-1 | | AC-043 | **Given** a user on the backlog view who cannot or chooses not to use drag-and-drop <br> **When** they select a topic and use the keyboard move-up/move-down controls <br> **Then** the topic's priority ordinal changes correctly, the UI reflects the new position, and the change is persisted with no mouse interaction. | FR-034, US-027 | PH-1 | | AC-044 | **Given** a user building an agenda on the agenda-item reorder UI <br> **When** they use the keyboard alternative (move-up/move-down or ARIA-described interaction) <br> **Then** items reorder correctly without drag-and-drop and the focus ring stays visible and moves to the moved item. | FR-047, US-034 | PH-1 | | AC-045 | **Given** any interactive element (buttons, links, form controls, DnD alternatives, modals) <br> **When** a keyboard-only user tabs through the page <br> **Then** every interactive element receives a visible focus indicator (contrast ratio at least 3:1, WCAG 2.2 AA SC 2.4.11) and nothing mouse-reachable is keyboard-unreachable. | FR-034, FR-047 | PH-1 | | AC-046 | **Given** any form field, label, button, or icon <br> **When** inspected for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance <br> **Then** every control has a programmatically associated label, every icon-only button has an `aria-label`, DOM reading order matches visual order in LTR and RTL, and contrast is at least 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text. | FR-004, FR-034 | PH-1 | | AC-047 | **Given** a user has unsaved changes to a form (topic edit, action update, MoM draft) <br> **When** they navigate away via browser back, nav menu, or route change <br> **Then** a confirmation prompt warns of unsaved changes and offers to leave without saving; confirming proceeds, cancelling keeps them on the form with data intact. | FR-015, US-009 | PH-1 | | AC-048 | **Given** a user is editing a form with unsaved changes <br> **When** they attempt to close the browser tab <br> **Then** the browser's built-in beforeunload dialog is triggered (standard behavior; no data is silently lost). | FR-015, US-009 | PH-1 | | AC-049 | **Given** a user attempts to attach a file to a topic <br> **When** the file exceeds the maximum allowed size (configurable, default 50 MB) or has a disallowed MIME type <br> **Then** the system rejects the upload with a clear locale-aware error naming the violated constraint; no partial file is stored and the topic is unchanged. | FR-006, FR-027, US-018 | PH-1 | | AC-050 | **Given** a valid file is uploaded to a topic <br> **When** the upload completes <br> **Then** the metadata (filename, size, MIME type, uploader ID, UTC timestamp, topic ID) is stored in SQL Server, the content is stored in MinIO via `IFileStore`, and a `DocumentAttached` audit event is emitted. | FR-006, US-018 | PH-1 | | AC-051 | **Given** a Secretary publishes an agenda for meeting MTG-2026-010 <br> **When** the publish is committed <br> **Then** an in-app notification is created for every committee member within 5 seconds, each carrying the meeting date, agenda title, and a deep link, appearing in the recipient's notification center. | FR-130, US-073 | PH-1 | | AC-052 | **Given** an eligible voter receives an in-app notification that vote VOTE-2026-008 is Open <br> **When** they click the notification <br> **Then** they navigate directly to the voting UI for that vote/topic with no additional steps. | FR-131, US-074 | PH-1 | | AC-053 | **Given** the Webex adapter is not yet active (v1 in-app only) <br> **When** any notification is dispatched <br> **Then** it is delivered only via the in-app notification center; no email or Webex is attempted and the absence of other channels raises no error. | FR-129, US-073 | PH-1 | | AC-054 | **Given** action ACT-2026-020 has a due date of T+3 days and the reminder threshold is 3 days <br> **When** the daily Hangfire reminder job runs <br> **Then** the action owner receives an in-app notification of the upcoming due date containing the action title and due date. | FR-083, US-060 | PH-1 | | AC-055 | **Given** action ACT-2026-021 is Overdue by more than 2 days (configurable) <br> **When** the Hangfire escalation job runs <br> **Then** the Secretary and Chairman receive an in-app escalation with the action title, owner, and days overdue, and the escalation is recorded in the audit log. | FR-084, US-061 | PH-1 | | AC-056 | **Given** ACMP is running in Docker Compose <br> **When** an Administrator opens the Hangfire dashboard <br> **Then** they see the job queue, history, and failed jobs; failed jobs retry per the configured policy and reminder and escalation executions are listed with timestamps. | FR-011 | PH-1 | | AC-057 | **Given** topic TOP-2026-030 has Urgency=Critical and has been in Triage for 4 days, exceeding the 3-day Critical SLA <br> **When** any user views the backlog <br> **Then** the topic shows an aging badge (for example "Overdue 1d"), the Secretary has received an in-app SLA-breach notification, and the badge updates daily. | FR-038, US-030 | PH-1 | | AC-058 | **Given** an Administrator deactivates the account for "Bob" <br> **When** Bob attempts to log in <br> **Then** his Keycloak session is valid but ACMP rejects access; all his historical votes, authorship credits, and action assignments stay attributed to "Bob" and no historical data is anonymized. | FR-020, US-013 | PH-1 | | AC-059 | **Given** any authenticated user of any role opens the Member Directory <br> **When** the page loads <br> **Then** a list of all active committee members is shown with name, role, stream membership, and email; deactivated users do not appear and no authentication error is raised for any viewer role. | FR-021, US-014 | PH-1 | | AC-060 | **Given** a user enters a query in the global search bar <br> **When** the search executes <br> **Then** results return within 3 seconds grouped by artifact type (Topics, Decisions, MoMs), each showing ID, title, matched excerpt, status, and a deep link that navigates to the artifact. | FR-143, FR-144, US-078 | PH-1 | | AC-061 | **Given** a user types an Arabic query in the global search bar <br> **When** the search executes <br> **Then** results include Arabic-language matches via the SQL Server Arabic word-breaker, relevant English results are also returned, and no character encoding errors occur. | FR-145, US-079 | PH-1 | | AC-062 | **Given** a user views the detail page of any artifact (topic, decision, action, risk, dependency, MoM) <br> **When** the traceability panel loads <br> **Then** all upstream and downstream typed relationships are displayed with relationship type, target artifact ID, title, and a navigable link. | FR-146, US-080 | PH-1 | | AC-063 | **Given** a Secretary creates a typed traceability edge from topic TOP-2026-040 (DerivedFrom) to decision DECN-2026-015 <br> **When** the edge is saved <br> **Then** the topic's panel shows the decision as an upstream DerivedFrom link, the decision's panel shows the topic as a downstream DerivedFrom link, and the edge creation is audited. | FR-147, US-081 | PH-1 | | AC-064 | **Given** any authenticated committee member logs in <br> **When** the committee dashboard loads <br> **Then** it displays backlog count by status and urgency, the next scheduled meeting with agenda link, open action counts by status (Open/InProgress/Blocked), overdue action count, and the titles/IDs of the last 5 issued decisions, all from live data. | FR-135, US-082 | PH-1 | | AC-065 | **Given** a Secretary logs in <br> **When** the secretary dashboard loads <br> **Then** it shows count of topics in Triage awaiting review, count of MoMs awaiting approval, count of overdue actions beyond the escalation threshold, and the list of topics exceeding their urgency SLA with aging day counts. | FR-136, US-083 | PH-1 | | AC-066 | **Given** the Chairman logs in <br> **When** the chairman dashboard loads <br> **Then** it shows votes awaiting chairman approval (Closed, not yet Ratified), escalated risks, escalated actions, and topics Deferred at least twice. | FR-137, US-084 | PH-1 | | AC-067 | **Given** Webex is enabled and a committee-wide event occurs (e.g. agenda published) <br> **When** the notification dispatches <br> **Then** exactly one Adaptive Card v1.3 (≤80 KB, no sensitive content) is posted to the committee Webex space and the in-app notification is still delivered per recipient. | FR-129, FR-130 | PH-2 | | AC-068 | **Given** the Webex API answers HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header <br> **When** the card-send job runs <br> **Then** it reschedules for exactly that delay (no tight loop) and dead-letters after the retry cap, visible in the admin job monitor. | FR-132 | PH-2 | | AC-069 | **Given** an inbound Webex webhook <br> **When** its `x-spark-signature` HMAC is invalid <br> **Then** the request is rejected 401 with no processing; a valid signature returns 200 and is processed idempotently (replayed events older than 5 minutes are dropped). | FR-057 | PH-2 | | AC-070 | **Given** a recording-ready webhook for a correlated Webex meeting <br> **When** it is processed <br> **Then** the recording reference (playback/download URL + duration) is stored on the ACMP meeting and a `Meetings.RecordingAttached` audit event is emitted. | FR-056, FR-057 | PH-2 | | AC-071 | **Given** `Webex:Enabled` is false (air-gapped / v1 posture) <br> **When** any notification dispatches or the webhook endpoint is called <br> **Then** no outbound Webex call is made, in-app remains the sole channel, and no error is raised. | FR-129 | PH-2 | | AC-072 | **Given** an online meeting is scheduled with Webex enabled and a valid OAuth token <br> **When** the create job runs <br> **Then** a Webex meeting is created and its id + join URL are stored on the ACMP meeting (enabling recording correlation); with no token the meeting persists without Webex and no error surfaces. | FR-056, FR-057 | PH-2 | | AC-073 | **Given** a Secretary or Chairman uploads a recording file (allowed video type, ≤ 2 GB) to a meeting <br> **When** the upload completes <br> **Then** the file is stored in MinIO (`acmp-recordings`) via `IFileStore` under a server-derived key, its metadata (filename, size, content type) is stored on the meeting, a `Meetings.RecordingUploaded` audit event is emitted, and any committee member can play it back via a short-lived pre-signed URL. | FR-056 | PH-2 | | AC-074 | **Given** a meeting has a recording (uploaded file or Webex reference) <br> **When** a Secretary or Chairman deletes it <br> **Then** the recording reference is cleared from the meeting, the stored MinIO object is removed for an uploaded file, and a `Meetings.RecordingRemoved` audit event is emitted; a Member is forbidden (403). | FR-056 | PH-2 |1
SEC-600DOC-064Traceability- Every Must-priority FR in `docs/requirements/functional.md` is covered by at least one AC (Keystone gate G-TRACE). - AC-### to US-### and AC-### to FR-### mappings are carried in the **Verifies** column of each row. - Governance rule coverage: authorization (AC-005 to AC-011), Segregation of Duties (AC-012 to AC-016, plus AC-007 for SoD-5), audit immutability and hash-chain (AC-017 to AC-020), voting integrity (AC-021 to AC-026), decision immutability and supersession (AC-027 to AC-029), topic lifecycle guards (AC-030 to AC-035), MoM versioning (AC-036 to AC-038), EN/AR and RTL (AC-039 to AC-042), accessibility (AC-043 to AC-046), unsaved-work guard (AC-047 to AC-048), file upload validation (AC-049 to AC-050), notification delivery (AC-051 to AC-053), background jobs (AC-054 to AC-056), aging (AC-057), membership (AC-058 to AC-059), search and traceability (AC-060 to AC-063), and dashboards (AC-064 to AC-066). - All criteria are PH-1 gates; PH-1 exit requires all Must-priority ACs passed (per `docs/planning/roadmap.md`). Test evidence per AC is tracked in `docs/validation/acceptance-audit.md` and the TEST- register in `docs/validation/test-strategy.md`.2
SEC-601DOC-065Preamble--- artifact: ph0-validation status: active version: v1 updated: 2026-06-25 --- # PH-0 — Validation Confirms domain/architecture understanding, applies the open-decision defaults that gate PH-0/PH-1, verifies the local toolchain, and records the Arabic FTS spike. Precedes any scaffolding. ---1
SEC-602DOC-0651. Domain & core loop (confirmed)ACMP is the single, auditable, bilingual (EN/AR) system of record for **one** Architecture Committee — on-prem, ≤20 users, low traffic. It is **architecture governance, not project management**. **Core governance loop (PH-1 must work end-to-end):** `Topic intake → triage → backlog → agenda → meeting → minutes → vote → decision → ADR → action`, with in-app notifications, immutable audit history, basic dashboards, EN+AR (RTL), light/dark. **Non-negotiables wired from day one:** policy (role) + ABAC authorization on every operation; an `AuditEvent` on every state change; votes/decisions/ADRs/published-minutes immutable (superseded, never edited) with SHA-256 hash-chaining; always-attributed voting; no hardcoded strings; self-contained deployment (CON-001).2
SEC-603DOC-0652. Modules (16) & roles**Core:** Membership · Topics · Meetings · Decisions(+Vote) · Actions · Risks · Dependencies · Governance(ADRs+Invariants) · Research · Knowledge · Diagrams. **Cross-cutting:** Notifications · Reporting · Search&Traceability(`trace`) · Audit&Records · Platform(`Acmp.Shared`). Rule: schema-per-module; no module reads another's tables; comms via MediatR contracts / domain events only. **Global roles (from Keycloak claims):** Chairman, Secretary, Member, Reviewer, Auditor, Administrator, Submitter, Guest/Presenter. **Per-topic capabilities (ABAC):** Owner, Assignee/Contributor, Presenter.3
SEC-604DOC-0653. Open decisions gating PH-0/PH-1 — defaults appliedOrg answers received 2026-06-25 are folded in. Defaults are applied to stay unblocked; flagged items still need human confirmation where noted. | OQ | Topic | Applied for PH-0/P1 | Confirm by | |---|---|---|---| | OQ-030 | CI system | **GitHub Actions, GitHub-hosted runners** (repo `github.com/A-H-911/acmp`) | self-hosted-for-prod → OQ-041 | | OQ-031 | Registry | **Public registry + image-digest pinning** (build machine has direct internet) | prod air-gap → P16/P18 | | OQ-032 | NuGet/npm mirror | **Direct access** (not air-gapped) | prod air-gap → P16/P18 | | OQ-024 | TLS | **TLS 1.2+**, no internal mTLS v1 | security review → P16 | | OQ-003 | MFA / session | **MFA Chairman+Secretary; 60-min idle** (Keycloak realm config, no code) | Keycloak setup | | OQ-020 | Standby VM | **Cold standby + documented restore** | revisit → P18 | | OQ-034 | Arabic FTS | **SQL Server FTS** in v1; spike below decides P2 escalation | spike (this doc §7) | | OQ-002 | Reviewer-may-vote | **No** | Secretary/Chairman | | OQ-004 | TopicRequest | **Merged into Topic** | — | | OQ-012 | SPA serving | **Resolved → (a) separate nginx `web` container** (proxies `/api`→api), per user instruction 2026-06-25 + docs/domain/repository-structure.md §8; overrides recommended default (b) | resolved | | OQ-013 | OIDC client | **oidc-client-ts + react-oidc-context** | — | | OQ-014 | Clustered key | **BIGINT IDENTITY** + GUID alt-key; `TOP-YYYY-###` separate display ID | — | | OQ-015 | Ballot storage | **Normalized tables** (VoteSession/VoteOption/VoteCast) | — | | OQ-016 | Notifications | **30s short polling** | — | | OQ-017 | Relationship types | **Fixed 8-type enum** (+`Other`) | — | | OQ-021 | Breakpoints | **Tablet + desktop only** | — | | OQ-022 | Charts | **Recharts + RTL validation spike** in dashboard build | — | | OQ-027 | SAST/DAST | **Semgrep OSS (block high) + ZAP baseline (alert); `dotnet list package --vulnerable` + `npm audit`** | — | | OQ-028 | CSP | **`script-src 'self'`** (Vite bundles, no inline) | — | | OQ-006/010/008 | Enum lists (Source / Meeting type / cadence) | proposed defaults; **Secretary validates before P5/P6** | Secretary | **New open question raised at PH-0 (to be added to `docs/decisions/open-decision-register.md` after confirmation):** - **OQ-041 — Prod CI runner:** PH-1 skeleton uses GitHub-hosted runners. Production builds for an on-prem (possibly air-gapped) VM likely need a **self-hosted GitHub Actions runner** + package/registry mirrors. Owner: DevSecOps/Org IT. Needed-by: P18. Default: provision a self-hosted runner before prod cutover. > **Renumbered 2026-06-25 (was OQ-038).** ADR-0015 / CHANGE-001 canonically assigned **OQ-038 = Keycloak > datastore** in `docs/decisions/open-decision-register.md`. This PH-0 CI-runner item was only ever a *proposed* note (never added to the > registry under that number), so it moves to **OQ-041** to avoid the collision. `docs/decisions/open-decision-register.md` keeps OQ-038 = KC store. **OQ-038 — Keycloak datastore (resolved 2026-06-25, CHANGE-001 infra-slice).** Chose **(a) dedicated Postgres-for-Keycloak** (`keycloak-db`, volume `kcdata`) — the `docs/decisions/open-decision-register.md` recommended default and Keycloak's most battle-tested store; ACMP *application* data stays SQL-Server-only (ADR-0003 / CON-002). Static validation done: `docker compose --env-file .env config` parses clean; the realm bootstrap is well-formed. **Live bring-up done (2026-06-25):** `docker compose up -d --build` started `keycloak` + `keycloak-db` (Postgres) HEALTHY; Keycloak imported the `acmp` realm successfully (KC 26.0.8); OIDC discovery issuer = `http://keycloak.localhost:8085/realms/acmp` with PKCE S256. The earlier "sandbox cannot launch the stack" note (P4-era, when Docker Desktop's engine was down) **no longer holds** — the daemon was reachable and the 6-service stack came up. Keycloak-on-SQL-Server (option b) was not pursued; revisit only if the Postgres sidecar proves operationally undesirable.4
SEC-605DOC-0654. Finding — search-engine discrepancy (recorded, follow ADR)`docs/decisions/open-decision-register.md OQ-034` prose names **Meilisearch** as the FTS escalation target, but the **canonical** decision `ADR-0011` / `README §A` / `docs/decisions/open-decision-register.md R-24` names **app-owned OpenSearch**. Canon wins: **v1 = SQL Server FTS; escalation fallback = self-hosted OpenSearch behind a search abstraction** (`ISearchIndex`). No engine is switched silently. Recommend a one-line correction to OQ-034 to match ADR-0011 (needs Tech-Lead sign-off before editing canon — not edited unilaterally here).5
SEC-606DOC-0655. Toolchain verification| Tool | Required | Found | Status | |---|---|---|---| | .NET SDK | 8.0.x LTS | 8.0.422 (also 9.0.315, 10.0.301) | ✅ pinned via `global.json` → resolves to 8.0.4xx | | .NET runtime | 8.0 | ASP.NET 8.0.28 + NETCore 8.0.28 | ✅ | | Node.js | LTS | v26.3.1 | ✅ | | Docker CLI | Compose | 29.5.3 | ✅ | | Docker daemon | running | Desktop Linux engine was **down** at start | ⚠️ started Docker Desktop; see §7 | | Git | — | 2.54.0 | ✅ | | Keycloak / SQL Server / Seq / MinIO | reachable or stubbed | not provisioned | ⏳ via `deploy/docker-compose.yml`; tests stub Keycloak with `TestAuthHandler` |6
SEC-607DOC-0656. Services & Compose planCompose stack (P1): `api` (ASP.NET Core 8 REST API), `web` (nginx serving the Vite-built SPA, proxies `/api`→`api` — OQ-012 resolved to (a)), `sqlserver` (own instance), `seq` (self-hosted logs/traces), `minio` (S3 storage). Tarseem sidecar = P2; Keycloak federated (not bundled) — for PH-1 dev, point at a dev Keycloak or use the test auth handler. No org runtime infra (CON-001). Secrets via git-ignored `.env` + `deploy/secrets/` (never committed).7
SEC-608DOC-0657. Arabic FTS spike (OQ-034)**Goal:** does SQL Server's built-in Arabic word-breaker give ≥80% recall on representative committee queries? If yes → keep SQL FTS for v1. If no → escalate to OpenSearch (per ADR-0011, §4) in P2. **Method:** SQL Server 2022 container; DB with a `topics` test table (Arabic title/description); full-text catalog + index with the Arabic LCID (1025); seed ~20 bilingual governance rows; run a set of representative `CONTAINS`/`FREETEXT` queries (technical terms, transliterations, inflected forms); recall = matched-expected / total-expected. **Result (2026-06-25):** **Inconclusive on recall — but a concrete deployment finding surfaced.** The stock `mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest` image returns `SERVERPROPERTY('IsFullTextInstalled') = 0`: Full-Text Search is **not bundled**, and the `mssql-server-fts` package is **not in the image's default apt sources**. So the Arabic word-breaker recall test could not be run on the stock image. **Interpretation (per advisor guidance):** "couldn't test → defer," **not** "SQL FTS inadequate." The v1 search decision is **unchanged**: SQL Server FTS (ADR-0011 / R-24); escalation fallback = app-owned OpenSearch behind a search abstraction. The Arabic recall measurement moves to the **P5/Topics+search build**, when the FTS-enabled image exists. **Action item (before P5 search work):** v1 SQL FTS requires a **derived SQL Server image** that installs `mssql-server-fts` from the Microsoft `mssql-server` apt repo (or FTS enabled on the on-prem SQL instance). The P1 `docker-compose` uses the stock image (no FTS needed yet for the foundation); a `Dockerfile.sqlserver` (FROM the base + FTS) is added when search lands. Logged to progress-log; carry into P5 backlog. **Resolved (P15f, 2026-07-15):** ran the spike on the derived `deploy/Dockerfile.sqlserver` (base + `mssql-server-fts`). `IsFullTextInstalled=1`; `sys.fulltext_languages` contains **lcid 1025 (Arabic)**. FREETEXT recall over 20 committee-term queries = **82.9% micro-recall (≥80% gate)** — the word-breaker handles inflection (ال- prefix, sound + broken plurals) but misses derivation (عمارة↔معماري, بحث). **Decision: SQL Server FTS + LIKE-substring booster** (the booster recovers the derivational misses), **single datastore — INV-002 not triggered, no OpenSearch, no ADR.** Realized in P15f; proven end-to-end by `SearchProvidersFtsTests` on a real FTS SQL Server (AC-061). OQ-034 → Resolved.8
SEC-609DOC-0658. P1 scaffold scope (what's in / deferred)**In (P1):** `global.json`, `acmp.sln`, `Acmp.Api` host, `Acmp.Shared` kernel, **Membership** reference module end-to-end (1 command + 1 query + validator + DbContext + migration), MediatR behaviors (validation/authorization/audit/logging), `IClock`/`ICurrentUser`/`IFileStore`/`INotificationChannel`, health checks, Serilog→Seq + OpenTelemetry, React shell (routing + i18n EN/AR + RTL + light/dark), `deploy/` (Dockerfiles + compose api/web/sqlserver/seq/minio), ArchUnit boundary tests, CI skeleton. **Deferred (later phases, per plan):** full OIDC wiring (P3), full Membership/ABAC (P4), all other modules (P5+), Hangfire jobs UX, Tarseem/Webex/Keystone, security hardening, full test matrix. No business features in P1 (per phase-prompt P1).9
SEC-610DOC-0659. PH-0 exit checklist- [x] Domain/modules/roles/core-loop confirmed against canon. - [x] PH-0/P1 open-decision defaults applied and recorded; org answers folded in. - [x] Toolchain verified (.NET 8 / Node / Docker CLI / Git). - [x] Docker daemon healthy (Docker Desktop started during PH-0). - [x] Arabic FTS spike run and recorded (§7) — stock image lacks FTS; recall test deferred to P5; v1 = SQL FTS stands. - [x] Repo scaffolded per `docs/domain/repository-structure.md`; `docker compose up` healthy (api + sqlserver + web healthy; seq + minio running). → **report before P2**.10
SEC-611DOC-066Preamble--- artifact: rebuild-findings status: active version: v1 updated: 2026-06-30 author: forensic self-review (read-only) purpose: Drive a design update + a second re-fidelity rebuild. Findings only — no code/design/docs changed. --- # ACMP Rebuild Findings — Forensic Self-Review **Scope.** Read-only forensic audit of ACMP execution through P6 + the S1–S7 test-hardening program. Sources: full git history (P3–P6 fidelity-refresh commits `125c0c5`→`b7ab531`; coverage commits `5b7141c`→`83aa33f`; the original UI-fidelity commit `16f2f8b`); the four `_progress` docs (progress-log, design-parity-ledger, acceptance-audit, ph0-validation); the running UI (`src/Acmp.Web`); every `.dc.html` in `/ACMP product context/`; and canon (`docs/domain/information-architecture.md`, `docs/domain/permission-role-matrix.md`, `docs/validation/acceptance-criteria.md`, `docs/decisions/open-decision-register.md`, `/adr`). **Severity key.** `BLOCKER` = misleads the rebuild or breaks doc/auth integrity, must resolve first · `MAJOR` = real divergence or gap needing a design/data decision · `MINOR` = cosmetic / small / already-justified. **Tagging.** Each row carries: ID · Category · Surface · Description · Evidence · Severity · Needs-from-design · Needs-restructure. "Op-approved" = operator signed off in the log; "Flagged" = recorded as drift but not yet blessed; "Settled-by-new-file?" answers whether a design file added after the build now governs it. **One load-bearing correction up front (verified against code, not the log).** The design-parity-ledger and the progress-log **disagree** on the StatusChip canonical size. `StatusChip.tsx:12` + `components.css:48` show the implementation uses **md 24/9/12 (DS §08)** and **sm 22/8/11.5 (§09)**. Ledger line 41 records "StatusChip 22/8/11.5 (DS §08 canonical, overrides the dc's 23/9/12)" — which conflates the §09 *sm* value (22) with the §08 *md* canon (24). The code is the tiebreaker: §08 = 24, §09 = 22, the `.dc` showed 23. This surfaces as both a §1 deviation (DV-01) and a §8 doc-integrity item (DI-06). ---1
SEC-612DOC-066§1 — Design Deviations / DriftDivergences from a `.dc.html` (visual or behavioural). "Settled-by-new-file?" = does a NEW/updated design file now resolve it. | ID | Surface | Description | Evidence | Class | Severity | Needs from design | Needs restructure | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | DV-01 | StatusChip (all screens) | Impl md = **24/9/12**, sm = **22/8/11.5**; the `.dc` showed **23/9/12**. Impl overrides the dc to the DS-canon size. | `StatusChip.tsx:12`; `styles/components.css:48,52`; ledger:41 (mis-records); git `125c0c5` | Token/anatomy (DS canon overrides dc) | MINOR (visual) / see DI-06 | One canonical chip size (is §08 = 24 or 23?) | No | | DV-02 | Notifications full page `/notifications` | Spec/docs/domain/information-architecture.md implies infinite scroll; impl uses an explicit **"Load more"** button over real server paging. | `api/notifications.ts:49` (`useInfiniteNotifications`); `NotificationsPage.tsx:120`; progress-log:597 | Op-approved ("simpler, keyboard-friendly") | MINOR | Bless Load-more vs infinite-scroll | No | | DV-03 | Meeting workspace — Elapsed timer | Live elapsed timer renders **mm:ss / h:mm:ss**; timeboxes/durations render in **minutes** (the intended mm:ss→minutes swap applies to timeboxes only). | `MeetingWorkspace.tsx:68-75`; ledger:44 ("mm:ss→minutes" intended) | Mixed (timer mm:ss live; timebox minutes intended) | MINOR | Confirm elapsed-timer format the design wants | No | | DV-04 | Rich text (Submit / Meeting notes / MoM) | Design shows rich-text editors; impl renders **three different things**: inert `aria-hidden` toolbar + plain `<Textarea>` (Submit), a **functional markdown-into-plaintext** toolbar (Meeting notes), and a deferred MoM. Stored content is plain text. | Submit `SubmitTopic.tsx:317-334`; Meeting `MeetingWorkspace.tsx:379-400`; MoM → P7 | Behavior SoT (data = plain text) | MAJOR | Decide rich vs markdown vs plain, one model | Yes — unify into one RTE component | | DV-05 | Notifications full page | docs/domain/information-architecture.md "type" filter dropped; impl ships a client-side **Unread/All** toggle only. | `NotificationsPage.tsx`; progress-log:595 | Flagged (YAGNI) | MINOR | Keep or restore a type/category filter | No | | DV-06 | Backlog rows / Kanban cards / Agenda viewer | Topic key (`TOP-YYYY-###`) **re-added** to rows the design omits, for traceability of official records. | `Backlog.tsx:295,339`; `Kanban.tsx:144`; progress-log:660 | Op-approved (CLAUDE.md canonical ID) | MINOR | Bless key-on-row | No | | DV-07 | Agenda publish dialog | Design's "notify groups" checkboxes → one honest static line (backend notifies all). | `AgendaBuilder.tsx:289-291`; progress-log:1280 | Op-approved | MINOR | Bless single-line vs group toggles | No | | DV-08 | Meetings list (`ACMP Meetings` isList) | **Kept** an Agenda-status chip column the design omits; **omitted** the design's filter chips + Saved-views (no backend, not faked). | git `8d55a5a`; progress-log:544-548 | Op-approved ("keep agenda chip") | MINOR | Bless added chip column; decide filters/saved-views | No | | DV-09 | Backlog filter bar | Stream + Owner filter chips rendered but **disabled** — no option source (stream registry = BL-024). | `Backlog.tsx:179-180`; progress-log (P5 refresh) | Flagged (no data) | MAJOR | Provide the stream/owner option source | No — needs BL-024 | | DV-10 | Submit topic — Affected streams | Free-text token input instead of the design's preset stream chips. | `SubmitTopic.tsx:355-380`; progress-log:718 (D3) | Flagged | MAJOR | Provide stream registry / chip source | No — needs BL-024 | | DV-11 | Backlog Calendar + Timeline views | Faithful chrome, **empty body** (no markers/bars) — Topics API exposes no scheduled/due/span data. | `Calendar.tsx:65-68`; `Timeline.tsx:40-43`; progress-log:711 (D1) | Flagged (data → P6) | MAJOR | Define the date/span data contract | No — needs API | | DV-12 | Topic detail | Single stored title; design's alternate-language title line dropped. | progress-log:723 | Flagged | MINOR | Decide bilingual title storage | No | | DV-13 | Backlog / Meetings rows | Row navigation via a single **title link**, not the design's whole-row `<button>` (invalid inside a real `<table>`). | `Backlog.tsx`; progress-log:549,720 | Behavior/a11y-justified | MINOR | Bless title-link rows | No | | DV-14 | Agenda builder — presenter | Design's avatar-cycle → an accessible shared `Select` sourced from `/api/members`. | `AgendaBuilder.tsx`; progress-log:1283 | A11y-justified | MINOR | Bless Select vs avatar-cycle | No | | DV-15 | Meeting page | In-page shared `Tabs` instead of the design's top-bar tab switcher; breadcrumb drops the 3rd "Agenda builder" segment. | `MeetingPage.tsx:109`; single-day meeting. | git `b7ab531`; `ui/DateField.tsx` | Justified fix | MINOR | Confirm single-day assumption | No | | DV-20 | Agenda viewer (read-only) | Budget bar kept; the design's read-only readiness sidebar needs data the app doesn't have. | progress-log:658 | Op-approved | MINOR | Provide readiness data or bless budget bar | No | | DV-21 | Agenda pool label | Pool labeled "Scheduled topics" in the design but sourced from **Prepared** topics (status precedes Scheduled). | `AgendaBuilder.tsx`; SEC-613DOC-066§2 — No-Reference CompositionsScreens/controls/states built with **no matching `.dc.html` at the time**. "Now exists?" reflects design files added later (`ACMP System States`, `ACMP Meetings`, `ACMP Create Flows & Dialogs`, `ACMP Diagrams` — all added in `3f7d06d` / PR #24, 2026-06-27). | ID | Surface | Built (commit) | Reference at build time | Reference NOW | Severity | Needs from design | Needs restructure | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | NR-01 | Notification inbox full page `/notifications` | `bd2584f` | None (progress-log:574) | **Yes** — `ACMP.dc.html` `notifications` screen + `ACMP System States` `notif` + docs/domain/information-architecture.md p.79 | MAJOR | One spec for the full inbox;
